patterns.title: Authentication
patterns.description: Three production-ready auth screens: Sign In with social providers, Sign Up with password strength, and Two-Factor OTP verification.

examples.title: Sign In
examples.code:
<!-- Sign In card with branding panel, social providers, email/password, remember me -->
<Flex Class="rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] overflow-hidden border border-border/40">
    <!-- Left branding panel -->
    <Stack Class="w-80 bg-primary p-10" Gap="6">
        <DynamicIcon Name="Zap" Class="h-5 w-5 text-primary-foreground" />
        <Heading Level="2" Class="text-2xl font-bold text-primary-foreground">
            The fastest way to ship products.
        </Heading>
    </Stack>

    <!-- Right form -->
    <Flex Class="flex-1 items-center justify-center bg-card p-12">
        <Stack Class="w-full max-w-sm" Gap="6">
            <Heading Level="2">Sign in</Heading>

            <!-- Social providers -->
            <Grid Class="grid-cols-3 gap-2">
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Class="gap-2">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="Chrome" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Google
                </Button>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Class="gap-2">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="Github" Class="h-4 w-4" /> GitHub
                </Button>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Class="gap-2">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="Apple" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Apple
                </Button>
            </Grid>

            <!-- Email / Password -->
            <Stack Gap="4">
                <Lumeo.Input type="email" placeholder="jordan@acme.com">
                    <Prefix><DynamicIcon Name="Mail" Class="h-4 w-4 text-muted-foreground" /></Prefix>
                </Lumeo.Input>
                <PasswordInput Placeholder="Enter your password" />
                <Flex Align="center" Gap="2">
                    <Checkbox @bind-Checked="_remember" />
                    <Label>Remember me for 30 days</Label>
                </Flex>
                <Button Class="w-full">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="LogIn" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Sign in
                </Button>
            </Stack>
        </Stack>
    </Flex>
</Flex>

examples.title: Two-Factor Verification
examples.code:
<!-- OTP Two-Factor card with countdown, backup code link, and trust badges -->
<Stack Class="max-w-md mx-auto" Gap="0">
    <Card Class="border-border/40">
        <CardHeader>
            <Flex Align="center" Gap="3">
                <Flex Class="h-10 w-10 items-center justify-center rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] bg-primary/10">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="ShieldCheck" Class="h-5 w-5 text-primary" />
                </Flex>
                <Stack>
                    <Heading Level="2">Two-factor auth</Heading>
                    <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Code sent to j••••@acme.com</Lumeo.Text>
                </Stack>
            </Flex>
        </CardHeader>
        <CardContent>
            <Stack Gap="6">
                <Flex Class="rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] bg-muted/50 border border-border/40 px-4 py-3" Gap="3">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="Info" Class="h-4 w-4 text-muted-foreground" />
                    <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Enter the 6-digit code. Expires in 5:00.</Lumeo.Text>
                </Flex>

                <Flex Justify="center">
                    <OtpInput Length="6" @bind-Value="_otp" OnComplete="HandleOtpComplete" />
                </Flex>

                <Button Class="w-full" IsLoading="_otpLoading" OnClick="HandleOtpVerify">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="ShieldCheck" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Verify code
                </Button>

                <Flex Justify="between">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" OnClick="GoBack">Back</Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" OnClick="ResendCode">Resend code</Button>
                </Flex>
            </Stack>
        </CardContent>
    </Card>
</Stack>

@code {
    private string _otp = "";
    private bool _otpLoading;

    private async Task HandleOtpComplete(string otp) { if (otp.Length == 6) await HandleOtpVerify(); }
    private async Task HandleOtpVerify()
    {
        _otpLoading = true;
        await Task.Delay(1400);
        _otpLoading = false;
        ToastService.Success("Verified", "Welcome back!");
    }
    private void ResendCode() { _otp = ""; ToastService.Info("Resent", "New code sent."); }
    private void GoBack() { _otp = ""; }
}

patterns.title: Chat
patterns.description: A full messaging interface with contact list, conversation view, time-grouped messages, and message input bar.

examples.title: Chat Messaging Interface
examples.code:
<!-- Chat layout: contact sidebar | message thread -->
<Stack Class="w-full border border-border/40 rounded-xl overflow-hidden" style="height: 600px;">
    <Flex Class="h-full">

        <!-- Contact Sidebar -->
        <Flex Direction="col" Class="w-72 border-r border-border/40 bg-card">
            <Flex Class="items-center justify-between px-4 pt-4 pb-3 border-b border-border/40">
                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-base font-bold text-foreground">Messages</Lumeo.Text>
                <Flex Class="items-center gap-1">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Search" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="SquarePen" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                </Flex>
            </Flex>
            <Stack Class="flex-1 overflow-y-auto">
                @foreach (var contact in _contacts)
                {
                    <button class="flex items-start gap-3 w-full px-3 py-2.5 hover:bg-muted/40 transition-colors" @onclick="@(() => SelectContact(contact))">
                        <Stack Class="relative shrink-0">
                            <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm">
                                <AvatarFallback>@contact.Initials</AvatarFallback>
                            </Avatar>
                            @if (contact.IsOnline)
                            {
                                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="absolute bottom-0 right-0 h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full bg-primary border-2 border-card block"></Lumeo.Text>
                            }
                        </Stack>
                        <Stack Class="flex-1 min-w-0 gap-0.5">
                            <Flex Class="items-center justify-between">
                                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground truncate">@contact.Name</Lumeo.Text>
                                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">@contact.Timestamp</Lumeo.Text>
                            </Flex>
                            <Flex Class="items-center justify-between">
                                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground truncate">@contact.LastMessage</Lumeo.Text>
                                @if (contact.UnreadCount > 0) { <Badge Class="ml-1 shrink-0 h-4 min-w-4 text-[10px] px-1">@contact.UnreadCount</Badge> }
                            </Flex>
                        </Stack>
                    </button>
                }
            </Stack>
        </Flex>

        <!-- Chat Area -->
        <Flex Direction="col" Class="flex-1 overflow-hidden bg-background">
            <!-- Header -->
            <Flex Class="items-center gap-3 px-4 py-3 border-b border-border/40 bg-card">
                <Stack Class="relative">
                    <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm"><AvatarFallback>PK</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="absolute bottom-0 right-0 h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full bg-primary border-2 border-card block"></Lumeo.Text>
                </Stack>
                <Stack>
                    <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">Priya Kapoor</Lumeo.Text>
                    <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-primary">Active now</Lumeo.Text>
                </Stack>
                <Flex Class="ml-auto items-center gap-1">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Phone" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Video" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Ellipsis" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                    </Button>
                </Flex>
            </Flex>

            <!-- Messages -->
            <Stack Class="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-4 py-4 gap-1">
                <!-- Time group separator -->
                <Flex Class="items-center gap-3 my-3">
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="flex-1 h-px bg-border/40 block"></Lumeo.Text>
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">Today</Lumeo.Text>
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="flex-1 h-px bg-border/40 block"></Lumeo.Text>
                </Flex>
                @foreach (var msg in _messages)
                {
                    <Flex Class="@(msg.IsSent ? \"items-end gap-2 justify-end\" : \"items-end gap-2 justify-start\")">
                        @if (!msg.IsSent)
                        {
                            <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" Class="shrink-0 mb-1"><AvatarFallback>PK</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                        }
                        <Stack Class="max-w-sm gap-1">
                            <Lumeo.Text As="div" Class="@(msg.IsSent ? \"rounded-2xl rounded-br-sm px-3.5 py-2.5 text-sm bg-primary text-primary-foreground\" : \"rounded-2xl rounded-bl-sm px-3.5 py-2.5 text-sm bg-muted text-foreground\")">@msg.Text</Lumeo.Text>
                            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground px-1">@msg.Time</Lumeo.Text>
                        </Stack>
                        @if (msg.IsSent)
                        {
                            <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" Class="shrink-0 mb-1"><AvatarFallback>ME</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                        }
                    </Flex>
                }
            </Stack>

            <!-- Input -->
            <Stack Class="border-t border-border/40 bg-card px-4 py-3 gap-2">
                <Flex Class="items-center gap-2 rounded-xl border border-border/40 bg-background px-3 py-2">
                    <Input placeholder="Type a message..." @bind-Value="_newMessage" Class="flex-1 border-0 shadow-none bg-transparent text-sm p-0 h-auto focus-visible:ring-0" />
                </Flex>
                <Flex Class="items-center justify-between">
                    <Flex Class="items-center gap-1">
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Paperclip" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Smile" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Mic" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                    </Flex>
                    <Button Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm" @onclick="SendMessage">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Send" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 mr-1.5" /> Send
                    </Button>
                </Flex>
            </Stack>
        </Flex>
    </Flex>
</Stack>

@code {
    private void SendMessage()
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_newMessage)) return;
        _messages.Add(new Message(_newMessage, true, DateTime.Now.ToString("h:mm tt")));
        _newMessage = string.Empty;
    }
}

patterns.title: Dashboard
patterns.description: A complete analytics dashboard built with Lumeo components. Includes a top bar, stats cards, revenue chart, activity feed, data table, and mini charts.

examples.title: Analytics Dashboard
examples.code:
<!-- Top Bar -->
<Flex Class="items-center justify-between gap-4 rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] bg-card px-4 py-3 shadow-sm">
    <Flex Class="items-center gap-3">
        <Flex Class="h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-md bg-primary">
            <DynamicIcon Name="BarChart3" Class="h-4 w-4 text-primary-foreground" />
        </Flex>
        <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">Nexara</Lumeo.Text>
    </Flex>
    <Flex Class="flex-1 max-w-sm items-center gap-2 rounded-md border border-border/40 bg-background px-3 h-9">
        <DynamicIcon Name="Search" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />
        <Input Placeholder="Search..." Class="h-8 flex-1 bg-transparent border-0 shadow-none" />
    </Flex>
    <Flex Class="items-center gap-1.5">
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
            <DynamicIcon Name="Bell" Class="h-4 w-4" />
        </Button>
        <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm">
            <AvatarFallback>PK</AvatarFallback>
        </Avatar>
        <DynamicIcon Name="ChevronDown" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />
    </Flex>
</Flex>

<!-- Stats Row -->
<Grid Class="grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4">
    @foreach (var stat in _stats)
    {
        <Card Class="rounded-[var(--radius-lg)] border-border/40">
            <CardHeader Class="pb-2">
                <Flex Class="items-center justify-between">
                    <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wider">@stat.Label</Lumeo.Text>
                    <DynamicIcon Name="@stat.IconName" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />
                </Flex>
            </CardHeader>
            <CardContent Class="pt-0">
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-2xl font-bold text-foreground">@stat.Value</Lumeo.Text>
                <Flex Class="mt-1.5 items-center gap-1.5">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="@(stat.IsPositive ? "TrendingUp" : "TrendingDown")" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs font-semibold">@stat.Trend</Lumeo.Text>
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">vs last month</Lumeo.Text>
                </Flex>
                <Progress Value="@stat.ProgressValue" Class="mt-3 h-1" Variant="@stat.ProgressVariant" />
            </CardContent>
        </Card>
    }
</Grid>

<!-- Revenue Chart + Activity -->
<Grid Class="grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-7 gap-4">
    <Card Class="lg:col-span-5">
        <CardHeader>
            <Heading Level="3">Revenue Overview</Heading>
            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">12-month revenue vs. expenses</Lumeo.Text>
        </CardHeader>
        <CardContent>
            <AreaChart Categories="@_areaCategories" Series="@_areaSeries" Smooth="true" Gradient="true" Height="340px" />
        </CardContent>
    </Card>
    <Card Class="lg:col-span-2">
        <CardHeader>
            <Heading Level="3">Activity</Heading>
        </CardHeader>
        <CardContent>
            @foreach (var a in _activities)
            {
                <Flex Class="gap-2.5 py-2.5 border-b border-border/40 last:border-0 items-start hover:bg-muted/30 transition-colors">
                    <Avatar Class="h-7 w-7 shrink-0"><AvatarFallback Class="text-[9px]">@a.Initials</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                    <Stack Gap="0">
                        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs">@a.Text</Lumeo.Text>
                        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground">@a.Time</Lumeo.Text>
                    </Stack>
                </Flex>
            }
        </CardContent>
    </Card>
</Grid>

patterns.title: Multi-Step Form Wizard
patterns.description: A polished four-step onboarding wizard with progress steps, real field inputs, preferences, and a review summary before submission.

examples.title: Onboarding Wizard
examples.code:
<!-- 4-step onboarding wizard with Steps progress bar, side illustration, and review summary -->
<Stack Class="max-w-3xl mx-auto" Gap="4">

    <!-- Steps progress indicator -->
    <Card>
        <CardContent Class="py-4">
            <Steps CurrentStep="@(_currentStep - 1)" Class="w-full">
                <StepsItem Title="Account" Description="Credentials" />
                <StepsItem Title="Profile" Description="About you" />
                <StepsItem Title="Preferences" Description="Customize" />
                <StepsItem Title="Review" Description="Confirm" />
            </Steps>
        </CardContent>
    </Card>

    <Flex Gap="4" Class="lg:flex-row">
        <!-- Form Card -->
        <Card Class="flex-1">
            <CardHeader>
                <Flex Align="center" Gap="3">
                    <Flex Class="h-9 w-9 items-center justify-center rounded-[var(--radius)] bg-primary/10">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="UserPlus" Class="h-4 w-4 text-primary" />
                    </Flex>
                    <Stack>
                        <Heading Level="3">Create your account</Heading>
                        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Set up your login credentials</Lumeo.Text>
                    </Stack>
                </Flex>
            </CardHeader>
            <CardContent>
                @if (_currentStep == 1)
                {
                    <!-- Step 1: First/Last name, Email, Password + PasswordInput with ShowStrength -->
                }
                @if (_currentStep == 2)
                {
                    <!-- Step 2: Job title, Department Select, Company, Country, Timezone, Bio Textarea -->
                }
                @if (_currentStep == 3)
                {
                    <!-- Step 3: Notification switches, Use-case RadioGroup in styled cards, Interest checkboxes -->
                }
                @if (_currentStep == 4)
                {
                    <!-- Step 4: Review sections with Edit links, Terms checkbox, Submit -->
                }
            </CardContent>
            <CardFooter>
                <Flex Justify="between" Class="w-full">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" OnClick="PreviousStep" Disabled="@(_currentStep == 1)">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="ChevronLeft" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Back
                    </Button>
                    @if (_currentStep < 4)
                    {
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" OnClick="NextStep">
                            Continue <DynamicIcon Name="ChevronRight" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" OnClick="Submit" Disabled="@(!_confirmed)">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Check" Class="h-4 w-4" /> Create Account
                        </Button>
                    }
                </Flex>
            </CardFooter>
        </Card>

        <!-- Side illustration / tips panel -->
        <Card Class="w-64 bg-muted/30 border-border/40">
            <CardContent Class="py-6">
                <!-- Step-specific icon, title, body copy, and tip bullets -->
            </CardContent>
        </Card>
    </Flex>
</Stack>

@code {
    private int _currentStep = 1;
    private bool _confirmed = false;

    private void NextStep() { if (_currentStep < 4) _currentStep++; }
    private void PreviousStep() { if (_currentStep > 1) _currentStep--; }
    private void Submit() { if (_confirmed) { /* create account */ } }
}

patterns.title: Mail
patterns.description: A three-column inbox with folder rail, message list, reading pane, and reply composer.

examples.title: Mail / Inbox
examples.code:
<!-- 3-column mail layout: icon rail | message list | reading pane -->
<Stack Class="w-full border border-border/40 rounded-xl overflow-hidden" style="height: 580px;">
    <Flex Class="h-full">

        <!-- Icon Rail -->
        <Flex Direction="col" Class="w-14 border-r border-border/40 bg-card items-center py-3 gap-1">
            <Stack Class="relative">
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-9 w-9">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="Inbox" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                </Button>
                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="absolute -top-1 -right-1 h-4 min-w-4 rounded-full bg-primary text-primary-foreground text-[10px] flex items-center justify-center px-1">3</Lumeo.Text>
            </Stack>
            <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-9 w-9">
                <DynamicIcon Name="Send" Class="h-4 w-4" />
            </Button>
            <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-9 w-9">
                <DynamicIcon Name="FileEdit" Class="h-4 w-4" />
            </Button>
            <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-9 w-9">
                <DynamicIcon Name="Star" Class="h-4 w-4" />
            </Button>
            <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-9 w-9">
                <DynamicIcon Name="Trash2" Class="h-4 w-4" />
            </Button>
        </Flex>

        <!-- Message List -->
        <Flex Direction="col" Class="w-72 border-r border-border/40 bg-card">
            <Stack Gap="2" Class="p-3 border-b border-border/40">
                <Flex Class="relative items-center">
                    <DynamicIcon Name="Search" Class="absolute left-2.5 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 h-3.5 w-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />
                    <Input placeholder="Search mail..." Class="pl-8 h-8 text-xs w-full" />
                </Flex>
                <Flex Class="items-center gap-1">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm" Class="h-7 text-xs">All</Button>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm" Class="h-7 text-xs">Unread</Button>
                    <Badge Class="ml-auto">3</Badge>
                </Flex>
            </Stack>
            <Stack Class="flex-1 overflow-y-auto">
                @foreach (var mail in _mailItems)
                {
                    <Stack Class="gap-1 px-3 py-2.5 border-b border-border/40 cursor-pointer hover:bg-muted/40">
                        <Flex Class="items-center justify-between gap-2">
                            <Flex Class="items-center gap-2 min-w-0">
                                @if (!mail.IsRead) { <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="h-2 w-2 rounded-full bg-primary shrink-0 block"></Lumeo.Text> }
                                <Avatar Class="h-7 w-7"><AvatarFallback Class="text-[10px]">@mail.Initials</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                                <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground truncate">@mail.Sender</Lumeo.Text>
                            </Flex>
                            <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground shrink-0">@mail.Time</Lumeo.Text>
                        </Flex>
                        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs font-semibold text-foreground pl-4 truncate">@mail.Subject</Lumeo.Text>
                        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground pl-4 truncate">@mail.Preview</Lumeo.Text>
                    </Stack>
                }
            </Stack>
        </Flex>

        <!-- Reading Pane -->
        <Flex Direction="col" Class="flex-1 bg-background">
            <Stack Class="p-4 border-b border-border/40 bg-card">
                <Flex Class="items-start justify-between gap-4">
                    <Flex Class="items-start gap-3">
                        <Avatar><AvatarFallback>PK</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
                        <Stack>
                            <Lumeo.Text As="span" Class="text-sm font-semibold text-foreground">Priya Kapoor</Lumeo.Text>
                            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-base font-semibold text-foreground mt-1">Re: Q2 roadmap review</Lumeo.Text>
                            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground mt-0.5">Apr 17, 2026, 9:42 AM</Lumeo.Text>
                        </Stack>
                    </Flex>
                    <Flex Class="items-center gap-1">
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Archive" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Icon" Class="h-8 w-8">
                            <DynamicIcon Name="Trash2" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                        </Button>
                    </Flex>
                </Flex>
            </Stack>
            <Stack Class="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-5">
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm text-foreground leading-relaxed">Email body content...</Lumeo.Text>
            </Stack>
            <Separator />
            <Stack Gap="2" Class="p-3 bg-card">
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground">Reply to Priya Kapoor</Lumeo.Text>
                <Textarea placeholder="Write your reply..." Class="min-h-[70px] text-sm resize-none" />
                <Flex Class="justify-end gap-2">
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Forward" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 mr-1.5" /> Forward
                    </Button>
                    <Button Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="Send" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 mr-1.5" /> Send
                    </Button>
                </Flex>
            </Stack>
        </Flex>
    </Flex>
</Stack>

patterns.title: Settings Page
patterns.description: A full settings layout with sidebar navigation, profile, account security, billing, notifications, team management, and danger zone.

examples.title: Settings
examples.code:
<!-- Settings Layout -->
<Flex Class="gap-8">
    <!-- Sidebar Nav -->
    <Stack Class="w-52 shrink-0 space-y-0.5">
        @foreach (var item in _sections) {
            <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost"
                    Class="w-full justify-start gap-3"
                    @onclick="@(() => _activeSection = item.Id)">
                <DynamicIcon Name="@item.Icon" Class="h-4 w-4" />
                @item.Label
            </Button>
        }
    </Stack>
    <!-- Content -->
    <Stack Class="flex-1" Gap="6">
        @if (_activeSection == "profile") { /* Profile form */ }
        @if (_activeSection == "billing") { /* Plan, payment, invoices */ }
        @if (_activeSection == "team")    { /* Members, pending invites */ }
        @if (_activeSection == "account") {
            /* Password change, 2FA, sessions, danger zone */
            <Card>
                <CardHeader><Heading Level="3">Danger Zone</Heading></CardHeader>
                <CardContent>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Destructive">Delete my account</Button>
                </CardContent>
            </Card>
        }
    </Stack>
</Flex>

patterns.title: Social Feed
patterns.description: A three-column social media layout with navigation rail, timeline feed with posts and interactions, and a right sidebar with trending topics and follow suggestions.

examples.title: Social Media Timeline
examples.code:
<!-- Three-column social feed layout -->
<Flex Class="w-full gap-0">

  <!-- Left Rail -->
  <Flex Direction="col" Class="w-56 shrink-0 pr-4 border-r border-border/40">
    <Flex Class="items-center gap-3 px-2 py-3">
      <Avatar><AvatarFallback>ME</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
      <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm font-semibold">You</Lumeo.Text>
    </Flex>
    <Separator />
    @foreach (var nav in _navItems)
    {
      <button class="flex items-center gap-3 w-full rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors
                      @(nav.IsActive ? 'bg-accent text-accent-foreground' : 'text-muted-foreground hover:bg-accent')">
        <DynamicIcon Name="@nav.Icon" Class="h-4 w-4" />
        @nav.Label
        @if (nav.Badge > 0) { <Badge Class="ml-auto">@nav.Badge</Badge> }
      </button>
    }
    <Button Class="w-full mt-2" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
      <DynamicIcon Name="PenLine" Class="h-4 w-4 mr-2" /> New Post
    </Button>
  </Flex>

  <!-- Center Feed -->
  <Stack Class="flex-1 px-6 min-w-0">
    <!-- Compose card -->
    <Card Class="mb-4 border-border/40">
      <CardContent Class="pt-4">
        <Flex Gap="3">
          <Avatar><AvatarFallback>ME</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
          <Stack Class="flex-1 gap-3">
            <Textarea AutoResize="true" placeholder="What's on your mind?" />
            <Button IsLoading="@_isPosting" OnClick="HandlePost">
              <DynamicIcon Name="Send" Class="h-4 w-4 mr-1.5" /> Post
            </Button>
          </Stack>
        </Flex>
      </CardContent>
    </Card>

    <!-- Post cards with like/comment/share/bookmark -->
    @foreach (var post in _posts) { /* ... */ }
  </Stack>

  <!-- Right Rail -->
  <Stack Class="w-64 shrink-0 pl-4 border-l border-border/40 gap-4">
    <Input placeholder="Search..." Class="pl-9" />

    <!-- Trending topics -->
    <Card Class="border-border/40">
      <CardHeader><Heading Level="4">Trending</Heading></CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        @foreach (var t in _trending) { /* tag + post count */ }
      </CardContent>
    </Card>

    <!-- Who to follow -->
    <Card Class="border-border/40">
      <CardHeader><Heading Level="4">Who to Follow</Heading></CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        @foreach (var s in _suggestions)
        {
          <Flex Class="items-center gap-3 py-2.5">
            <Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm"><AvatarFallback>@s.Initials</AvatarFallback></Avatar>
            <Stack Class="flex-1 min-w-0">
              <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm font-medium">@s.Name</Lumeo.Text>
              <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">@s.Followers followers</Lumeo.Text>
            </Stack>
            <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">Follow</Button>
          </Flex>
        }
      </CardContent>
    </Card>
  </Stack>

</Flex>

services.name: Density
services.summary: Orthogonal "tightness" axis applied across sized components. Sits next to the size scale (Size) rather than replacing it: a Size.Md button can render at Density.Compact for a dense admin grid or Density.Spacious for a marketing hero, without scaling the type ramp itself.

enumValues.name: Compact
enumValues.summary: Minimum padding. For data grids, dense admin tables, sidebar pickers. Visual hit area is ~24-28px.

enumValues.name: Comfortable
enumValues.summary: Default. Standard app density. Hit area ~36-40px.

enumValues.name: Spacious
enumValues.summary: Generous padding. Marketing pages, onboarding wizards, thumb-first mobile UIs. Hit area ~44-48px (Apple HIG minimum).

services.name: DialogOverlayOptions
services.summary: Dialog-shaped overlay options. Semantic marker (same caveat as SheetOverlayOptions) — typed for documentation and IDE auto-complete, not enforced at the service overload boundary.

properties.name: ScrollableBody
properties.summary: Wrap the rendered component in a scrollable body region inside the overlay's chrome (Sheet only — Dialog and Drawer manage their own scroll). Default true so consumers can pass a long form to OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} without rolling their own overflow-y-auto wrapper — and without hitting the focus- ring clip that bare overflow-y: auto introduces (per spec, overflow-y: auto promotes overflow-x to auto too, which clips inputs' box-shadow focus rings at the left/right edge). The built-in wrapper uses the px-1 -mx-1 trick to give focus rings 4px breathing room without shifting the visual padding. Set to false for sheets whose content sets its own scrolling strategy (e.g. an embedded PdfViewer that already paints inside a fixed canvas). Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileFullscreen
properties.summary: When true and the viewport width is below MobileBreakpoint, the overlay is forced full-screen (full width + full height, no corner radius). Works across Dialog, Sheet and Drawer overlays — replaces the consumer-side max-md:!h-full max-md:!w-full max-md:!max-w-full Tailwind override chain that was previously the only way to get a full-screen Dialog on mobile. For Sheets, this is equivalent to setting MobileSize to Full but composes more naturally for the Sheet+Dialog mixed case. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: MobileSwipeToClose
properties.summary: SwipeToClose override below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use SwipeToClose at all sizes. Typical pattern: off on desktop, on for mobile bottom-sheet pull-down. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileSheetSize
properties.summary: Legacy Sheet-only alias for MobileSize. Applies to: Sheet. Reads/writes MobileSize via a 1:1 mapping (null round-trips to null). Kept for source compatibility with pre-3.6 callers.

properties.name: MobileSize
properties.summary: Overlay size to use when the viewport is below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use Size at all sizes. Typical pattern: OverlaySize.Default on desktop, OverlaySize.Full on mobile. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet.

properties.name: MobileSheetSide
properties.summary: Sheet side to use when the viewport is below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use SheetSide at all sizes. Typical pattern: Lumeo.Side.Right on desktop, Lumeo.Side.Bottom on mobile. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileBreakpoint
properties.summary: Viewport width threshold (CSS pixels) below which the Mobile* overrides apply. Default 768 (Tailwind md). Set to null to disable responsive switching even if the Mobile* fields are populated. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer (gates every Mobile* override).

properties.name: SwipeToClose
properties.summary: When true, a Sheet opened via OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} can be dismissed by swiping in the direction opposite to its SheetSide (e.g. swipe-down on a Bottom sheet). Ignored for non-Sheet overlay types. Drawers already enable swipe by default. Default false to preserve the existing programmatic-open behaviour. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: SheetSize
properties.summary: Legacy Sheet-only size alias. Applies to: Sheet. Reads/writes Size via a 1:1 mapping; setting either sets the same backing value. Kept for source compatibility with pre-3.6 callers.

properties.name: Size
properties.summary: Overlay content size. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet. (Drawer sizes to its content and ignores this.) For a Dialog it maps to the max-w-* preset (Sm→max-w-sm … Xl→max-w-4xl, Full→near-viewport); for a Sheet it maps to the side-appropriate width (Left/Right) or height (Top/Bottom). Replaces the Sheet-only SheetSize as the single size knob for service overlays.

properties.name: SheetSide
properties.summary: Edge the sheet slides in from. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: ShowCloseButton
properties.summary: Whether the shell renders its top-end close (X) button. null (default) keeps the legacy coupling — the X shows whenever PreventClose is false. Set true to force the X even on a modal overlay (PreventClose=true: backdrop/Escape stay disabled, but the X still offers an explicit way out — it routes through the same dismiss guard, so an OnBeforeClose veto still applies), or false to hide it and own the close chrome yourself. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet. (Drawer has no X by design — drag handle.)

properties.name: PreventClose
properties.summary: Suppress dismissal via backdrop click / Escape. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: Class
properties.summary: Extra CSS classes merged onto the overlay's content element. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

services.name: DrawerOverlayOptions
services.summary: Drawer-shaped overlay options. Drawer enables swipe-to-close by default; the inherited SwipeToClose is ignored for drawers. Semantic marker (see SheetOverlayOptions for the enforcement caveat).

properties.name: ScrollableBody
properties.summary: Wrap the rendered component in a scrollable body region inside the overlay's chrome (Sheet only — Dialog and Drawer manage their own scroll). Default true so consumers can pass a long form to OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} without rolling their own overflow-y-auto wrapper — and without hitting the focus- ring clip that bare overflow-y: auto introduces (per spec, overflow-y: auto promotes overflow-x to auto too, which clips inputs' box-shadow focus rings at the left/right edge). The built-in wrapper uses the px-1 -mx-1 trick to give focus rings 4px breathing room without shifting the visual padding. Set to false for sheets whose content sets its own scrolling strategy (e.g. an embedded PdfViewer that already paints inside a fixed canvas). Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileFullscreen
properties.summary: When true and the viewport width is below MobileBreakpoint, the overlay is forced full-screen (full width + full height, no corner radius). Works across Dialog, Sheet and Drawer overlays — replaces the consumer-side max-md:!h-full max-md:!w-full max-md:!max-w-full Tailwind override chain that was previously the only way to get a full-screen Dialog on mobile. For Sheets, this is equivalent to setting MobileSize to Full but composes more naturally for the Sheet+Dialog mixed case. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: MobileSwipeToClose
properties.summary: SwipeToClose override below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use SwipeToClose at all sizes. Typical pattern: off on desktop, on for mobile bottom-sheet pull-down. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileSheetSize
properties.summary: Legacy Sheet-only alias for MobileSize. Applies to: Sheet. Reads/writes MobileSize via a 1:1 mapping (null round-trips to null). Kept for source compatibility with pre-3.6 callers.

properties.name: MobileSize
properties.summary: Overlay size to use when the viewport is below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use Size at all sizes. Typical pattern: OverlaySize.Default on desktop, OverlaySize.Full on mobile. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet.

properties.name: MobileSheetSide
properties.summary: Sheet side to use when the viewport is below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use SheetSide at all sizes. Typical pattern: Lumeo.Side.Right on desktop, Lumeo.Side.Bottom on mobile. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileBreakpoint
properties.summary: Viewport width threshold (CSS pixels) below which the Mobile* overrides apply. Default 768 (Tailwind md). Set to null to disable responsive switching even if the Mobile* fields are populated. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer (gates every Mobile* override).

properties.name: SwipeToClose
properties.summary: When true, a Sheet opened via OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} can be dismissed by swiping in the direction opposite to its SheetSide (e.g. swipe-down on a Bottom sheet). Ignored for non-Sheet overlay types. Drawers already enable swipe by default. Default false to preserve the existing programmatic-open behaviour. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: SheetSize
properties.summary: Legacy Sheet-only size alias. Applies to: Sheet. Reads/writes Size via a 1:1 mapping; setting either sets the same backing value. Kept for source compatibility with pre-3.6 callers.

properties.name: Size
properties.summary: Overlay content size. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet. (Drawer sizes to its content and ignores this.) For a Dialog it maps to the max-w-* preset (Sm→max-w-sm … Xl→max-w-4xl, Full→near-viewport); for a Sheet it maps to the side-appropriate width (Left/Right) or height (Top/Bottom). Replaces the Sheet-only SheetSize as the single size knob for service overlays.

properties.name: SheetSide
properties.summary: Edge the sheet slides in from. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: ShowCloseButton
properties.summary: Whether the shell renders its top-end close (X) button. null (default) keeps the legacy coupling — the X shows whenever PreventClose is false. Set true to force the X even on a modal overlay (PreventClose=true: backdrop/Escape stay disabled, but the X still offers an explicit way out — it routes through the same dismiss guard, so an OnBeforeClose veto still applies), or false to hide it and own the close chrome yourself. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet. (Drawer has no X by design — drag handle.)

properties.name: PreventClose
properties.summary: Suppress dismissal via backdrop click / Escape. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: Class
properties.summary: Extra CSS classes merged onto the overlay's content element. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

services.name: IComponentInteropService
services.summary: Provides JS interop helpers for Lumeo components (click-outside, focus traps, floating positioning, swipe gestures, scroll utilities, etc.). Inject this interface in consumers to enable mocking in tests.

methods.name: RegisterClickOutside
methods.signature: RegisterClickOutside(string elementId, string? triggerElementId, Func<Task> handler)

methods.name: GetOrderedDescendantIds
methods.summary: Returns the id attributes of the descendants of containerId matching selector, in live DOM order (document order). Compound widgets whose children self-register (RadioGroup / ToggleGroup / Segmented / Stepper / Splitter, …) consult this at NAVIGATION time so roving / arrow-key / neighbour order tracks the real DOM even after a keyed reorder MOVES reused child instances without re-rendering them (so the C# mount-order registry has gone stale). Callers MUST treat an empty result as "DOM order unavailable" and fall back to their own registry order, so prerender / JS-unavailable / non-configured-test-double paths keep working. Default implementation returns an empty array so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling and behave exactly as before (registry-order fallback).

methods.name: FocusMenuItemByTypeahead
methods.signature: FocusMenuItemByTypeahead(string containerId, string query, int currentIndex)
methods.summary: Type-to-focus (Radix menu typeahead). Focuses the first enabled menu item in containerId whose text content starts with query (case-insensitive), searching after currentIndex first then wrapping, and returns its index (or -1 when nothing matches). Shared by DropdownMenu / Menubar / MegaMenu via the MenuTypeahead buffer helper. Default implementation returns -1 so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling.

methods.name: SetHtmlClass
methods.summary: Toggles a class on document.documentElement. Useful for global modes (e.g. hiding floating chrome while a DataGrid is fullscreen).

methods.name: SetupFocusTrap
methods.summary: Engages a Tab-cycling focus trap on the element, saves the previously focused element (the trigger) and moves focus into the trap. initialFocusSelector optionally names the element (resolved within the trap) to focus first — e.g. AlertDialog targets its least destructive action via [data-lumeo-initial-focus]; when null/unmatched, the first focusable element receives focus.

methods.name: RemoveFocusTrap
methods.summary: Releases the trap and returns focus to the element that was focused when SetupFocusTrap ran, if it is still in the document.

methods.name: SaveFocus
methods.summary: Saves the currently-focused element keyed by key so RestoreFocus can hand focus back later. Unlike SetupFocusTrap this installs NO Tab trap — use it for non-modal surfaces (menus, listbox popovers) that move focus inward on open but must let Tab close them per the WAI-ARIA pattern.

methods.name: RestoreFocus
methods.summary: Returns focus to the element saved by SaveFocus under the same key, if it is still in the document (WCAG 2.4.3).

methods.name: AttachOverlaySlideEnd
methods.summary: Registers a native animationend listener that filters strictly on the slide-in animation name and, on completion, sets the element's inline transform: none. Bypasses Blazor's event roundtrip so the cleanup runs synchronously from the browser's animation pipeline. Used by Sheet, Drawer and any future slide-in overlay to defeat the animation-fill-mode: both identity-matrix transform trap that would otherwise establish a containing block for fixed-positioned descendants (Select / DatePicker / Combobox popovers).

methods.name: AttachOverlayExitEnd
methods.summary: Radix-Presence-style overlay EXIT: clears the open-time containing-block guard (the inline animation:none/transform:none that AttachOverlaySlideEnd stamped) so the panel's animate-slide-out-*/animate-zoom-out class can actually run, then invokes OnExitAnimationEnd on dotNetRef once the panel's own exit animation finishes. Lets the overlay content drop backdrop + panel together on the REAL animation end instead of a blind timer (which slips late under main-thread load). The component keeps its timer strictly as a fallback. Default no-op so test doubles / prerender fall back to that timer.

methods.name: RegisterSvDrag
methods.signature: RegisterSvDrag(string elementId, Func<double, double, Task> handler)

methods.name: PositionFixed
methods.signature: PositionFixed(string contentId, string referenceId, string align = "start", bool matchWidth = false, string side = "bottom")

methods.name: PositionFixed
methods.signature: PositionFixed(string contentId, string referenceId, string align, bool matchWidth, string side, int offset)
methods.summary: 3.12.x — extended overload with an explicit trigger→content gap in pixels (Tooltip Offset). The default implementation ignores the offset and falls back to the 5-arg overload (JS hardcoded 4px) so existing implementations keep compiling unchanged. Returns the resolved side (see above).

methods.name: PositionFixed
methods.signature: PositionFixed(string contentId, string referenceId, string align, bool matchWidth, string side, int offset, Func<string, Task>? onSideChanged)
methods.summary: round-14 — extended overload that ALSO reports LIVE collision flips: the synchronous return only covers the initial placement, but a later scroll/resize reposition can flip the box to a different side while the tooltip stays open, and a directional-arrow consumer needs to follow that too (Codex P2). onSideChanged is invoked with the newly-resolved side whenever a LATER reposition changes it (never for the initial placement — that's the synchronous return). The default implementation ignores the callback and behaves like the 6-arg overload — only ComponentInteropService overrides this with real live notification; test doubles don't run real JS, so there is nothing to notify.

methods.name: PositionAtPoint
methods.signature: PositionAtPoint(string contentId, double x, double y)
methods.summary: Positions a fixed-position element at the viewport point (x, y) and clamps it inside the viewport (flipping up/left of the point if it would overflow). Used by ContextMenu, which opens at raw click coordinates with no anchor element. Default implementation is a no-op so existing implementers (and test doubles) keep compiling.

methods.name: InitToolbarRoving
methods.summary: Initialise a single-tab-stop roving tabindex over the toolbar's items.

methods.name: MoveToolbarFocus
methods.summary: Move focus delta items from the focused toolbar item (clamped, no wrap).

methods.name: FocusToolbarEdge
methods.summary: Focus the first (last=false) or last toolbar item.

methods.name: RegisterPullToRefresh
methods.summary: Attaches a non-passive touchmove guard so PullToRefresh can claim a downward drag at scrollTop 0 instead of the browser consuming it as native overscroll. No-op off touch devices. See registerPullToRefresh.

methods.name: RegisterDrawerSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterDrawerSwipe(string elementId, string direction, Func<Task> handler)

methods.name: RegisterDrawerSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterDrawerSwipe(string elementId, string direction, Func<Task> handler, int? activationPx, int? firePx)
methods.summary: 3.0.1 — extended overload that accepts swipe-to-close thresholds. Both parameters are optional; passing null falls back to the JS hardcoded defaults so existing callers keep working unchanged. Mirrors the LumeoGestureOptions bag.

methods.name: RegisterDrawerSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterDrawerSwipe(string elementId, string direction, Func<Task> handler, int? activationPx, int? firePx, double? velocity)
methods.summary: 3.19 — adds velocity (px/ms): a flick faster than this in the dismiss direction closes the drawer even below firePx. Default impl ignores it so non-overriding implementations keep working.

methods.name: RegisterDrawerSnap
methods.signature: RegisterDrawerSnap(string elementId, string direction, Func<Task<bool>> dismissHandler, Func<int, Task> snapHandler, IReadOnlyList<double> snapPoints, int activeIndex, bool dismissible, int? activationPx, int? firePx, double? velocity)
methods.summary: Registers a snap-point gesture: the drawer rests at one of snapPoints (fractions 0<f≤1), drags between them, and dismisses when flicked/dragged below the lowest. snapHandler fires with the new index on each settle; dismissHandler fires on close. Default impl falls back to plain swipe-dismiss.

methods.name: SetDrawerSnap
methods.summary: Programmatically move a snap-point drawer to index.

methods.name: RegisterTabSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterTabSwipe(string elementId, bool wrap, Func<string, Task> handler)

methods.name: RegisterTabSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterTabSwipe(string elementId, bool wrap, Func<string, Task> handler, int? swipeThresholdPx, int? verticalDeadZonePx)
methods.summary: 3.0.1 — extended overload that accepts horizontal swipe and vertical dead-zone thresholds. Passing null falls back to the JS hardcoded defaults so existing callers keep working unchanged.

methods.name: RegisterSortableTouch
methods.signature: RegisterSortableTouch(string containerId, Func<int, int, Task> handler)

methods.name: RegisterCarouselSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterCarouselSwipe(string elementId, string orientation, Func<string, Task> swipeHandler, Func<double, double, int, Task> scrollHandler)

methods.name: RegisterCarouselSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterCarouselSwipe(string elementId, string orientation, Func<string, Task> swipeHandler, Func<double, double, int, Task> scrollHandler, int? swipeThresholdPx, int? verticalDeadZonePx)
methods.summary: 3.0.1 — overload with explicit swipe / vertical dead-zone thresholds (Carousel).

methods.name: CarouselScrollTo
methods.signature: CarouselScrollTo(string elementId, int index, string behavior = "smooth")

methods.name: RegisterHorizontalSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterHorizontalSwipe(string elementId, Func<string, Task> handler, int? swipeThresholdPx, int? verticalDeadZonePx)
methods.summary: 3.0.1 — overload with explicit swipe / vertical dead-zone thresholds (Calendar).

methods.name: RegisterGallerySwipe
methods.signature: RegisterGallerySwipe(string elementId, Func<string, Task> handler, int? swipeThresholdPx, int? verticalDeadZonePx)
methods.summary: 3.0.1 — overload with explicit swipe / vertical dead-zone thresholds (ImageGallery).

methods.name: RegisterResizeHandle
methods.signature: RegisterResizeHandle(string elementId, string direction, Func<double, Task> resizeHandler, Func<Task> resizeEndHandler)

methods.name: RegisterScrollspy
methods.signature: RegisterScrollspy(string containerId, int offset, bool smooth, Func<string?, Task> handler)

methods.name: ScrollspyScrollTo
methods.signature: ScrollspyScrollTo(string containerId, string sectionId, bool smooth)

methods.name: ScrollspyScrollTo
methods.signature: ScrollspyScrollTo(string containerId, string sectionId, bool smooth, int offset)
methods.summary: Click-scroll overload that honours the Scrollspy Offset (#246) so a programmatic jump lands at the same scroll position the observer treats as "active" (e.g. clearing a sticky header). The default implementation ignores the offset and forwards to the 3-arg overload so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling unchanged.

methods.name: RegisterTabsOverflow
methods.signature: RegisterTabsOverflow(string listId, Func<bool, bool, Task> handler)

methods.name: TabsScrollBy
methods.signature: TabsScrollBy(string listId, double delta, bool horizontal)
methods.summary: Scrolls the tablist by delta px (smooth). Horizontal scrolls scrollLeft; vertical scrolls scrollTop.

methods.name: RegisterToastSwipe
methods.signature: RegisterToastSwipe(string elementId, string toastId, Func<string, Task> handler)

methods.name: RegisterOtpPaste
methods.signature: RegisterOtpPaste(string baseId, int length, Func<string, Task> handler)

methods.name: RegisterColumnResize
methods.signature: RegisterColumnResize(string handleId, double minWidth, double? maxWidth, Func<double, Task> commitHandler)

methods.name: RegisterColumnResize
methods.signature: RegisterColumnResize(string handleId, double minWidth, double? maxWidth, Func<double, bool, Task> commitHandler)
methods.summary: Pointer-based overload that also reports whether the final width came from a double-click auto-fit-to-content rather than a drag (commitHandler's bool). Round-9 #4: the original 4-parameter member above stays the abstract contract byte-for-byte (a prior round widened ITS delegate type in place, which broke every external implementer / test double the moment they updated). This is an ADDITIVE default interface member instead — the default body delegates to the 4-parameter member with autoFit always false (a legacy handler has no way to distinguish the two), so existing implementers keep compiling unchanged. ComponentInteropService overrides this overload directly to report the real autoFit flag.

methods.name: NudgeColumnResize
methods.summary: Keyboard resize: nudges the column width by delta px (JS clamps to min/max and re-commits through the registered handler). Additive DIM — no-op default so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling; DataGrid's own keyboard resize simply has no effect against a legacy double.

methods.name: RegisterColumnReorder
methods.signature: RegisterColumnReorder(string gridId, Func<string, string, Task> commitHandler)

methods.name: ClearColumnReorderTransforms
methods.summary: Snaps every header/body cell back to identity WITHOUT capturing — the no-animation counterpart to CaptureColumnRects for a delayed reorder commit that gets REJECTED (columns changed during the settle window), so the transforms JS left in place never get an accept-path capture to clear them (round-8 #4). Additive DIM (round-9 #4) — no-op default so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling.

methods.name: RegisterRowReorder
methods.signature: RegisterRowReorder(string gridId, Func<string, string, Task> commitHandler)

methods.name: ClearRowReorderTransforms
methods.summary: Snaps every row (and any expanded detail sibling) back to identity WITHOUT capturing — the no-animation counterpart to CaptureRowRects for a delayed reorder commit that gets REJECTED (backing rows changed during the settle window), so the transforms JS left in place never get an accept-path capture to clear them (round-8 #2). Additive DIM (round-9 #4) — no-op default so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling.

methods.name: ScrollSelectorIntoView
methods.summary: Instantly scrolls the first element matching selector into the viewport (block: center). Call BEFORE LockScroll — a locked body can't be scrolled, programmatically or otherwise.

methods.name: ScrollIntoView
methods.summary: Scrolls the element with id elementId into view within its nearest scroll container. Used by keyboard-navigated lists (e.g. the Command palette active item) to keep the highlighted row visible. block maps to scrollIntoView's block option ("nearest" by default so already-visible rows don't jump). Default implementation is a no-op so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling.

methods.name: RegisterAffix
methods.signature: RegisterAffix(string elementId, int offsetTop, int? offsetBottom, string? target, Func<bool, Task> handler)

methods.name: RegisterBackToTop
methods.signature: RegisterBackToTop(string id, int threshold, Func<bool, Task> handler)

methods.name: RegisterBackToTop
methods.signature: RegisterBackToTop(string id, int threshold, Func<bool, Task> handler, string? target)

methods.name: DownloadFile
methods.signature: DownloadFile(string fileName, string contentBase64, string mimeType = "application/octet-stream")

methods.name: ResetFileInput
methods.summary: Clears the value of a native <input type="file"> so that re-picking the SAME file fires the change event again. The browser suppresses change when the chosen path is identical to the input's current value, so UploadTrigger (a pure pick-trigger with no accumulating list to mask it) must reset the element after each pick. Default impl is a no-op so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling unchanged (#70).

methods.name: PrefersReducedMotion
methods.summary: Core-side prefers-reduced-motion: reduce query (mirrors the Lumeo.Motion helper) for core components that animate via Blazor/JS and can't be fully neutralised by a CSS @media block alone. Default returns false (motion allowed) so existing implementers/test doubles keep compiling unchanged.

methods.name: IsActiveElementFocusVisible
methods.summary: True when the currently-focused element is in the browser's :focus-visible state (keyboard/programmatic focus) rather than a plain :focus a mouse click also leaves behind. Tooltip uses this to gate opening on focusin so a clicked-then-abandoned trigger doesn't stay open forever — a native button keeps DOM focus after a mouse click with nothing to clear it, but :focus-visible is false for that case in supporting browsers. Default returns true (old behaviour: always open on focus) so existing implementers/test doubles — which have no real DOM to query — keep compiling and behaving unchanged.

methods.name: TouchRippleCoords
methods.signature: TouchRippleCoords(string hostElementId, double clientX, double clientY)
methods.summary: Resolves a pointer's viewport coordinates (clientX, clientY) into coordinates relative to the element identified by hostElementId. Used by TouchRipple so a ripple is centred on the click point even when the pointer lands on a nested child (where OffsetX/OffsetY would be relative to the child, not the ripple host). Default returns (0,0) for test doubles.

methods.name: SetPlaybackRate
methods.summary: Sets the media element's playbackRate (clamped 0.25–4×).

methods.name: GetMediaState
methods.summary: Reads the live duration and currentTime off an HTMLMediaElement. Required because Blazor's media event args don't expose these — they're properties of the element itself.

methods.name: MotionPrefersReducedMotion
methods.summary: Queries the browser's prefers-reduced-motion: reduce media query via the Lumeo.Motion JS module. Lets a component branch in C# (skip a burst, snap to the end value) before scheduling any JS-driven animation that a CSS @media block can't reach. The default implementation returns false (motion allowed) so existing implementers and test doubles keep compiling and behave exactly as before.

methods.name: MotionTickNumber
methods.signature: MotionTickNumber(string elementId, double from, double to, int durationMs, int decimals, string separator = ",", string decimalSeparator = ".")

methods.name: MotionRevealText
methods.signature: MotionRevealText(string elementId, int staggerMs, double threshold)

methods.name: MotionBlurFade
methods.signature: MotionBlurFade(string elementId, int delayMs, bool once, bool forceHidden = false)

methods.name: MotionAnimatedBeam
methods.signature: MotionAnimatedBeam(string elementId, string fromId, string toId, object options)

methods.name: AiObserveScrollButton
methods.summary: Observes a message list's scroll position and reports (via dotNetRef's OnScrollAwayChanged(bool)) whether it is scrolled away from the bottom, driving the visibility of AgentMessageList's floating scroll-to-latest button. Generic in the .NET reference type so this core interop interface stays decoupled from the UI component. Default no-op so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling (they can drive the callback directly instead of via real JS).

methods.name: AiDisposeScrollButton
methods.summary: Tears down the scroll-button observer registered by AiObserveScrollButton{T}. Default no-op.

methods.name: GanttRefreshAsync
methods.summary: Pushes a partial options bag (any of readonly / todayHighlight / barHeight / columnWidth — and optionally tasks / viewMode) to a LIVE Gantt instance and re-renders it (gantt.refresh). The init-only options were previously captured once at GanttInitAsync, so flipping Readonly / TodayHighlight / BarHeight / ColumnWidth after init was silently ignored (battle-test wave 1 #2). Default implementation is a no-op so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling and the non-Gantt paths are unaffected.

methods.name: GanttV3ScrollToXAsync
methods.summary: Centers targetX (a pixel offset within the timeline's own scrollable content) in el's viewport — GanttV3's own tiny scroll slice (gantt-v3.js), used to mirror v2's init-time center-on-today behavior (gantt-v2.js's tryScroll) so the initial viewport shows task data instead of the empty padding columns Gantt3.ComputeInitialRange adds before the first task. Default no-op so existing implementers / test doubles keep compiling.

methods.name: GanttV3GetLocalDateAsync
methods.summary: Browser-local "today" as an ISO "yyyy-MM-dd" string (Codex round 2, P2 #9) — v2 parity: v2 derives "today" via the BROWSER's new Date() (gantt-v2.js), while Gantt3/GanttTimeline previously used C#'s DateTime.Today, which on Blazor Server is the SERVER's local date, not the visiting browser's. Called once from Gantt3's first post-render (never during prerendering, where JS interop would throw — see Gantt3.OnAfterRenderAsync's remarks). Returns null when JS interop isn't available (a non-Gantt implementer, prerendering, or a torn-down circuit), in which case the caller keeps its server-side Today fallback. This is additive surface on an interface that otherwise stays UI-framework-agnostic — recorded in PublicAPI alongside the rest of GanttV3's Additive set, joining the same "narrow, Gantt-specific, default no-op DIM" precedent as GanttV3ScrollToXAsync rather than a general-purpose "get the client's locale/timezone" API (out of scope — no other consumer has asked for one). Default no-op DIM so existing implementers/test doubles keep compiling.

methods.name: GanttV3RegisterHeaderScrollSyncAsync
methods.summary: Registers a one-way horizontal-scroll mirror from canvasEl (the row-canvas's own scrollable element) onto headerInnerEl (a transform: translateX(...) target) — Codex round 2, P1 #3's sticky- header fix. The header can no longer physically BE the same scrolling element as the row canvas (see GanttTimeline.razor's remarks for why: position: sticky must resolve against Gantt3's shared OUTER vertical scroller, which requires NO intervening scroll-container-establishing ancestor between the header and it), so this keeps the two horizontally column-aligned instead. Idempotent for the same canvasEl — calling again updates nothing (a fresh headerInnerEl would require an unregister first; GanttTimeline only ever registers once, on mount). Default no-op DIM so existing implementers/test doubles keep compiling.

methods.name: GanttV3UnregisterHeaderScrollSyncAsync
methods.summary: Tears down the scroll mirror registered by GanttV3RegisterHeaderScrollSyncAsync. Default no-op.

methods.name: GanttV3RegisterVerticalScrollTrackingAsync
methods.summary: Registers a rAF-throttled vertical-scroll listener on scrollEl (Codex round 4, P2 #3) — reports (scrollTop, clientHeight) back via dotNetRef's OnGanttV3VerticalScroll JSInvokable method, so GanttArrowLayer can cull dependency arrows whose rows are scrolled out of view (the row canvas's own bars/tree already virtualize via <Virtualize>; the SVG arrow overlay previously did not). Independent of GanttV3RegisterHeaderScrollSyncAsync — that one is skipped entirely in Gantt3's shared-pane mode (see GanttTimeline.razor's own remarks), precisely the mode where arrow virtualization matters most. Default no-op DIM so existing implementers/test doubles keep compiling.

methods.name: GanttV3UnregisterVerticalScrollTrackingAsync
methods.summary: Tears down the listener registered by GanttV3RegisterVerticalScrollTrackingAsync{T}. Default no-op.

methods.name: GanttV3GetScrollCenterXAsync
methods.summary: Reads el's CURRENT logical horizontal scroll center (Codex round 5, P2 #5) — in the SAME "logical" coordinate space (0 = the scrollable content's own physical-left origin, RTL-normalized) that GanttV3ScrollToXAsync's own targetX already uses. Gantt3 uses this to capture what the user ACTUALLY has centered on screen before a view-mode switch recomputes the visible range, instead of assuming the outgoing range's own midpoint (a proxy that silently diverges the moment a user pans manually). Returns null when the element can't be measured (not yet laid out, or JS interop unavailable) so the caller can fall back to that proxy. Default no-op DIM so existing implementers/test doubles keep compiling.

methods.signature: RegisterToolbarOverflow(string elementId, Func<int, int, Task> handler)

methods.signature: RichTextCommandAsync(string id, string name, params object?[]? args)

methods.name: RichTextSetAriaAttributesAsync
methods.signature: RichTextSetAriaAttributesAsync(string id, bool ariaInvalid, string? ariaDescribedBy)

services.name: IOverlayService
services.summary: Provides methods to show and close overlay components (Dialog, Sheet, Drawer, AlertDialog). Inject this interface in consumers to enable mocking in tests.

methods.name: ShowDialogAsync
methods.signature: ShowDialogAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title = null, OverlayParameters? parameters = null, OverlayOptions? options = null)

methods.name: ShowSheetAsync
methods.signature: ShowSheetAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title = null, Lumeo.Side side = Lumeo.Side.Right, SheetSize size = SheetSize.Default, OverlayParameters? parameters = null, OverlayOptions? options = null)
