services.name: IResponsiveService
services.summary: Reactive viewport/breakpoint information. Use this to make components respond to viewport size changes (e.g. switching a Sheet from side panel on desktop to fullscreen on mobile, or hiding sidebars below md). Subscribe to ViewportChanged to react to live resize / orientation change.

properties.name: Width
properties.summary: Current viewport width in CSS pixels (window.innerWidth). 0 until the first interop sync — read inside OnAfterRenderAsync or subscribe to ViewportChanged to get the populated value.

properties.name: Height
properties.summary: Current viewport height in CSS pixels (window.innerHeight). 0 until first interop sync. See Width for the timing caveat.

properties.name: Current
properties.summary: Current Tailwind-aligned breakpoint based on Width. Maps to the same boundaries Tailwind v4 uses: Sm=640, Md=768, Lg=1024, Xl=1280, Xxl=1536. Use the convenience flags below for the common case.

properties.name: IsMobile
properties.summary: True when Width is below Md (768px) — the conventional mobile boundary. Same value the OverlayOptions.MobileBreakpoint default checks against.

properties.name: IsTablet
properties.summary: True when Width is in [Md, Lg) — 768-1023px. Tablets in portrait, large phones in landscape.

properties.name: IsDesktop
properties.summary: True when Width is >= Lg (1024px). Includes typical laptop / desktop layouts.

methods.name: EnsureInitialisedAsync
methods.summary: Lazily registers the JS resize listener and pulls the initial viewport size. Called automatically the first time a property is read, but consumers can call it eagerly from OnAfterRenderAsync(firstRender: true) to avoid the first-render zero-value read.

events.name: ViewportChanged
events.summary: Fires whenever the viewport size crosses a meaningful boundary. Implementations debounce raw resize events (~100ms) so frequent listeners don't fire per scroll-bar nudge. The event arg carries the new width, height, and breakpoint.

services.name: IThemeService
services.summary: Provides theme mode and scheme management (dark/light/system, color schemes, layout direction). Inject this interface in consumers to enable mocking in tests.

methods.name: SetDirectionAsync
methods.summary: Set the page layout direction (LTR / RTL). Persisted to localStorage.

methods.name: GetDirectionAsync
methods.summary: Read the current direction (reads from document.documentElement.dir).

services.name: LayoutDirection
services.summary: Layout direction. Mirrors the dir attribute on <html>.

services.name: Orientation
services.summary: Layout axis for components that can lay their content out either left-to-right or top-to-bottom (Tabs, Separator, Carousel, Stepper, Timeline, Splitter, MegaMenu, ButtonGroup, ImageCompare, FormField, Steps, ToggleGroup, etc.). Replaces per-component *Orientation / *Direction enums in 3.0 (BREAKING).

properties.name: ZIndex
properties.summary: Backdrop z-index for this overlay. Content sits at ZIndex + 1 so a nested overlay's backdrop (allocated later with a strictly larger value) lands above the parent's content. Assigned by OverlayService at registration time.

properties.name: Class
properties.summary: Extra CSS classes merged onto the overlay's content element. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: PreventClose
properties.summary: Suppress dismissal via backdrop click / Escape. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: ShowCloseButton
properties.summary: Whether the shell renders its top-end close (X) button. null (default) keeps the legacy coupling — the X shows whenever PreventClose is false. Set true to force the X even on a modal overlay (PreventClose=true: backdrop/Escape stay disabled, but the X still offers an explicit way out — it routes through the same dismiss guard, so an OnBeforeClose veto still applies), or false to hide it and own the close chrome yourself. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet. (Drawer has no X by design — drag handle.)

properties.name: SheetSide
properties.summary: Edge the sheet slides in from. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: Size
properties.summary: Overlay content size. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet. (Drawer sizes to its content and ignores this.) For a Dialog it maps to the max-w-* preset (Sm→max-w-sm … Xl→max-w-4xl, Full→near-viewport); for a Sheet it maps to the side-appropriate width (Left/Right) or height (Top/Bottom). Replaces the Sheet-only SheetSize as the single size knob for service overlays.

properties.name: SheetSize
properties.summary: Legacy Sheet-only size alias. Applies to: Sheet. Reads/writes Size via a 1:1 mapping; setting either sets the same backing value. Kept for source compatibility with pre-3.6 callers.

properties.name: SwipeToClose
properties.summary: When true, a Sheet opened via OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} can be dismissed by swiping in the direction opposite to its SheetSide (e.g. swipe-down on a Bottom sheet). Ignored for non-Sheet overlay types. Drawers already enable swipe by default. Default false to preserve the existing programmatic-open behaviour. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileBreakpoint
properties.summary: Viewport width threshold (CSS pixels) below which the Mobile* overrides apply. Default 768 (Tailwind md). Set to null to disable responsive switching even if the Mobile* fields are populated. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer (gates every Mobile* override).

properties.name: MobileSheetSide
properties.summary: Sheet side to use when the viewport is below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use SheetSide at all sizes. Typical pattern: Lumeo.Side.Right on desktop, Lumeo.Side.Bottom on mobile. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileSize
properties.summary: Overlay size to use when the viewport is below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use Size at all sizes. Typical pattern: OverlaySize.Default on desktop, OverlaySize.Full on mobile. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet.

properties.name: MobileSheetSize
properties.summary: Legacy Sheet-only alias for MobileSize. Applies to: Sheet. Reads/writes MobileSize via a 1:1 mapping (null round-trips to null). Kept for source compatibility with pre-3.6 callers.

properties.name: MobileSwipeToClose
properties.summary: SwipeToClose override below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use SwipeToClose at all sizes. Typical pattern: off on desktop, on for mobile bottom-sheet pull-down. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileFullscreen
properties.summary: When true and the viewport width is below MobileBreakpoint, the overlay is forced full-screen (full width + full height, no corner radius). Works across Dialog, Sheet and Drawer overlays — replaces the consumer-side max-md:!h-full max-md:!w-full max-md:!max-w-full Tailwind override chain that was previously the only way to get a full-screen Dialog on mobile. For Sheets, this is equivalent to setting MobileSize to Full but composes more naturally for the Sheet+Dialog mixed case. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: ScrollableBody
properties.summary: Wrap the rendered component in a scrollable body region inside the overlay's chrome (Sheet only — Dialog and Drawer manage their own scroll). Default true so consumers can pass a long form to OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} without rolling their own overflow-y-auto wrapper — and without hitting the focus- ring clip that bare overflow-y: auto introduces (per spec, overflow-y: auto promotes overflow-x to auto too, which clips inputs' box-shadow focus rings at the left/right edge). The built-in wrapper uses the px-1 -mx-1 trick to give focus rings 4px breathing room without shifting the visual padding. Set to false for sheets whose content sets its own scrolling strategy (e.g. an embedded PdfViewer that already paints inside a fixed canvas). Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: BaseZIndex
properties.summary: Base z-index assigned to the first overlay. Subsequent overlays stack at BaseZIndex + n * Step. The backdrop sits at the assigned value; the content sits one tier above (Z + 1) so a nested overlay's backdrop always lands above the parent's content.

properties.name: Step
properties.summary: Distance between consecutive overlay tiers. Step = 10 leaves room for backdrop (Z) + content (Z+1) without colliding with the next overlay's backdrop.

methods.name: ShowDialogAsync
methods.signature: ShowDialogAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title = null, OverlayParameters? parameters = null, OverlayOptions? options = null)

methods.name: ShowSheetAsync
methods.signature: ShowSheetAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title = null, Lumeo.Side side = Lumeo.Side.Right, SheetSize size = SheetSize.Default, OverlayParameters? parameters = null, OverlayOptions? options = null)

methods.name: ShowDrawerAsync
methods.signature: ShowDrawerAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title = null, OverlayParameters? parameters = null, OverlayOptions? options = null)

services.name: OverlaySize
services.summary: Unified overlay content size as exposed through OverlayService. Drives both DialogContent.DialogSize and SheetContent.SheetSize from a single Size value, so the same options record can size a Dialog or a Sheet. Kept in the service layer (not the UI namespace) so consumers don't take a hard UI dependency. Full is layout-intent (entire viewport).

services.name: SheetOverlayOptions
services.summary: Sheet-shaped overlay options. Semantic marker — at the call site you get clearer intent ("this configures a Sheet") and IDE navigation, but the existing OverlayService.ShowDialogAsync{T} / OverlayService.ShowDrawerAsync{T} still accept any OverlayOptions via standard subtype polymorphism, so nothing prevents passing this record to the wrong service overload — the unused sheet-only properties stay silent no-ops there. Compile-time enforcement requires narrowing the service-method signatures to the typed record (planned for 4.0 as a breaking change).

properties.name: ScrollableBody
properties.summary: Wrap the rendered component in a scrollable body region inside the overlay's chrome (Sheet only — Dialog and Drawer manage their own scroll). Default true so consumers can pass a long form to OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} without rolling their own overflow-y-auto wrapper — and without hitting the focus- ring clip that bare overflow-y: auto introduces (per spec, overflow-y: auto promotes overflow-x to auto too, which clips inputs' box-shadow focus rings at the left/right edge). The built-in wrapper uses the px-1 -mx-1 trick to give focus rings 4px breathing room without shifting the visual padding. Set to false for sheets whose content sets its own scrolling strategy (e.g. an embedded PdfViewer that already paints inside a fixed canvas). Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileFullscreen
properties.summary: When true and the viewport width is below MobileBreakpoint, the overlay is forced full-screen (full width + full height, no corner radius). Works across Dialog, Sheet and Drawer overlays — replaces the consumer-side max-md:!h-full max-md:!w-full max-md:!max-w-full Tailwind override chain that was previously the only way to get a full-screen Dialog on mobile. For Sheets, this is equivalent to setting MobileSize to Full but composes more naturally for the Sheet+Dialog mixed case. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: MobileSwipeToClose
properties.summary: SwipeToClose override below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use SwipeToClose at all sizes. Typical pattern: off on desktop, on for mobile bottom-sheet pull-down. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileSheetSize
properties.summary: Legacy Sheet-only alias for MobileSize. Applies to: Sheet. Reads/writes MobileSize via a 1:1 mapping (null round-trips to null). Kept for source compatibility with pre-3.6 callers.

properties.name: MobileSize
properties.summary: Overlay size to use when the viewport is below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use Size at all sizes. Typical pattern: OverlaySize.Default on desktop, OverlaySize.Full on mobile. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet.

properties.name: MobileSheetSide
properties.summary: Sheet side to use when the viewport is below MobileBreakpoint. Null = use SheetSide at all sizes. Typical pattern: Lumeo.Side.Right on desktop, Lumeo.Side.Bottom on mobile. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: MobileBreakpoint
properties.summary: Viewport width threshold (CSS pixels) below which the Mobile* overrides apply. Default 768 (Tailwind md). Set to null to disable responsive switching even if the Mobile* fields are populated. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer (gates every Mobile* override).

properties.name: SwipeToClose
properties.summary: When true, a Sheet opened via OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} can be dismissed by swiping in the direction opposite to its SheetSide (e.g. swipe-down on a Bottom sheet). Ignored for non-Sheet overlay types. Drawers already enable swipe by default. Default false to preserve the existing programmatic-open behaviour. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: SheetSize
properties.summary: Legacy Sheet-only size alias. Applies to: Sheet. Reads/writes Size via a 1:1 mapping; setting either sets the same backing value. Kept for source compatibility with pre-3.6 callers.

properties.name: Size
properties.summary: Overlay content size. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet. (Drawer sizes to its content and ignores this.) For a Dialog it maps to the max-w-* preset (Sm→max-w-sm … Xl→max-w-4xl, Full→near-viewport); for a Sheet it maps to the side-appropriate width (Left/Right) or height (Top/Bottom). Replaces the Sheet-only SheetSize as the single size knob for service overlays.

properties.name: SheetSide
properties.summary: Edge the sheet slides in from. Applies to: Sheet.

properties.name: ShowCloseButton
properties.summary: Whether the shell renders its top-end close (X) button. null (default) keeps the legacy coupling — the X shows whenever PreventClose is false. Set true to force the X even on a modal overlay (PreventClose=true: backdrop/Escape stay disabled, but the X still offers an explicit way out — it routes through the same dismiss guard, so an OnBeforeClose veto still applies), or false to hide it and own the close chrome yourself. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet. (Drawer has no X by design — drag handle.)

properties.name: PreventClose
properties.summary: Suppress dismissal via backdrop click / Escape. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

properties.name: Class
properties.summary: Extra CSS classes merged onto the overlay's content element. Applies to: Dialog, Sheet, Drawer.

services.name: SheetSize
services.summary: Sheet size as exposed through OverlayService. Mirrors SheetContent.SheetSize — kept distinct so the service layer doesn't take a hard dependency on the UI namespace. Full is layout-intent (entire viewport) and intentionally NOT folded into Size. Retained as the parameter type of OverlayService.ShowSheetAsync{T} and as the backing type of the obsolete SheetSize alias; new code should prefer OverlaySize.

services.name: Side
services.summary: Cardinal side used for overlay placement and edge anchoring. Replaces the per-component *Side enums (PopoverSide, TooltipSide, DropdownMenuSide, HoverCardSide, DrawerSide, SheetSide, SidebarSide, TourPlacement) in 3.0 (BREAKING).

services.name: Size
services.summary: Unified size scale used across Lumeo components. Replaces the per-component *Size enums in 3.0 (BREAKING).

enumValues.name: Sm
enumValues.summary: Small. Used by most sized components (Avatar, Chip, Input, etc.).

enumValues.name: Md
enumValues.summary: Medium / default. The neutral middle value for every sized component.

enumValues.name: Lg
enumValues.summary: Large. Used by most sized components.

enumValues.name: Xl
enumValues.summary: Extra large. Used by Avatar, Icon.

enumValues.name: Xxl
enumValues.summary: 2x extra large. Reserved for future use.

enumValues.name: Xxs
enumValues.summary: Double extra small. For dense-mode Avatar / Chip where a smaller indicator is needed (e.g. inline-with-text presence dot, stacked-avatar list previews). Replaces consumer-side text-[10px] / h-5 w-5 arbitrary-value Tailwind escape hatches with a first-class token. Added in 3.3; appended at value 6 instead of inserted before Xs so existing serialised ints (Sm=1, Md=2, …) still resolve to the same enum member.

services.name: TextareaCaretInfo
services.summary: Top/Left are viewport-relative (legacy). OffsetTop/OffsetLeft are relative to the textarea's offset parent (its position: relative wrapper) so a caret-anchored dropdown can be positioned absolutely and track the textarea on scroll. LineHeight is the computed line height so callers can drop the dropdown one line below the caret.

services.name: Theme
services.summary: Entry point for the fluent ThemeBuilder.

services.name: ThemeBuilder
services.summary: Fluent builder that emits a custom CSS-variable override block on top of a Lumeo base scheme (zinc / slate / stone / …). Lets consumers tweak design tokens without writing a CSS file or forking a scheme. await Theme.Customize(Js) .WithPrimary("#3b82f6") .WithBorderRadius(0.5) .WithFontFamily("'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif") .ApplyAsync(); Calls are idempotent — each ApplyAsync replaces the previous `<style id="lumeo-custom-theme">` block, so consumers can rebuild on every settings change without leaking stale rules.

methods.name: WithPrimary
methods.summary: Override --color-primary. Accepts any CSS color string (hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch(), CSS named color).

methods.name: WithBorderRadius
methods.summary: Override the global border-radius scale. Accepts a rem value — Lumeo uses 0.5 by default. Components derive from calc(var(--radius) - 2px) / calc(var(--radius) + 4px).

methods.name: WithFontFamily
methods.summary: Override the root font family. Pass the full CSS font-family stack including quotes / fallbacks. Writes the font-family declaration directly on :root — Lumeo's theme CSS doesn't consume a --font-family custom property, so emitting the raw declaration is what actually takes effect (matching the path used by ThemeService.SetFont).

methods.name: WithVariable
methods.summary: Set an arbitrary CSS custom property. The name MUST start with -- and must match a token the bundled lumeo.css actually reads. Color tokens are prefixed with --color- (e.g. --color-primary, --color-ring); non-color tokens are bare (--radius, --shadow-sm). Throws ArgumentException when the name does not start with -- so a shorthand typo ("primary") fails loud instead of emitting a silently-unused --primary.

methods.name: ApplyAsync
methods.summary: Write the accumulated overrides to a managed <style id="lumeo-custom-theme"> block. Replaces the previous block on each call, so successive Apply calls don't leak.

methods.name: ClearAsync
methods.summary: Remove the custom-theme block, reverting to the base scheme.

properties.name: AvailableSchemes

[
        new("zinc", "Zinc", "hsl(240 5.9% 10%)"),
        new("blue", "Blue", "hsl(221 83% 53%)"),

    ]

methods.name: OnExternalThemeChange
methods.summary: Invoked from JS when the OS color scheme changes (in System mode) or another tab updates the theme. Re-reads the live state and raises OnThemeChanged so subscribed components repaint.

services.name: ViewportInfo
services.summary: Immutable snapshot of the viewport at a point in time. Passed to ViewportChanged subscribers.

methods.name: FromWidth
methods.summary: Maps a width to a Breakpoint using the Tailwind thresholds. Pure function; consumers can call it directly with an arbitrary width (e.g. to pre-compute a breakpoint from a server-rendered User-Agent hint).

Accordion.name: Accordion
Accordion.description: Vertically stacked collapsible sections that expand to reveal content.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The accordion's items, typically a sequence of AccordionItem elements.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Whether one item can be open at a time (Single, default) or several at once (Multiple).

parameters.name: DefaultValues
parameters.description: Initial open items for the uncontrolled usage. Ignored once Value / Values drive the component.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Controlled open item for Single. When non-null the accordion is controlled — its open state comes entirely from this parameter and toggles are reported via ValueChanged (the consumer owns the state). Pair with @bind-Value. An empty string means "all collapsed". Leave null for the uncontrolled behaviour seeded by DefaultValues.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Two-way callback for Value (Single mode).

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: Controlled open items for Multiple. When non-null the accordion is controlled. Pair with @bind-Values. Leave null for the uncontrolled behaviour seeded by DefaultValues.

parameters.name: ValuesChanged
parameters.description: Two-way callback for Values (Multiple mode).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root element.

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Two-way callback for Value (Single mode).

events.name: ValuesChanged
events.description: Two-way callback for Values (Multiple mode).

records.name: AccordionContext
records.signature:
AccordionContext(
        AccordionType Type,
        HashSet<string> OpenItems,
        EventCallback<string> Toggle,
        Action<string, string, bool> RegisterTrigger,
        Action<string, string, string, bool> ReplaceTrigger,
        Action<string> UnregisterTrigger,
        EventCallback<(string TriggerId, int Delta, bool ToEdge, bool Last)> MoveFocus)

examples.title: Single (Default)
examples.code:
<Accordion Type="Accordion.AccordionType.Single">
    <AccordionItem Value="item-1">
        <AccordionTrigger>Is it accessible?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Yes. It adheres to the WAI-ARIA design pattern.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem Value="item-2">
        <AccordionTrigger>Is it styled?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Yes. It comes with default styles that match the other components' aesthetic.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem Value="item-3">
        <AccordionTrigger>Is it animated?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Yes. It's animated by default, but you can disable it if you prefer.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

examples.title: FAQ
examples.code:
<Accordion Type="Accordion.AccordionType.Single">
    <AccordionItem Value="q1">
        <AccordionTrigger>What payment methods do you accept?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>We accept all major credit cards, PayPal, and bank transfers.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem Value="q2">
        <AccordionTrigger>Can I cancel my subscription at any time?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Yes, cancel anytime. Access continues until end of billing period.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem Value="q3">
        <AccordionTrigger>Do you offer a free trial?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>14-day free trial, no credit card required.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

examples.title: Multiple
examples.code:
<Accordion Type="Accordion.AccordionType.Multiple">
    <AccordionItem Value="item-1">
        <AccordionTrigger>What is Lumeo?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Lumeo is a Blazor component library inspired by shadcn/ui, built with Tailwind CSS.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem Value="item-2">
        <AccordionTrigger>Which .NET version?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Lumeo targets .NET 10 and uses Tailwind CSS v4 for styling.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem Value="item-3">
        <AccordionTrigger>Can I customize it?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Absolutely. All components accept a Class parameter for additional CSS classes.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

examples.title: Type — interactive
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_accordionTypeSel" Options="_accordionTypeOptions" />
<Accordion Type="@_activeAccordionType">
    <AccordionItem Value="a">
        <AccordionTrigger>Is it accessible?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Yes. It adheres to the WAI-ARIA design pattern.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem Value="b">
        <AccordionTrigger>Is it styled?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Yes. It comes with default styles.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
    <AccordionItem Value="c">
        <AccordionTrigger>Is it animated?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Yes. Animated by default.</AccordionContent>
    </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

@code {
    private string _accordionTypeSel = "Single";
    private Accordion.AccordionType _activeAccordionType => Enum.Parse<Accordion.AccordionType>(_accordionTypeSel);
    private List<Segmented.SegmentedOption> _accordionTypeOptions = new()
    {
        new() { Label = "Single", Value = "Single" },
        new() { Label = "Multiple", Value = "Multiple" },
    };
}

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The panel's content, revealed when the parent AccordionItem is open.

parameters.name: ForceMount
parameters.description: Preserved for API compatibility only — Accordion content is now always mounted to allow the grid-rows close animation to run on collapse, so this flag is effectively a no-op. Children always live in the DOM whether the panel is open or closed.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the panel's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the panel's root element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The item's trigger and content, typically an AccordionTrigger followed by an AccordionContent.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The item's unique identifier, used by the parent Accordion to track which items are open.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the item's trigger and excludes it from roving keyboard focus. Default false.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the item's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the item's root element.

records.name: AccordionItemContext
records.signature: AccordionItemContext(string Value, bool Disabled, string TriggerId, string ContentId)

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The trigger's label content, rendered before the chevron icon.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger button.

parameters.name: HeadingLevel
parameters.description: Heading level (the aria-level on the role="heading" wrapper) so the accordion's headers sit at the right place in the document outline for screen-reader heading navigation. Default 3 (matching the WAI-ARIA accordion example); set 2/4/... to fit the surrounding page.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the trigger button.

Affix.name: Affix
Affix.description: Pins an element to the viewport edge as the user scrolls.

parameters.name: OffsetTop
parameters.description: Distance in pixels from the top of the scroll Target (or the window) at which the content becomes fixed. Default: 0.

parameters.name: OffsetBottom
parameters.description: Distance in pixels from the bottom of the scroll Target (or the window) at which the content becomes fixed, anchoring to the bottom edge instead of the top. Null (the default) disables bottom-anchoring.

parameters.name: Target
parameters.description: CSS selector of the scrollable container to watch. Null (the default) watches the window's scroll position.

parameters.name: OnChange
parameters.description: Invoked whenever the fixed state toggles; the argument is true once the content becomes fixed and false when it unsticks.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the root div.

events.name: OnChange
events.description: Invoked whenever the fixed state toggles; the argument is true once the content becomes fixed and false when it unsticks.

AgentMessageList.name: AgentMessageList
AgentMessageList.description: Chat message stream for AI agents with role-based styling.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The message rows to render (typically a sequence of AgentMessage). Ignored when Virtualize is true and Items is supplied.

parameters.name: AutoScroll
parameters.description: When true (default), the list auto-scrolls to the bottom as new content arrives, via a JS interop observer registered on this render tree.

parameters.name: Virtualize
parameters.description: Opt-in virtualization for long threads. When true AND Items is supplied, messages render through Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.Virtualization.Virtualize{TItem} so only the rows near the viewport are in the DOM. Leave false to use the simple ChildContent path (unchanged default).

parameters.name: Items
parameters.description: The per-message render fragments to virtualize. Each entry is rendered as one row (typically an AgentMessage). Only used when Virtualize is true; ignored otherwise.

parameters.name: ItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated row height in pixels for the virtualizer (default 80).

parameters.name: OverscanCount
parameters.description: Extra rows to render above/below the viewport to smooth scrolling (default 4).

parameters.name: ShowScrollButton
parameters.description: When true (default), shows a floating scroll-to-latest button that appears whenever the list is scrolled away from the bottom and, when clicked, jumps back to the newest message. Screen-reader labelled. Set false to hide it (e.g. lists that are never longer than the viewport).

parameters.name: EmptyState
parameters.description: Renders in place of the messages when IsEmpty is true (shadcn ConversationEmptyState parity). When null, a localized default title + description is shown instead.

parameters.name: IsEmpty
parameters.description: When true, the EmptyState slot (or the built-in default) is rendered instead of the message content. The consumer drives this since the list can't introspect an arbitrary ChildContent.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the scroll container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root scroll container.

parameters.name: Role
parameters.description: Which conversation turn this message represents (default Assistant). Drives the bubble/row layout: User right-aligns in a filled bubble, Assistant left-aligns as plain text, System centers as a muted pill, and Tool renders ChildContent unwrapped edge-to-edge.

parameters.name: Avatar
parameters.description: Avatar rendered beside User/Assistant messages (e.g. an Avatar component). Omitted for System/Tool turns.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Display name shown above the message alongside Timestamp. Omitted when both are unset.

parameters.name: Timestamp
parameters.description: When set, renders a machine-readable <time> element formatted per TimestampFormat/TimestampCulture and (optionally) converted to TimeZone.

parameters.name: TimeZone
parameters.description: Time zone to render Timestamp in. When set, the timestamp is converted to this zone — pass the END USER'S zone here (in Blazor Server the component runs on the server, so the previous ToLocalTime() showed the SERVER'S zone, which is the bug #303 fixed). When null, the timestamp is rendered in the offset it already carries (a DateTimeOffset knows its own offset), so it is deterministic and server-independent.

parameters.name: TimestampFormat
parameters.description: Custom format string for the visible timestamp (default "HH:mm").

parameters.name: TimestampCulture
parameters.description: Culture used to format the timestamp (defaults to CurrentCulture).

parameters.name: IsStreaming
parameters.description: When true, appends a blinking caret after the message body to indicate content is still arriving (e.g. token streaming).

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The message body content (markdown, plain text, tool output, etc.).

parameters.name: Actions
parameters.description: Optional actions toolbar rendered beneath the message body — typically an AgentMessageActions (copy / regenerate / retry) and/or an AgentMessageBranchNav. Only shown for User and Assistant turns.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the message's root element (the bubble, row, or pill, depending on Role).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the message's root element.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Accessible name for the button — used for aria-label, the visually-hidden label, and the tooltip fallback. Required for a11y.

parameters.name: Icon
parameters.description: Icon to render (e.g. LumeoIcons.Copy). Ignored when ChildContent is supplied.

parameters.name: Tooltip
parameters.description: Native title tooltip; defaults to Label when unset.

parameters.name: OnClick
parameters.description: Invoked when the action button is clicked (e.g. copy, regenerate, retry).

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the button and suppresses click handling.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Custom button content (overrides Icon).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the button.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the button.

events.name: OnClick
events.description: Invoked when the action button is clicked (e.g. copy, regenerate, retry).

parameters.name: CopyText
parameters.description: Text copied to the clipboard by the built-in Copy action. When null, no Copy button is rendered.

parameters.name: OnCopy
parameters.description: Invoked after a successful copy (receives the copied text).

parameters.name: OnRegenerate
parameters.description: When set, renders a Regenerate action invoking this callback.

parameters.name: OnRetry
parameters.description: When set, renders a Retry action invoking this callback.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Additional custom actions appended after the built-ins.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the toolbar's root role="group" element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the toolbar's root element.

events.name: OnCopy
events.description: Invoked after a successful copy (receives the copied text).

events.name: OnRegenerate
events.description: When set, renders a Regenerate action invoking this callback.

events.name: OnRetry
events.description: When set, renders a Retry action invoking this callback.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Current branch, 0-based. Two-way bindable via @bind-Index.

parameters.name: IndexChanged
parameters.description: Raised whenever the previous/next buttons move to a different branch, carrying the new 0-based index. Pair with Index for @bind-Index.

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: Total number of branches.

parameters.name: Loop
parameters.description: When true, previous/next wrap around the ends instead of disabling at the first/last branch.

parameters.name: OnPrevious
parameters.description: Invoked (after IndexChanged) when moving to the previous branch.

parameters.name: OnNext
parameters.description: Invoked (after IndexChanged) when moving to the next branch.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root role="group" element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root element.

events.name: IndexChanged
events.description: Raised whenever the previous/next buttons move to a different branch, carrying the new 0-based index. Pair with Index for @bind-Index.

events.name: OnPrevious
events.description: Invoked (after IndexChanged) when moving to the previous branch.

events.name: OnNext
events.description: Invoked (after IndexChanged) when moving to the next branch.

Alert.name: Alert
Alert.description: Inline callout for status, warning, or informational messages.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Extra content rendered below the Title/Description text, inside the body column.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: The alert's semantic tone, driving its border/background color and default icon. Default Default.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Controls padding, icon size and text size. Default Md.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-alert density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only the box padding shifts — text/icon size stay governed by Size.

parameters.name: IsDismissible
parameters.description: Shows a close button that dismisses the alert on click. Default false.

parameters.name: OnDismiss
parameters.description: Invoked when the alert is dismissed, either via the close button or AutoDismissMs expiring.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The alert's bold heading line, rendered above Description.

parameters.name: Description
parameters.description: The alert's body text, rendered below Title at reduced opacity.

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component.

parameters.name: ShowIcon
parameters.description: Renders the default variant icon (or IconContent if set) beside the text. Default true.

parameters.name: AutoDismissMs
parameters.description: Milliseconds until the alert dismisses itself automatically. Null (the default) disables auto-dismiss.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the alert's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the alert's root element.

events.name: OnDismiss
events.description: Invoked when the alert is dismissed, either via the close button or AutoDismissMs expiring.

examples.title: Default

<Alert>

AlertDialog.name: AlertDialog
AlertDialog.description: Modal confirmation dialog that interrupts the user for destructive actions.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The dialog's content — typically an AlertDialogTrigger plus an AlertDialogContent. Rendered inside a cascading AlertDialogContext that descendants read to coordinate open state and focus.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Controlled open state. Supplying this parameter (or OpenChanged) switches the dialog into controlled mode, where the consumer owns the open/closed state; otherwise it falls back to DefaultOpen and manages state internally.

parameters.name: OpenChanged
parameters.description: Raised whenever the open state changes (trigger click, Escape, Cancel/Action buttons, or a cancelled/completed OnBeforeClose). Pair with Open for @bind-Open.

parameters.name: DefaultOpen
parameters.description: Uncontrolled initial open state (Radix defaultOpen). Used only when the consumer does NOT bind Open: the dialog then owns its open state internally, seeded from this value. Ignored when Open is supplied (controlled mode).

parameters.name: OnBeforeClose
parameters.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape key, the Cancel button, or the Action button). Set Cancel to true to abort. The Reason tells you which path fired ("escape", "cancel", "action").

parameters.name: IsOpen
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for Open; forwards straight through.

parameters.name: IsOpenChanged
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for OpenChanged; forwards straight through.

events.name: OpenChanged
events.description: Raised whenever the open state changes (trigger click, Escape, Cancel/Action buttons, or a cancelled/completed OnBeforeClose). Pair with Open for @bind-Open.

events.name: OnBeforeClose
events.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape key, the Cancel button, or the Action button). Set Cancel to true to abort. The Reason tells you which path fired ("escape", "cancel", "action").

events.name: IsOpenChanged
events.description: Deprecated alias for OpenChanged; forwards straight through.

records.name: AlertDialogContext
records.signature: AlertDialogContext(bool IsOpen, EventCallback<bool> SetOpen, string TitleId, string DescriptionId, Func<string, Task<bool>> TryDismiss, AlertDialogDescriptionRegistry DescriptionRegistry = null!, Action RegisterDescription = null!, Action UnregisterDescription = null!)

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<AlertDialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <AlertDialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Delete Account</Button>
    </AlertDialogTrigger>
    <AlertDialogContent>
        <AlertDialogHeader>
            <AlertDialogTitle>Are you absolutely sure?</AlertDialogTitle>
            <AlertDialogDescription>
                This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete your account
                and remove your data from our servers.
            </AlertDialogDescription>
        </AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogFooter>
            <AlertDialogCancel>Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>
            <AlertDialogAction>Continue</AlertDialogAction>
        </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
</AlertDialog>

@code {
    private bool _isOpen;
}

examples.title: With Action Handler
examples.code:
<AlertDialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <AlertDialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Destructive">Remove Item</Button>
    </AlertDialogTrigger>
    <AlertDialogContent>
        <AlertDialogHeader>
            <AlertDialogTitle>Remove this item?</AlertDialogTitle>
            <AlertDialogDescription>
                This item will be permanently removed from your collection.
            </AlertDialogDescription>
        </AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogFooter>
            <AlertDialogCancel>Keep It</AlertDialogCancel>
            <AlertDialogAction OnAction="HandleRemove">Yes, Remove</AlertDialogAction>
        </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
</AlertDialog>

@code {
    private bool _isOpen;
    private void HandleRemove() { /* handle removal */ }
}

examples.title: Unsaved Changes
examples.code:
<AlertDialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <AlertDialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost">
            <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.X)" class="h-4 w-4" /> Close Editor
        </Button>
    </AlertDialogTrigger>
    <AlertDialogContent>
        <AlertDialogHeader>
            <AlertDialogTitle>Discard unsaved changes?</AlertDialogTitle>
            <AlertDialogDescription>
                You have unsaved changes. If you close now, your changes will be lost.
            </AlertDialogDescription>
        </AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogFooter>
            <AlertDialogCancel>Keep Editing</AlertDialogCancel>
            <AlertDialogAction>Discard Changes</AlertDialogAction>
        </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
</AlertDialog>

examples.title: Loading Action
examples.code:
<AlertDialog @bind-Open="_loadingDialogOpen">
    <AlertDialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Destructive">Delete Account</Button>
    </AlertDialogTrigger>
    <AlertDialogContent>
        <AlertDialogHeader>
            <AlertDialogTitle>Are you absolutely sure?</AlertDialogTitle>
            <AlertDialogDescription>This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete your account.</AlertDialogDescription>
        </AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogFooter>
            <AlertDialogCancel>Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>
            <AlertDialogAction IsLoading="@_isDeleting" OnAction="HandleDelete">
                @(_isDeleting ? "Deleting..." : "Delete")
            </AlertDialogAction>
        </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
</AlertDialog>

@code {
    private bool _loadingDialogOpen;
    private bool _isDeleting;

    private async Task HandleDelete()
    {
        _isDeleting = true;
        await Task.Delay(2000);
        _isDeleting = false;
        _loadingDialogOpen = false;
    }
}

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The button's content, typically the confirming action label (e.g. "Delete").

parameters.name: OnAction
parameters.description: Invoked when the action button is clicked, before the dialog auto-closes (via Context.TryDismiss("action")) unless IsLoading is true.

parameters.name: IsLoading
parameters.description: When true, shows a spinner and disables the button, and suppresses the auto-close so the dialog stays open while an async action is in flight.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the button.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the button.

events.name: OnAction
events.description: Invoked when the action button is clicked, before the dialog auto-closes (via Context.TryDismiss("action")) unless IsLoading is true.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The button's content, typically the least-destructive "Cancel" label.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the button.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the button.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The dialog panel's content — typically an AlertDialogHeader/AlertDialogTitle/AlertDialogDescription plus an AlertDialogFooter with Cancel/Action buttons.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the panel.

parameters.name: PlayExitAnimation
parameters.description: When true (the default), closing keeps the panel mounted for a brief zoom-out (and backdrop fade-out) before unmounting instead of vanishing instantly — so a declarative alert dialog animates its close the same way OverlayProvider already animates service-opened ones. Set to false to opt OUT (immediate unmount) for tests or consumers who want an instant dismiss.

parameters.name: ZIndex
parameters.description: Backdrop z-index. Content sits at ZIndex + 1. Defaults to BaseZIndex; OverlayProvider supplies an incrementing value per nested overlay.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the dialog panel element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The description text, referenced by the dialog panel's aria-describedby.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description:
Additional CSS classes merged onto the

.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description:
Unmatched attributes splatted onto the

.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The footer content, typically an AlertDialogCancel and an AlertDialogAction.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the footer.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the footer.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The header content, typically an AlertDialogTitle and AlertDialogDescription.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the header.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the header.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The title text, referenced by the dialog panel's aria-labelledby.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <h2>.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the <h2>.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The trigger's content — typically a Button or text/icon markup that opens the dialog when clicked or activated via keyboard.

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary markup — receives the TriggerSlot to splat manually. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, renders NO wrapper and cascades a TriggerSlot a cooperating child (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot) folds onto its own single root element — the Radix asChild goal: one DOM node, no nested-interactive markup. Default false keeps the current role=button wrapper.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the wrapper (or forwarded via TriggerSlot when AsChild is true).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the wrapper (or merged into TriggerSlot when AsChild is true).

AnimatedBeam.name: AnimatedBeam
AnimatedBeam.description: SVG beam that traces an animated gradient path between two DOM elements.

parameters.name: ContainerId
parameters.description: The id of the container element that holds both the from/to nodes and this AnimatedBeam. Coordinates are measured relative to this element (matching Magic UI's containerRef pattern). If null, the SVG's own bounding rect is used as fallback.

parameters.name: FromId
parameters.description: The element id of the source node.

parameters.name: ToId
parameters.description: The element id of the destination node.

parameters.name: DurationMs
parameters.description: Duration of one full beam cycle in milliseconds.

parameters.name: DelayMs
parameters.description: Delay before the first animation cycle starts.

parameters.name: Curvature
parameters.description: Curvature of the bezier path. 0 = straight. Positive = bows up.

parameters.name: Reverse
parameters.description: Reverses the direction of travel.

parameters.name: GradientStartColor
parameters.description: Color at the start of the gradient.

parameters.name: GradientStopColor
parameters.description: Color at the end of the gradient.

parameters.name: PathColor
parameters.description: CSS color of the guide track path.

parameters.name: PathWidth
parameters.description: Width of the guide track in px.

parameters.name: PathOpacity
parameters.description: Opacity of the guide track (0–1).

parameters.name: BeamWidth
parameters.description: Width of the animated beam stroke in px.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root svg element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the root svg element.

AppBar.name: AppBar
AppBar.description: Top application bar with start, center, and end slots; sticky and elevated variants.

parameters.name: StartContent
parameters.description: Content pinned to the leading edge of the bar (e.g. logo, nav toggle), shrink-wrapped and never scrolled off.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The bar's main content, stretched to fill the space between StartContent and EndContent.

parameters.name: EndContent
parameters.description: Content pinned to the trailing edge of the bar (e.g. actions, avatar), shrink-wrapped and never scrolled off.

parameters.name: Sticky
parameters.description: When true (default), pins to the top of the viewport.

parameters.name: Elevated
parameters.description: When true, adds a subtle shadow beneath the bar.

parameters.name: Bordered
parameters.description: When true (default), adds a bottom border.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Tailwind height class for the bar. Default is "h-14".

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the bar's root header element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the bar's root header element.

AspectRatio.name: AspectRatio
AspectRatio.description: Constrains child content to a fixed width-to-height ratio.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The content (typically an image or video) constrained to the given Ratio, absolutely positioned to fill the box.

parameters.name: Ratio
parameters.description: The width-to-height ratio (e.g. 16.0/9) the box is padded to maintain. Default 1 (square). Non-finite or non-positive values fall back to 1:1.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the inner content wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the outer padding-box element.

parameters.name: Src
parameters.description: URL of the audio file to play. Bound directly to the underlying <audio> element's src; required.

parameters.name: Autoplay
parameters.description: Starts playback automatically once the media can play. Subject to browser autoplay policy — the UI reflects actual play/pause state from the element's own events, not this flag.

parameters.name: Loop
parameters.description: Restarts playback from the beginning when the track ends.

parameters.name: ShowDownload
parameters.description: Shows a download link (rendered as an anchor with download) pointing at Src. Hidden when Src is empty.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Track title shown above the transport controls. Omitted from the layout entirely when both Title and Artist are empty.

parameters.name: Artist
parameters.description: Artist name shown beneath Title in a smaller, muted style.

parameters.name: Cover
parameters.description: Custom cover art content rendered in the leading artwork tile. Falls back to a music-note icon when not provided.

parameters.name: PreloadMode
parameters.description: Value forwarded to the native <audio> element's preload attribute ("none", "metadata", or "auto"). Defaults to "metadata".

parameters.name: ShowSkipButtons
parameters.description: Show skip-back / skip-forward buttons (±SkipSeconds).

parameters.name: SkipSeconds
parameters.description: Seconds to jump per skip-button press / arrow key. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: ShowPlaybackRate
parameters.description: Show the playback-rate cycle button (0.5× → 2×).

parameters.name: PlaybackRates
parameters.description: Selectable playback rates, cycled by the rate button. Defaults to 0.5×, 1×, 1.25×, 1.5×, 2×.

parameters.name: ShowVolume
parameters.description: Show the volume slider next to the mute toggle.

parameters.name: OnTimeUpdate
parameters.description: Fires on every playback-position update (native timeupdate, plus skip/seek actions) with the new current time in seconds.

parameters.name: OnEnded
parameters.description: Fires when playback reaches the end of the track.

parameters.name: OnPlay
parameters.description: Fires when playback starts, driven by the element's own play event (not the button click) so it reflects actual playback state.

parameters.name: OnPause
parameters.description: Fires when playback pauses, driven by the element's own pause event (not the button click) so it reflects actual playback state.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the outer wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the outer wrapper.

events.name: OnTimeUpdate
events.description: Fires on every playback-position update (native timeupdate, plus skip/seek actions) with the new current time in seconds.

events.name: OnEnded
events.description: Fires when playback reaches the end of the track.

events.name: OnPlay
events.description: Fires when playback starts, driven by the element's own play event (not the button click) so it reflects actual playback state.

events.name: OnPause
events.description: Fires when playback pauses, driven by the element's own pause event (not the button click) so it reflects actual playback state.

examples.title: Playback events
examples.code:
<AudioPlayer Src="https://www.kozco.com/tech/piano2-CoolEdit.mp3"
             Title="Tracked sample"
             Artist="Cool Edit demo"
             OnPlay="HandlePlay"
             OnPause="HandlePause"
             OnEnded="HandleEnded" />

@@code {
    private string _lastEvent = "(none)";
    private void HandlePlay() => _lastEvent = "play";
    private void HandlePause() => _lastEvent = "pause";
    private void HandleEnded() => _lastEvent = "ended";
}

Avatar.name: Avatar
Avatar.description: Circular user image with initials fallback and status indicator.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The avatar's content — typically an AvatarImage and a fallback AvatarFallback (initials), rendered inside the shape-clipped inner wrapper.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Avatar size. Supported values: Sm, Md (default), Lg, Xl. Other values fall through to Md.

parameters.name: Shape
parameters.description: Avatar outline. Circle (default) and Square are literal contracts — a Circle avatar stays a circle in every theme. Themed follows the theme radius token instead: it renders identically to Circle at stock radii (the computed radius exceeds half the avatar size, so corners clamp to a full circle) and squares off along with the rest of the UI in sharp themes (--radius: 0) — pick it for sharp-brand designs.

parameters.name: Status
parameters.description: Presence indicator dot rendered at the bottom-end corner. None (default) shows no dot; otherwise a colour-coded dot is drawn with an aria-label from StatusLabel.

parameters.name: StatusLabel
parameters.description: Accessible label for the status dot (e.g. "Online"). Defaults to the AvatarStatus name; override to localize. Exposed as the dot's aria-label so the status isn't conveyed by colour alone (WCAG 1.4.1).

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name announced for the avatar. When set, the component renders role="img" + aria-label so a screen-reader has something to read. Useful for image-only avatars (no initials in ChildContent) where the user's name is the meaningful AT context.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the outer wrapper (also inherited by the shape-clipped inner wrapper's border/ring styling).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the outer wrapper.

examples.title: With Image
examples.code:
<Avatar>
    <AvatarImage Src="https://github.com/shadcn.png" Alt="User avatar" />
    <AvatarFallback>CN</AvatarFallback>

    <AvatarFallback>SM</AvatarFallback>
</Avatar>
<Avatar Size="Lumeo.Size.Lg">
    <AvatarImage Src="..." Alt="Large" />
    <AvatarFallback>LG</AvatarFallback>
</Avatar>

examples.title: Group
examples.code:
<Flex Class="-space-x-3">
    <Avatar Class="border-2 border-background">
        <AvatarImage Src="..." Alt="User 1" />
        <AvatarFallback>U1</AvatarFallback>
    </Avatar>
    <Avatar Class="border-2 border-background">
        <AvatarFallback>U2</AvatarFallback>
    </Avatar>
    <Avatar Class="border-2 border-background">
        <AvatarFallback>+3</AvatarFallback>
    </Avatar>
</Flex>

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The fallback content, typically the person's initials.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the fallback's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the fallback's root element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The group's members — a sequence of Avatars, which register with the group to compute overlap ordering and the Max cap.

parameters.name: Max
parameters.description: Maximum number of avatars to show before collapsing the rest into a single "+N" overflow chip. 0 (default) shows them all.

parameters.name: Total
parameters.description: Optional explicit total used to compute "+N". Set this when the group represents more people than the avatars actually rendered (e.g. "show 3 of 50"): the chip then reads Total - Max. When left at 0 the overflow is computed from the number of child avatars instead, so a plain group of N children with Max set "just works".

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the group's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the group's root element.

records.name: AvatarGroupContext
records.signature: AvatarGroupContext(AvatarGroupRegistrar Registrar, int Max)
records.description: Cascaded to child Avatars so the group can cap how many are visible. Tokens at an ordinal index >= Max report IsHidden true and collapse, leaving room for the "+N" chip.

parameters.name: Src
parameters.description: Image URL. A blank or whitespace-only value is treated as an immediate error, skipping the request and showing the sibling AvatarFallback right away.

parameters.name: Alt
parameters.description: Alt text for the ; defaults to an empty string (decorative) when unset.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the .

parameters.name: OnError
parameters.description: Invoked when the image fails to load (browser onerror), after the fallback has been switched in.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the .

events.name: OnError
events.description: Invoked when the image fails to load (browser onerror), after the fallback has been switched in.

BackToTop.name: BackToTop
BackToTop.description: Floating button that scrolls the page back to the top.

parameters.name: VisibilityThreshold
parameters.description: Scroll offset in pixels (default 300) past which the button becomes visible.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true, disables scroll tracking entirely — no JS scroll observer is registered and the button never renders, even past the threshold.

parameters.name: Target
parameters.description: Optional CSS selector for a scrollable container to track (and scroll back to the top of) instead of the window. When null (the default) the component observes window/document scrolling. (#98)

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Custom button content; defaults to an up-arrow icon when unset.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the button.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the button.

examples.title: Default
examples.code: <BackToTop />

examples.title: Custom Threshold
examples.code: <BackToTop VisibilityThreshold="500" />

examples.title: Custom Content
examples.code:
<BackToTop>
    <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.ChevronUp)" class="h-5 w-5" />
</BackToTop>

examples.title: Interactive: Visibility Threshold
examples.code: <BackToTop VisibilityThreshold="300" />

Badge.name: Badge
Badge.description: Small label for counts, statuses, or category tags.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The badge's content. Ignored when IsDot is true.

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Color variant. Default (primary) is applied when unset.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Badge size — drives text size + padding. Md (default) reproduces the historical badge metrics. Use Sm for dense inline tags / counts and Lg for prominent status chips — avoids consumer-side !px-* / !h-* / !text-* overrides.

parameters.name: Pill
parameters.description: Pill shape instead of the default rounded-md — for count/quota chips. Theme-radius-aware: renders as a full pill at stock radii and squares off along with the rest of the UI in sharp themes (--radius: 0).

parameters.name: IsDot
parameters.description: Renders the badge as a small filled dot instead of a chip with text — for status indicators. When set, ChildContent/IconContent/ IsRemovable are not rendered.

parameters.name: Pulse
parameters.description: Adds a pulsing ping ring around a dot badge. Only has an effect when IsDot is also true.

parameters.name: IsRemovable
parameters.description: Renders a trailing remove (×) button that raises OnRemove when clicked.

parameters.name: OnRemove
parameters.description: Invoked when the remove button is clicked. Only rendered when IsRemovable is true.

parameters.name: Animated
parameters.description: When true, the badge plays a one-shot pop-in entrance animation (scale + opacity) on mount. Default is false.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-badge density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Adjusts padding (Spacious also adds a touch of vertical padding); text size + radius are unchanged. Has no effect on dot badges.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the badge's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the badge's root element.

events.name: OnRemove
events.description: Invoked when the remove button is clicked. Only rendered when IsRemovable is true.

examples.title: Variants
examples.code:
<Badge>Default</Badge>
<Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Secondary">Secondary</Badge>
<Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Outline">Outline</Badge>
<Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Destructive">Destructive</Badge>
<Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Success">Success</Badge>
<Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Warning">Warning</Badge>

examples.title: Sizes & Pill
examples.code:
<Badge Size="Badge.BadgeSize.Sm">Small</Badge>
<Badge Size="Badge.BadgeSize.Md">Medium</Badge>
<Badge Size="Badge.BadgeSize.Lg">Large</Badge>

<Badge Pill="true">99+</Badge>
<Badge Pill="true" Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Secondary">New</Badge>
<Badge Pill="true" Size="Badge.BadgeSize.Lg" Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Success">Online</Badge>

examples.title: Variant (interactive)
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_variant">
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Badge.BadgeVariant.Default">Default</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Badge.BadgeVariant.Secondary">Secondary</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Badge.BadgeVariant.Outline">Outline</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Badge.BadgeVariant.Destructive">Destructive</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Badge.BadgeVariant.Success">Success</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Badge.BadgeVariant.Warning">Warning</SegmentedItem>
</Segmented>
<Badge Variant="@_variant">@_variant.ToString()</Badge>

@code { private Badge.BadgeVariant _variant = Badge.BadgeVariant.Default; }

examples.title: Usage Examples
examples.code:
<Flex Align="center" Gap="2">
    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Size="sm" Weight="medium">Status</Lumeo.Text>
    <Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Success">Active</Badge>
</Flex>
<Flex Align="center" Gap="2">
    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Size="sm" Weight="medium">Priority</Lumeo.Text>
    <Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Destructive">High</Badge>
</Flex>

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The data to encode. Interpretation depends on Format: any ASCII 32–126 string for Code 128, the 43-char Code 39 alphabet (auto-uppercased), or 12/13 digits for EAN-13 (a check digit is computed or validated). Empty renders nothing.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The symbology used to encode Value. Defaults to Code128.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Height in pixels of the bars themselves (excludes the human-readable text row, which adds 20px more when ShowText is enabled). Defaults to 80.

parameters.name: BarWidth
parameters.description: Width in pixels of one narrow module; every bar/space is a multiple of this value, and the quiet zone scales to 10× it. Defaults to 2.

parameters.name: ShowText
parameters.description: Whether the encoded text is rendered below the bars. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Theme color token (without the --color- prefix) used for the bars and text. Defaults to "foreground".

parameters.name: BackgroundColor
parameters.description: Theme color token (without the --color- prefix) used for the SVG background rect. Defaults to "background".

parameters.name: OnError
parameters.description: Fired after (re)encoding with the human-readable error message, or null when the value encoded successfully — a validation hook so a consumer can surface its own UI / gate a submit. (#291)

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root div.

events.name: OnError
events.description: Fired after (re)encoding with the human-readable error message, or null when the value encoded successfully — a validation hook so a consumer can surface its own UI / gate a submit. (#291)

examples.title: Default (Code 128B)
examples.code: <Barcode Value="HELLO-WORLD" />

examples.title: Numeric Data
examples.code: <Barcode Value="1234567890" />

examples.title: No Text
examples.code: <Barcode Value="ABC-123" ShowText="false" />

examples.title: Custom Height
examples.code:
<Barcode Value="SMALL" Height="40" />
<Barcode Value="MEDIUM" Height="80" />
<Barcode Value="TALL" Height="120" />

Bento.name: Bento
Bento.description: Masonry grid for dashboard tiles and marketing feature layouts.

parameters.name: Columns
parameters.description: Number of columns at the widest breakpoint. Values 1–6 get responsive Tailwind classes that collapse on mobile/tablet; other values fall back to an inline grid-template-columns with no responsive collapsing. Default 4.

parameters.name: Gap
parameters.description: Gap between tiles. Default Md.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the grid's root element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The grid's tiles, typically a sequence of BentoTile elements.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the grid's root element.

examples.title: Basic 4-column bento
examples.code:
$48,290

No events in the last hour.

4m 12s

Sm Md Lg

<Bento Columns="3" Gap="Bento.BentoGap.Md">

</Bento>

parameters.name: Span
parameters.description: Number of grid columns the tile spans at the lg: breakpoint (1–6); narrower breakpoints widen automatically for tiles wider than 1. Default 1.

parameters.name: RowSpan
parameters.description: Number of grid rows the tile spans at the lg: breakpoint (1–4); stays single-row on mobile so content height drives layout. Default 1.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The tile's heading text, shown above Description when HeaderContent isn't supplied.

parameters.name: Description
parameters.description: The tile's subheading text, shown below Title when HeaderContent isn't supplied.

parameters.name: HeaderContent
parameters.description: Custom header content, replacing the default Title/Description rendering.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The tile's main body content.

parameters.name: FooterContent
parameters.description: Content rendered below a top border at the bottom of the tile.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the tile's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the tile's root element.

BlurFade.name: BlurFade
BlurFade.description: Motion primitive: blur + fade-in on mount or when in view.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The content that blurs and fades into view as it enters the viewport.

parameters.name: DelayMs
parameters.description: Milliseconds to wait after the element enters the viewport before starting the animation. Default 0.

parameters.name: DurationMs
parameters.description: Duration of the blur/fade/slide transition in milliseconds. Default 600.

parameters.name: BlurPx
parameters.description: Starting blur radius in pixels, animated down to 0 as the element reveals. Default 8.

parameters.name: Yoffset
parameters.description: Starting vertical offset in pixels, animated down to 0 as the element reveals. Default 8.

parameters.name: Once
parameters.description: When true (default), the reveal animation plays only the first time the element enters the viewport; when false, it replays on every entry.

parameters.name: ForceHidden
parameters.description: When true, always start in the hidden state and animate in — even if the element is already in the viewport at mount. Default (false) is prerender-friendly: above-the-fold BlurFades stay statically visible to avoid a flash of blurred content on first paint. Set to true for docs demos or controlled "Replay" scenarios where you always want the animation to play.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root element; a caller-supplied style is merged with the blur CSS variables rather than overwritten.

BorderBeam.name: BorderBeam
BorderBeam.description: Animated gradient border beam effect for hero elements.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Content wrapped inside the beamed border, rendered as the element the animated border traces around.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Thickness of the animated border beam. Defaults to Md (1.5px).

parameters.name: DurationMs
parameters.description: How long, in milliseconds, one full loop of the beam animation takes. Defaults to 8000 (8s).

parameters.name: ColorFrom
parameters.description: Starting color of the beam. Accepts any CSS color — defaults to var(--color-primary).

parameters.name: ColorTo
parameters.description: Ending color of the beam. Accepts any CSS color — defaults to var(--color-primary).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root div.

BottomNav.name: BottomNav
BottomNav.description: Mobile-first bottom navigation bar with icon items.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The BottomNavItem (and optionally BottomNavFab) elements rendered inside the nav bar.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible label for the nav landmark. Defaults to "Bottom navigation".

parameters.name: Fixed
parameters.description: When true (default), pins to the bottom of the viewport with position: fixed. Set false to render inline.

parameters.name: ReserveSpace
parameters.description: When true (and Fixed is on), also renders an in-flow, aria-hidden spacer equal to the nav's height plus the bottom safe-area inset, so the fixed bar never overlaps the end of the page content — no consumer padding-bottom hack needed. The height is driven by the --lumeo-bottom-nav-height CSS variable (default 3.5rem); override it if your nav is taller (e.g. the Pill variant or two-line labels). Place BottomNav at the end of the scrolling content so the spacer reserves room there. Default false.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual variant. Default is a solid bar with a top border; Pill is a floating centered pill.

parameters.name: AnimatedIndicator
parameters.description: When true, each BottomNavItem gets smooth background-color and color transitions, and the small active pill transitions its transform with a spring easing. Default is false.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root nav element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes are splatted onto the root nav element.

<BottomNav>
    <BottomNavItem Href="/" Label="Home">
        <IconContent><Icon Name="House" Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" /></IconContent>

    </BottomNavItem>

    </BottomNavItem>
    <BottomNavItem Href="/profile" Label="Profile">
        <IconContent><Icon Name="User" Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" /></IconContent>

</BottomNav>

</BottomNavItem>

<BottomNav>
    <BottomNavItem Href="/" Label="Home" />
    <BottomNavItem Href="/search" Label="Search" />

    <BottomNavFab AriaLabel="Create" OnClick="HandleCreate">
        <Icon Name="Plus" Size="Lumeo.Size.Md" />
    </BottomNavFab>

    <BottomNavItem Href="/inbox" Label="Inbox" />
