    <Pills>
        @if (_search != "")
        {
            <FilterPill Label="Search" Value="@_search" OnDismiss="@(() => _search = "")" />
        }
    </Pills>
</FilterBar>

examples.title: Filters with multi-select
examples.code:
<FilterBar>
    <ChildContent>
        <DropdownMenu>
            <DropdownMenuTrigger>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
                    Category: @_categories.Count selected
                </Button>
            </DropdownMenuTrigger>
            <DropdownMenuContent>
                @foreach (var cat in _allCategories)
                {
                    <Flex Gap="2" Align="center" Class="px-2 py-1.5">
                        <Checkbox Checked="@_categories.Contains(cat)"
                                  CheckedChanged="@(v => Toggle(cat, v))" />
                        <Lumeo.Text As="span" Size="sm">@cat</Lumeo.Text>
                    </Flex>
                }
            </DropdownMenuContent>
        </DropdownMenu>
    </ChildContent>
    <Pills>
        @foreach (var cat in _categories)
        {
            <FilterPill Label="Category" Value="@cat"
                        OnDismiss="@(() => _categories.Remove(cat))" />
        }
    </Pills>
</FilterBar>

Flex.name: Flex

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.description: Flex-direction keyword appended to the flex- Tailwind prefix (e.g. "row", "col", "row-reverse"). Defaults to "row".

parameters.name: Gap

parameters.name: Align
parameters.description: Cross-axis alignment keyword appended to items- (e.g. "center", "start", "stretch"). Omitted when null/empty.

parameters.name: Justify

parameters.name: Wrap

parameters.name: Inline

Form.name: Form
Form.description: EditForm wrapper with styled validation, field groups, and submit state.

parameters.name: Model
parameters.description: The form's data model. A JSON snapshot is captured on init (and re-captured whenever a new instance is bound) so ResetValues can restore it later.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The form's fields — typically a sequence of FormFields wrapping bound inputs.

parameters.name: OnValidSubmit
parameters.description: Invoked with Model on submit when validation passes (no Validator, or it reports no errors).

parameters.name: OnInvalidSubmit
parameters.description: Invoked with Model on submit when Validator reports validation errors.

parameters.name: Validator
parameters.description: Optional synchronous validator run on submit and via ValidateField; its errors are merged into the cascaded FormContext alongside any async field-level errors.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <form> element.

parameters.name: ModelTypeInfo
parameters.description: Optional source-generated JsonTypeInfo{T} for TModel, used for the init/ResetValues snapshot round-trip instead of reflection-based serialization. Without this, the snapshot uses JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize<TModel> via reflection: TModel itself carries [DynamicallyAccessedMembers(All)] so ITS members survive trimming, but that annotation does NOT cascade into the types of TModel's own reference-type properties (e.g. a nested Address POCO) — their members can still be trimmed away, silently dropping nested values on ResetValues. Pass your own JsonSerializerContext-generated MyModelContext.Default.TModel here to cover the whole object graph and be fully trim-safe (#364 review).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the <form> element.

events.name: OnValidSubmit
events.description: Invoked with Model on submit when validation passes (no Validator, or it reports no errors).

events.name: OnInvalidSubmit
events.description: Invoked with Model on submit when Validator reports validation errors.

examples.title: Input with Validation
examples.code:
<FormField Name="email" Label="Email" Required="true" Error="@_emailError" HelpText="We will never share your email.">
    <Input Type="email" Placeholder="you@example.com" @bind-Value="_emailValue" />
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateEmail">Validate</Button>

@code {
    private string? _emailValue;
    private string? _emailError;

    private void ValidateEmail()
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_emailValue) || !_emailValue.Contains('@'))
            _emailError = "Please enter a valid email address.";
        else
            _emailError = null;
    }
}

examples.title: Select with Validation
examples.code:
<FormField Name="role" Label="Role" Required="true" Error="@_roleError">
    <Select @bind-Value="_roleValue">
        <SelectTrigger>
            <Text As="span" Class="@(_roleValue == null ? \"text-muted-foreground\" : "")">
                @(_roleValue ?? "Select a role")
            </Text>
        </SelectTrigger>
        <SelectContent>
            <SelectItem Value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem Value="editor">Editor</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem Value="viewer">Viewer</SelectItem>
        </SelectContent>
    </Select>
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateRole">Validate</Button>

@code {
    private string? _roleValue;
    private string? _roleError;

    private void ValidateRole()
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(_roleValue))
            _roleError = "Please select a role.";
        else
            _roleError = null;
    }
}

examples.title: Checkbox and Switch with Validation

<FormField Name="terms" Error="@_termsError">
    <Flex Gap="2" Align="center">
        <Checkbox @bind-Checked="_termsChecked" />
        <Label>I accept the terms and conditions</Label>
    </Flex>
</FormField>
<FormField Name="notifications" Label="Email Notifications" HelpText="Receive updates about your account.">
    <Switch @bind-Checked="_notifChecked" />
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateTerms">Submit</Button>

@code {
    private bool _termsChecked;
    private bool _notifChecked = true;
    private string? _termsError;

    private void ValidateTerms()
    {
        _termsError = _termsChecked ? null : "You must accept the terms to continue.";
    }
}

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Helper text describing the field, rendered below its label.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description:
Additional CSS classes merged onto the

.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description:
Unmatched attributes splatted onto the

.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label text rendered above (or, in Horizontal mode, beside) the control, linked via <label for> to ChildContent's bound input.

parameters.name: HelpText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the control when there is no active error.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.description: Manually-set error message, used when there's no parent Form context (or no Name) to source live errors from. A live error from the form's validation always takes precedence.

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, rendering a visual asterisk next to Label.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Layout direction: Vertical (default) stacks label above the control; Horizontal places a right-aligned label beside it in a two-column grid.

parameters.name: AutoRenderMessage
parameters.description: When true (default — the simple-mode API used by the vast majority of consumers), FormField renders the validation error itself below the control. Set false when you compose the field with a child FormMessage that should own the error rendering: this suppresses FormField's own error so the two never render it twice. The error still flows into the cascaded context either way, so a deferred FormMessage can pick it up.

parameters.name: LabelWidth
parameters.description: Fixed CSS width for the label column in Horizontal mode (e.g. "8rem"). Ignored in Vertical mode.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Field key used to look up live validation errors from the parent Form's context, and as the async-validation registration key.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The bound input control(s) this field wraps.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the field's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the field's root element.

parameters.name: AsyncValidator
parameters.description: Optional async validator. Receives the current Value, returns null when valid or an error message string otherwise. The result is stored in Errors against Name.

parameters.name: AsyncValidationDebounceMs
parameters.description: Debounce window in ms. 0 disables debouncing.

parameters.name: AsyncValidateOn
parameters.description: When async validation runs. Defaults to OnBlur.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Current value of the bound input. Required for OnChange auto-triggering; also passed to the validator on blur. If unset, consumers may invoke ValidateAsync(object?) directly.

parameters.name: OnValidatingChanged
parameters.description: Fired whenever IsValidating flips.

events.name: OnValidatingChanged
events.description: Fired whenever IsValidating flips.

records.name: FormFieldContext
records.signature: FormFieldContext(string? Name, string? Error, bool IsValidating, string ControlId = "", string? DescribedBy = null, bool AutoRenderMessage = true, string? ErrorId = null, string? LabelId = null)

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The item's content — typically a label, control, and description/message grouped as one field unit.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The label's text content.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <label>.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the <label>.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description:
Additional CSS classes merged onto the rendered error

.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description:
Unmatched attributes splatted onto the rendered error

.

parameters.name: Tasks
parameters.description: The tasks (and milestones) rendered as bars. Two-way bindable via TasksChanged, which also fires after an in-chart drag/resize/progress edit so the parent can persist it.

parameters.name: TasksChanged
parameters.description: Raised whenever the task set changes — either because the parent's Tasks genuinely changed, or because the user dragged a bar / changed its progress in the chart.

parameters.name: ViewMode
parameters.description: The active zoom level (Day/Week/Month/Year, plus the non-toolbar QuarterDay/HalfDay). Two-way bindable via ViewModeChanged; without a binding the component still tracks toolbar selections on its own. Default Day.

parameters.name: ViewModeChanged
parameters.description: Raised when the user picks a new zoom level from the toolbar. Bind it (@bind-ViewMode) to keep the parent's ViewMode in lock-step with the toolbar; without a binding the component still tracks the toolbar selection on its own (uncontrolled usage).

parameters.name: ZoomLevels
parameters.description: Restricts which zoom buttons the toolbar renders, in the given order. When null or empty the full default set (Day, Week, Month, Year) is shown — matching the previous hardcoded toolbar. Only toolbar-representable levels are drawn: QuarterDay / HalfDay have no toolbar button and are silently skipped here (still settable via ViewMode). Example: ZoomLevels="new[]{ GanttViewMode.Day, GanttViewMode.Week, GanttViewMode.Month }".

parameters.name: DefaultZoom
parameters.description: The zoom level selected on first render. When set it seeds the initial toolbar selection (and the level the timeline mounts at), overriding ViewMode's default — handy for uncontrolled usage, e.g. ZoomLevels="…" DefaultZoom="GanttViewMode.Week". After mount, ViewMode / @bind-ViewMode govern as before. Null falls back to ViewMode (the existing behaviour).

parameters.name: OnTaskClick
parameters.description: Raised when a task bar is clicked, with the clicked task.

parameters.name: OnDateChange
parameters.description: Raised after a task bar's start/end dates change via drag, with the updated task. Also folded into TasksChanged.

parameters.name: OnProgressChange
parameters.description: Raised after a task bar's progress changes via drag, with the updated task. Also folded into TasksChanged.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: CSS max-height of the chart's scrollable viewport (a CSS length string, e.g. "500px"). The chart shrinks to its content and only scrolls when the task list exceeds this height. Default "500px".

parameters.name: Readonly
parameters.description: Disables drag-to-reschedule and drag-to-progress editing in the underlying chart.

parameters.name: TodayHighlight
parameters.description: Highlight today with a vertical line and dot marker. Default true.

parameters.name: BarHeight
parameters.description: Override the pixel height of each task bar (default 22).

parameters.name: ColumnWidth
parameters.description: Override the pixel width of each date column for the current view mode.

parameters.name: BarColor
parameters.description: Per-task bar colour override. Return a CSS colour string (hex, rgb, or a CSS variable reference like "var(--color-destructive)") or null to use the primary theme colour.

parameters.name: GroupBy
parameters.description: Optional grouping function. When set, tasks are sorted by group label and a visual section-header row is injected before the first task in each group.

parameters.name: TrailingContent
parameters.description: Extra content rendered at the end of the toolbar row, alongside the zoom-level buttons.

parameters.name: OnInitError
parameters.description: Raised when the underlying JS chart fails to initialize, with the error message. The component also shows a destructive-styled error box in place of the chart and retries init on the next relevant parameter change.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the root container.

events.name: TasksChanged
events.description: Raised whenever the task set changes — either because the parent's Tasks genuinely changed, or because the user dragged a bar / changed its progress in the chart.

events.name: ViewModeChanged
events.description: Raised when the user picks a new zoom level from the toolbar. Bind it (@bind-ViewMode) to keep the parent's ViewMode in lock-step with the toolbar; without a binding the component still tracks the toolbar selection on its own (uncontrolled usage).

events.name: OnTaskClick
events.description: Raised when a task bar is clicked, with the clicked task.

events.name: OnDateChange
events.description: Raised after a task bar's start/end dates change via drag, with the updated task. Also folded into TasksChanged.

events.name: OnProgressChange
events.description: Raised after a task bar's progress changes via drag, with the updated task. Also folded into TasksChanged.

events.name: OnInitError
events.description: Raised when the underlying JS chart fails to initialize, with the error message. The component also shows a destructive-styled error box in place of the chart and retries init on the next relevant parameter change.

enums.name: GanttViewMode
enums.description: Gantt timeline zoom level. Supported by the Lumeo SVG engine: QuarterDay = 6-hour columns, HalfDay = 12-hour columns, Day = 1 column/day, Week = 1 column/week, Month = 1 column/month, Year = 1 column/year.

examples.title: Basic project plan
examples.code:
<Gantt Tasks="_tasks" ViewMode="GanttViewMode.Day" Height="420px" />

@code {
    private List<GanttTask> _tasks = new() {
        new("research",    "Research",    DateTime.Today,              DateTime.Today.AddDays(5)),
        new("design",      "Design",      DateTime.Today.AddDays(5),   DateTime.Today.AddDays(12), Dependencies: new[] { "research" }),
        new("development", "Development", DateTime.Today.AddDays(12),  DateTime.Today.AddDays(25), Dependencies: new[] { "design" }),
        new("testing",     "Testing",     DateTime.Today.AddDays(22),  DateTime.Today.AddDays(30), Dependencies: new[] { "development" }),
        new("launch",      "Launch",      DateTime.Today.AddDays(30),  DateTime.Today.AddDays(32), Dependencies: new[] { "testing" }),
    };
}

examples.title: With progress bars
examples.code:
<Gantt Tasks="_tasks" ViewMode="GanttViewMode.Day" Height="420px" />

@code {
    private List<GanttTask> _tasks = new() {
        new("research",    "Research",    DateTime.Today,             DateTime.Today.AddDays(5),  Progress: 100),
        new("design",      "Design",      DateTime.Today.AddDays(5),  DateTime.Today.AddDays(12), Progress: 60,  Dependencies: new[] { "research" }),
        new("development", "Development", DateTime.Today.AddDays(12), DateTime.Today.AddDays(25), Progress: 30,  Dependencies: new[] { "design" }),
        // ...
    };
}

examples.title: Drag to reschedule + change progress
examples.code:
<Gantt @bind-Tasks="_tasks"
       ViewMode="GanttViewMode.Day"
       OnDateChange="HandleDateChange"
       OnProgressChange="HandleProgressChange" />

@code {
    private IEnumerable<GanttTask> _tasks = new List<GanttTask> { /* ... */ };
    private GanttTask? _last;

    Task HandleDateChange(GanttTask t)     { _last = t; StateHasChanged(); return Task.CompletedTask; }
    Task HandleProgressChange(GanttTask t) { _last = t; StateHasChanged(); return Task.CompletedTask; }
}

examples.title: View modes
examples.code: <Gantt Tasks="_tasks" ViewMode="GanttViewMode.Week" Height="420px" />

Gauge.name: Gauge
Gauge.description: Single-value gauge with radial, arc, and linear variants and threshold colour bands.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The current value, clamped to Min–Max for both the drawn arc/bar and the announced aria-valuenow.

parameters.name: Min
parameters.description: Lower bound of the gauge's range (default 0).

parameters.name: Max
parameters.description: Upper bound of the gauge's range (default 100).

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual form: Radial (default, 270° arc with a gap at the bottom), Linear (horizontal bar), or Arc (180° semicircle).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Overall pixel size — width for Linear, diameter for Radial/Arc (default 120).

parameters.name: StrokeWidth
parameters.description: Thickness in pixels of the track/value stroke (default 8).

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Custom text shown in place of the computed percentage, and used as the meter's aria-label when set.

parameters.name: LabelContent
parameters.description: Custom center/label content — overrides the default text. Drop in a <NumberTicker> (Lumeo.Motion) here for an animated value, instead of duplicating count-up animation in core. (#277)

parameters.name: ShowValue
parameters.description: When true (default), renders the value/label text centered on (or below, for Linear) the gauge.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Theme color token (without the --color- prefix) for the filled value stroke/bar when no Thresholds match (default "primary").

parameters.name: TrackColor
parameters.description: Theme color token (without the --color- prefix) for the unfilled track (default "muted").

parameters.name: Thresholds
parameters.description: Optional value-based color overrides, evaluated highest-threshold-first: the first entry whose Value is <= the current Value wins, falling back to Color when none match.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the gauge's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the gauge's root role="meter" element.

records.name: GaugeThreshold
records.signature: GaugeThreshold(double Value, string Color)

Grid.name: Grid
Grid.description: CSS grid wrapper with columns + gap as props.

parameters.name: Columns
parameters.description: Number of grid columns (emitted as grid-cols-N, or the responsive 1–6 ramp when Responsive is true). Default 1.

parameters.name: Responsive
parameters.description: When true the grid collapses to 1 column on mobile and 2 on small screens, expanding to Columns only at the lg breakpoint — so a many-column grid stays readable on phones. Uses statically-known responsive utility strings (purge-safe) for 1–6 columns; falls back to a fixed grid-cols-N for other counts. Off by default (unchanged behavior). (#250)

parameters.name: Gap
parameters.description: Tailwind spacing scale value applied as gap-{value} on both axes. Default "4"; set null to omit.

parameters.name: RowGap
parameters.description: Tailwind spacing scale value applied as gap-y-{value}, overriding the row gap independently of Gap.

parameters.name: ColGap
parameters.description: Tailwind spacing scale value applied as gap-x-{value}, overriding the column gap independently of Gap.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The grid's items.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the grid's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the grid's root element.

Heading.name: Heading
Heading.description: Semantic h1-h6 heading with Lumeo typographic scale.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.description: Heading level 1–6, driving both the rendered h{Level} tag (unless As overrides the tag) and the default size/weight/tracking scale. Clamped to [1, 6] (default 2).

parameters.name: As
parameters.description: Render the heading's visual styling on this element tag instead of the semantic h{Level} (e.g. "p", "div", "span") — the asChild-style escape hatch for "looks like a heading, isn't one in the document outline". Visual only; supply role/aria-level via attributes for heading semantics. Null = the normal h{Level}. (#295)

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Text-size utility suffix (e.g. "xl", "2xl"), overriding the size the current Level would default to.

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.description: Font-weight utility suffix (e.g. "semibold"), overriding the weight the current Level would default to.

parameters.name: Tracking
parameters.description: Letter-spacing utility suffix (e.g. "tight"), overriding the tracking the current Level would default to. Levels 3–6 default to no tracking class unless set here.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The heading's text content.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the heading element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the heading element.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Plain-text headline, rendered as an h1 that also names the section landmark. Ignored (and the h1 omitted) when TitleSlot is used instead.

parameters.name: TitleSlot
parameters.description: Rich content slot for the headline, for when a plain-text Title isn't enough (e.g. inline styling, a gradient span). Rendered alongside Title if both are set.

parameters.name: Subtitle
parameters.description: Plain-text supporting copy below the title. Ignored when SubtitleSlot is used instead.

parameters.name: SubtitleSlot
parameters.description: Rich content slot for the supporting copy, for when plain-text Subtitle isn't enough.

parameters.name: Eyebrow
parameters.description: Small chip/label rendered above the title (e.g. "New" or a category tag).

parameters.name: Actions
parameters.description: Call-to-action buttons/links rendered below the subtitle.

parameters.name: Media
parameters.description: Side illustration/media slot. When set, the hero switches to a two-column grid layout with the text on one side and this media on the other.

parameters.name: Alignment
parameters.description: Horizontal alignment of the text column and its actions. Default: Start.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root section.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the root section.

Highlighter.name: Highlighter
Highlighter.description: Wraps occurrences of one or more search terms in the text with highlight marks.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The full source text to render; matched terms are wrapped in Tag, the rest is emitted verbatim.

parameters.name: Highlight
parameters.description: A single term to highlight within Text. Combined with HighlightTerms when both are set (de-duplicated, longest match wins).

parameters.name: HighlightTerms
parameters.description: Multiple terms to highlight within Text. Combined with Highlight when both are set (de-duplicated, longest match wins).

parameters.name: CaseSensitive
parameters.description: Matches terms case-sensitively. Defaults to false (case-insensitive matching).

parameters.name: WholeWord
parameters.description: Only matches terms on word boundaries (anchored with \b on sides adjacent to a word character), so e.g. "cat" won't match inside "category". Ignored when RegexMode is enabled.

parameters.name: RegexMode
parameters.description: Treat Highlight / HighlightTerms as regular- expression patterns instead of literal text (the default). Invalid patterns fall back to rendering the text un-highlighted. WholeWord is ignored in this mode — express boundaries in the pattern itself. (#293)

parameters.name: HighlightClass
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes appended to the built-in highlight-mark styling (bg-warning/30 text-foreground rounded-sm px-0.5) on each matched segment.

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: Element name used to wrap each matched segment. Defaults to "mark"; falls back to "mark" if set to whitespace/empty.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the outer <span> wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the outer <span> wrapper.

examples.title: Single Term
examples.code: <Highlighter Text="The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog" Highlight="fox" />

examples.title: Case Sensitive
examples.code:
<!-- Case insensitive (default) -->
<Highlighter Text="Blazor is awesome. blazor runs on .NET." Highlight="blazor" />

<!-- Case sensitive -->
<Highlighter Text="Blazor is awesome. blazor runs on .NET." Highlight="Blazor" CaseSensitive="true" />

examples.title: Whole Word
examples.code:
<!-- Partial matches -->
<Highlighter Text="highlight highlighting highlighted" Highlight="highlight" />

<!-- Whole word only -->
<Highlighter Text="highlight highlighting highlighted" Highlight="highlight" WholeWord="true" />

examples.code:

<Highlighter Text="@sampleText" Highlight="@_searchTerm" />

HoverCard.name: HoverCard
HoverCard.description: Popover that opens on hover for rich previews.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The hover card's parts: typically a HoverCardTrigger and a HoverCardContent.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Whether the card is open. Uncontrolled by default (self-managed by hover/tap-pin); bind OpenChanged for controlled usage.

parameters.name: OpenChanged
parameters.description: Two-way callback for Open, fired as hover/tap-pin state changes.

parameters.name: IsOpen
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for Open.

parameters.name: IsOpenChanged
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for OpenChanged.

parameters.name: OpenDelay
parameters.description: Milliseconds the pointer must hover the trigger before the card opens. Default 200.

parameters.name: CloseDelay
parameters.description: Milliseconds after the pointer leaves before the card closes; cancelled if the pointer re-enters trigger or content first. Default 300.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the wrapper element.

events.name: OpenChanged
events.description: Two-way callback for Open, fired as hover/tap-pin state changes.

events.name: IsOpenChanged
events.description: Deprecated alias for OpenChanged.

records.name: HoverCardContext
records.signature: HoverCardContext(bool IsOpen, EventCallback RequestOpen, EventCallback RequestClose, EventCallback RequestTogglePin, string WrapperId)

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The card's content.

parameters.name: Align
parameters.description: Alignment of the card along the trigger's edge. Default Center.

parameters.name: Side
parameters.description: Which side of the trigger the card is anchored to. Default Bottom.

parameters.name: ShowArrow
parameters.description: Renders a small pointer triangle on the edge facing the trigger. Default false.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the card's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the card's root element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The trigger's content.

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" and wire @onmouseenter="slot.OnMouseEnter.InvokeAsync" (etc.) onto their own single element. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, the trigger renders NO wrapper element and instead cascades a TriggerSlot that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element — yielding ONE DOM node with the hover/focus open-close handlers, the pin toggle and aria-expanded forwarded (the Radix asChild goal). The child must resolve to exactly one cooperating element (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot splat). Default false preserves the current inline-flex wrapper exactly.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger element (or the TriggerSlot in AsChild mode).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the trigger element (or merged into the TriggerSlot in AsChild mode).

Icon.name: Icon
Icon.description: Icon wrapper — renders the built-in LumeoIcons set (Lucide-derived) or any IconSource natively.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Lucide icon name (PascalCase, e.g. "Search", "ChevronDown", "TrendingUp"). Unknown names fall back to a Circle. Use Svg to supply any icon directly (any IconSource, e.g. from a Lumeo icon pack).

parameters.name: Svg
parameters.description: Direct IconSource reference — takes precedence over Name. Lets consumers supply any vendored icon (e.g. LumeoIcons.Search or an icon-pack member).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Size preset. Sets Tailwind h-{n} w-{n} on the SVG. Defaults to Md (h-4 w-4).

parameters.name: StrokeWidth
parameters.description: Overrides the IconSource's stroke width on the rendered svg. Only affects Stroke-style icons (Lucide, Tabler); ignored for Fill sets (Phosphor, Material, Bootstrap) where stroke is not used. When null (default) the icon source's own StrokeWidth is used.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Accessible name for the icon. When set, the icon is exposed to assistive technology as role="img" with this string as its aria-label (and a <title> for tooltip/hover parity). When left null the icon is treated as decorative and hidden from screen readers via aria-hidden="true" — the correct default for icons that sit beside a visible text label (a button's own text already conveys the meaning). A consumer-supplied role/aria-label/aria-hidden/ aria-labelledby in attributes always wins over these defaults.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the rendered <svg> (after Size's dimension classes).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the rendered <svg>. Any of role/aria-label/aria-labelledby/aria-hidden/title passed here suppresses the automatic Title-driven a11y defaults entirely.

parameters.name: Svg
parameters.description: The icon to render. Its Content becomes the inner markup and its ViewBox/RenderStyle/ StrokeWidth style the root <svg>.

parameters.name: StrokeWidth
parameters.description: Optional root stroke-width override for Stroke icons. When null (default) the icon's own StrokeWidth is used; ignored for Fill icons.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Arbitrary attributes (class, aria-*, role, title, …) splatted onto the root <svg>. Sizing is done here via classes (e.g. h-4 w-4), exactly like the icon component it replaces. A consumer-supplied attribute that collides with one this component sets (e.g. fill) wins, because the splat is applied last.

Image.name: Image
Image.description: Image with lazy-loading, loading skeleton, and error fallback.

parameters.name: Src
parameters.description: The image URL. When it fails to load, Fallback (or the built-in broken-image placeholder) is shown instead.

parameters.name: Alt
parameters.description: Alt text for the image, also used to build the preview trigger's accessible name.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Width in pixels applied as the width attribute. When both this and Height are unset, the image fills its parent.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Height in pixels applied as the height attribute. When both this and Width are unset, the image fills its parent.

parameters.name: Lazy
parameters.description: Sets loading="lazy" on the so the browser defers loading until the image nears the viewport.

parameters.name: Fallback
parameters.description: URL swapped in when Src fails to load. When null, a muted placeholder with a broken-image icon is shown instead.

parameters.name: Preview
parameters.description: Enables a click-to-zoom overlay: a hover/focus trigger opens the image fullscreen with zoom in/out controls and Escape-to-close.

parameters.name: Zoomable
parameters.description: When true, registers a two-finger pinch-zoom gesture on the image container so touch users can zoom in/out on the image directly (independent of the fullscreen Preview overlay). Sets touch-action: none on the wrapper so the browser doesn't intercept the gesture for native page zoom.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root div.

examples.title: Basic Image
examples.code: <Image Src="photo.jpg" Alt="Sample" Width="400" Height="250" />

examples.title: With Preview
examples.code: <Image Src="photo.jpg" Preview="true" />

examples.title: Gallery
examples.code: <ImageGallery Images="@_images" Columns="4" />

examples.title: Pinch-to-Zoom
examples.code: <Image Src="photo.jpg" Zoomable="true" Alt="Pinch to zoom on mobile" />

parameters.name: Images
parameters.description: The images to display as a grid of thumbnails, each opening the fullscreen preview overlay on click.

parameters.name: Columns
parameters.description: Number of grid columns, from 1 to 6. Any other value falls back to 3.

parameters.name: Gap
parameters.description: Tailwind gap class between thumbnails. Defaults to "gap-2".

parameters.name: GesturesEnabled
parameters.description: When true (default), enables horizontal swipe (prev/next image) and pinch-to-zoom gestures on the fullscreen preview overlay on touch devices.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the thumbnail grid div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the thumbnail grid div.

records.name: ImageItem
records.signature: ImageItem(string Src, string? Alt = null)

ImageCompare.name: ImageCompare
ImageCompare.description: Before/after slider comparison for two images.

parameters.name: BeforeSrc
parameters.description: URL of the "before" image, clipped by the divider and drawn on top of AfterSrc.

parameters.name: AfterSrc
parameters.description: URL of the "after" image, drawn full-size behind BeforeSrc.

parameters.name: BeforeLabel
parameters.description: Badge text shown over the before image. Also doubles as its accessible name (via alt) unless blank, in which case a localized fallback is used. Defaults to "Before".

parameters.name: AfterLabel
parameters.description: Badge text shown over the after image. Also doubles as its accessible name (via alt) unless blank, in which case a localized fallback is used. Defaults to "After".

parameters.name: InitialPosition
parameters.description: Starting divider position as a percentage (0–100) along the comparison axis. Clamped to [0, 100] on init. Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: CSS width of the comparison container (any valid CSS length). Defaults to "100%".

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: CSS height of the comparison container (any valid CSS length). Defaults to "400px".

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Axis the divider drags along: Horizontal for a left/right slider, Vertical for top/bottom. Defaults to Horizontal.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the root container.

examples.title: Horizontal Comparison
examples.code:
<ImageCompare BeforeSrc="before.jpg" AfterSrc="after.jpg"
             BeforeLabel="Before" AfterLabel="After" Height="320px" />

examples.title: Vertical Comparison
examples.code:
<ImageCompare BeforeSrc="img1.jpg" AfterSrc="img2.jpg"
             Orientation="Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical"
             Height="320px" />

examples.title: Orientation (interactive)
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_orientation">
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Lumeo.Orientation.Horizontal">Horizontal</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical">Vertical</SegmentedItem>
</Segmented>
<ImageCompare BeforeSrc="before.jpg" AfterSrc="after.jpg"
             BeforeLabel="Before" AfterLabel="After"
             Orientation="@_orientation" Height="280px" />

@code { private Lumeo.Orientation _orientation = Lumeo.Orientation.Horizontal; }

examples.title: Custom Initial Position
examples.code:
<ImageCompare BeforeSrc="before.jpg" AfterSrc="after.jpg"
             InitialPosition="25" Height="280px" />

InplaceEditor.name: InplaceEditor
InplaceEditor.description: Click-to-edit text/number field that swaps in an input.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The committed value, shown in display mode and used to seed the field on entering edit mode.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Invoked with the new value when an edit is committed (Save, blur-save, or Enter).

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Custom display-mode content. When set, it renders instead of the plain Value/Placeholder text (e.g. a formatted or styled preview).

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: Placeholder text shown in display mode when Value and ChildContent are both empty.

parameters.name: EditMode
parameters.description: The edit control rendered on entering edit mode: a single-line input (default), a textarea, or a select bound to SelectOptions.

parameters.name: SelectOptions
parameters.description: Options rendered when EditMode is Select.

parameters.name: SaveOnBlur
parameters.description: Commits the edit when the field loses focus (unless ShowButtons is set, in which case blur is ignored so a click on Save/Cancel isn't pre-empted). Default: true.

parameters.name: SaveOnEnter
parameters.description: Commits the edit on Enter. Only applies to the single-line text mode. Default: true.

parameters.name: ShowButtons
parameters.description: Renders explicit Save/Cancel buttons next to the edit control instead of relying on blur/Enter/Escape alone.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables editing — the field stays in read-only display mode and cannot be activated.

parameters.name: OnSave
parameters.description: Invoked with the new value when an edit is committed, alongside ValueChanged.

parameters.name: OnCancel
parameters.description: Invoked when an edit is cancelled (Escape, or the Cancel button).

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, adding an asterisk to the label and setting aria-required.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Renders the field in an invalid/error state. Also set implicitly when inside a FormField that has a validation error.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error message shown below the field when Invalid is true and the field isn't inside a FormField (which renders its own error text).

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the field when there's no error, and the field isn't inside a FormField.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label rendered above the field. Ignored when inside a FormField, which renders its own label.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name attribute on the underlying input/textarea/select, for native form submission. Falls back to the enclosing FormField's name when unset.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the field's container div.

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Invoked with the new value when an edit is committed (Save, blur-save, or Enter).

events.name: OnSave
events.description: Invoked with the new value when an edit is committed, alongside ValueChanged.

events.name: OnCancel
events.description: Invoked when an edit is cancelled (Escape, or the Cancel button).

examples.title: Basic Inplace Edit
examples.code:
<InplaceEditor @bind-Value="_name" Placeholder="Click to edit..." />

@code {
    private string? _name = "John Doe";
}

examples.title: With Save / Cancel Buttons
examples.code: <InplaceEditor @bind-Value="_title" ShowButtons="true" Placeholder="Click to edit title..." />

examples.title: Textarea Mode
examples.code:
<InplaceEditor @bind-Value="_bio"
               EditMode="InplaceEditor.InplaceEditMode.Textarea"
               Placeholder="Click to add a bio..." />

examples.title: Select Mode
examples.code:
<InplaceEditor @bind-Value="_status"
               EditMode="InplaceEditor.InplaceEditMode.Select"
               SelectOptions="@_options" />

@code {
    private string? _status = "Active";
    private List<string> _options = new() { "Active", "Inactive", "Pending" };
}

Input.name: Input
Input.description: Styled text input with label, prefix/suffix, icons, error state.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The input's text value. Controlled together with ValueChanged; when left unbound the input tracks its own typing internally.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Fires on every edit (keystroke, clear, paste) with the new value.

parameters.name: OnInput
parameters.description: Raw oninput passthrough, fired alongside ValueChanged for consumers that need the underlying ChangeEventArgs.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true, the input is not editable/focusable and renders with reduced opacity.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: Content rendered before the text (e.g. an icon or unit). Forces the wrapped-input layout even while the field is empty.

parameters.name: Suffix
parameters.description: Content rendered after the text (e.g. an icon or unit). Forces the wrapped-input layout.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Controls the input's height, font size and horizontal padding. Defaults to Md.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Overrides the ambient DensityScope for this input's vertical rhythm and accessory padding. Falls back to the cascaded density, then Comfortable.

parameters.name: Clearable
parameters.description: When true, shows an X button that clears the value while it's non-empty. Forces the wrapped-input layout even when the field is empty, so the clear button never triggers a remount on the first/last character.

parameters.name: OnClear
parameters.description: Fires after the user clicks the clear button, once the value has already been reset to empty and echoed via ValueChanged.

parameters.name: ShowStepButtons
parameters.description: When true (the default) and the field is a type="number" input, renders the custom vertical ▲▼ stepper column (replacing the native browser spinner, which is always hidden for number inputs regardless of this setting). When false, no stepper buttons are rendered — a clean bare number field with no visible spinner at all; native ArrowUp/ArrowDown and mouse-drag-to-select still work since it's still a real <input type="number">. Has no effect on non-number inputs.

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, setting aria-required and (outside a FormField) appending a destructive-colored asterisk to the label.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Forces the invalid/error visual state (destructive border and focus ring) and aria-invalid, independent of the ambient FormField's validation state.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error message shown below the input (outside a FormField) when Invalid is set. Takes precedence over HelperText.

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the input (outside a FormField) when there is no error to display.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label text rendered above the input (outside a FormField, which supplies its own label).

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: HTML name attribute, used for native form submission and posted alongside the value.

parameters.name: ShowCount
parameters.description: Renders a character counter below the input, matching Textarea's pattern.

parameters.name: MaxLength
parameters.description: HTML maxlength on the input plus the limit shown in the counter. When null and ShowCount is true, only the current length is rendered.

parameters.name: CountFormat
parameters.description: Optional custom counter formatter (e.g. c => $"{c} of 60 chars"). Default renders 42 or 42/60 when MaxLength is set.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the underlying <input> on first render. The HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM because the browser's autofocus algorithm runs during initial document parsing — at parse time the WASM runtime hasn't booted yet and no <input> exists for it to focus. This parameter uses ElementReference.FocusAsync in OnAfterRenderAsync(firstRender) which is the canonical workaround.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual / semantic input variant. Default is the plain text input. Search auto-renders a search icon prefix, sets type="search" (gives platforms a clear-button + IME hints), and applies a search-friendly minimum width so the field doesn't collapse in narrow flex containers — replaces the min-w-[180px] max-w-md + manual icon prefix consumers previously shipped at every search field call-site.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the input (or its wrapper when Prefix/Suffix/Clearable/Search is active).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the underlying <input> element (not the wrapper), so placeholder/id/autocomplete/aria-* reach the focusable element.

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Fires on every edit (keystroke, clear, paste) with the new value.

events.name: OnInput
events.description: Raw oninput passthrough, fired alongside ValueChanged for consumers that need the underlying ChangeEventArgs.

events.name: OnClear
events.description: Fires after the user clicks the clear button, once the value has already been reset to empty and echoed via ValueChanged.

examples.title: Default
examples.code: <Input placeholder="Email" />

examples.title: With Binding
examples.code:
<Input Value="@_name" OnInput="@(e => _name = e.Value?.ToString() ?? "")" placeholder="Enter your name" />
<Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Value: @_name</Lumeo.Text>

@code {
    private string _name = "";
}

examples.title: File Input
examples.code: <Input type="file" />

examples.title: Disabled
examples.code: <Input placeholder="Disabled input" disabled />

InputMask.name: InputMask
InputMask.description: Masked input for phone numbers, dates, and custom patterns.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The unmasked (raw) value — only the mask-significant characters, without literals. Pair with ValueChanged for two-way binding.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Raised after each edit with the updated raw value.

parameters.name: Mask
parameters.description: The mask pattern: # matches a digit, A a letter, * any character; any other character is a literal inserted verbatim (e.g. "(###) ###-####").

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: Placeholder shown when empty. When unset, derived from Mask with each significant slot replaced by PromptChar.

parameters.name: PromptChar
parameters.description: Character used to fill unfilled mask slots when building the default placeholder. Defaults to '_'.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables interaction and dims the input.

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required: sets aria-required and renders a destructive-colored asterisk after Label.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Forces the invalid/error visual and aria-invalid, independent of any cascaded FormField error state.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error message shown below the input when invalid and not composed inside a FormField (which owns its own error display).

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the input when not invalid and not composed inside a FormField.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Visible label rendered above the input when not composed inside a FormField (which renders its own label).

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Field name for the native <input>. Falls back to the cascaded FormField's name when unset.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the input element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes are splatted onto the input element (an explicit id is ignored when inside a FormField).

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Raised after each edit with the updated raw value.

examples.title: Phone Number
examples.code: <InputMask @bind-Value="_phone" Mask="(###) ###-####" />

examples.title: Date Mask
examples.code: <InputMask @bind-Value="_date" Mask="##/##/####" Placeholder="MM/DD/YYYY" />

examples.title: Credit Card
examples.code: <InputMask @bind-Value="_card" Mask="#### #### #### ####" />

examples.title: IP Address
examples.code: <InputMask @bind-Value="_ip" Mask="###.###.###.###" />

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The board's KanbanColumns.

parameters.name: OnCardDrop
parameters.description: Raised when a card is dropped onto a column (mouse/touch drag) and the board can resolve which card moved, from which column, to which column and at what index. Fires only when cards carry a CardId and columns a ColumnId; otherwise the per-column OnDrop still fires with the raw event for fully-manual boards.

parameters.name: OnCardMove
parameters.description: Raised when a card is moved via the keyboard (arrow keys on a focused card): Left/Right move it to the previous/next column, Up/Down reorder it within its column. Provides the keyboard-accessible alternative to drag-and-drop. Requires CardId on cards and ColumnId on columns so the board can resolve the move.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the board's scrolling row container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the board's scrolling row container.

events.name: OnCardDrop
events.description: Raised when a card is dropped onto a column (mouse/touch drag) and the board can resolve which card moved, from which column, to which column and at what index. Fires only when cards carry a CardId and columns a ColumnId; otherwise the per-column OnDrop still fires with the raw event for fully-manual boards.

events.name: OnCardMove
events.description: Raised when a card is moved via the keyboard (arrow keys on a focused card): Left/Right move it to the previous/next column, Up/Down reorder it within its column. Provides the keyboard-accessible alternative to drag-and-drop. Requires CardId on cards and ColumnId on columns so the board can resolve the move.

enums.name: MoveDirection
enums.description: The direction of a keyboard-initiated card move.

records.name: DropEventArgs
records.signature: DropEventArgs(string CardId, string? FromColumnId, string ToColumnId, int ToIndex)
records.description: Payload describing a drag-drop of a card between/within columns.

records.name: MoveEventArgs
records.signature: MoveEventArgs(string CardId, string FromColumnId, MoveDirection Direction)
records.description: Payload describing a keyboard-initiated card move.

records.name: BoardContext
records.signature: BoardContext
records.description: A single stable instance cascaded per board. Tracks the in-flight drag so a column's drop handler can read which card is being dragged and from where — the HTML5 DataTransfer is unreliable (and empty under the touch shim), so the board carries the drag identity itself.

examples.title: Drag & Drop Board
examples.code:
<Kanban>
    <KanbanColumn Title="To Do" Badge="3">
        <KanbanCard Title="Design landing page"
                    Labels="@(new List<KanbanCard.KanbanLabel> { new() { Text = "Design", Color = "primary" } })" />
        <KanbanCard Title="Write API docs"
                    Labels="@(new List<KanbanCard.KanbanLabel> { new() { Text = "Docs", Color = "secondary" } })" />
    </KanbanColumn>
    <KanbanColumn Title="In Progress" Badge="1">
        <KanbanCard Title="Build auth flow"
                    Description="Login and register flow."
                    Labels="@(new List<KanbanCard.KanbanLabel> { new() { Text = "High", Color = "destructive" } })" />
    </KanbanColumn>
    <KanbanColumn Title="Done" Badge="1">
        <KanbanCard Title="Project setup" Labels="@(new List<KanbanCard.KanbanLabel> { new() { Text = "Done", Color = "success" } })" />
    </KanbanColumn>
</Kanban>

examples.title: With Add Card Button
examples.code: <KanbanColumn Title="Backlog" AllowAdd="true" OnAdd="@AddCard" />

examples.title: Custom Header
examples.code:
<KanbanColumn>
    <HeaderContent>
        <Flex Class="items-center gap-2">
            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm font-medium">Sprint 4</Lumeo.Text>
            <Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Secondary">5</Badge>
        </Flex>
    </HeaderContent>
    <KanbanCard Title="Task A" />
</KanbanColumn>

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Card title, shown in the default body layout when ChildContent is not supplied.

parameters.name: Description
parameters.description: Supporting text shown below the title (default body layout only), clamped to two lines.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Custom card body. When supplied it replaces the default Title/Description layout entirely.

parameters.name: Labels
parameters.description: Colored label chips rendered above the card body.

parameters.name: Avatar
parameters.description: URL of an assignee avatar image rendered bottom-right of the card. Omitted when null/empty.

parameters.name: OnClick
parameters.description: Fires when the card is clicked or activated via Enter/Space. Makes the card focusable/interactive.

parameters.name: Draggable
parameters.description: Whether the card can be picked up via HTML5 drag-and-drop. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: CardId
parameters.description: Stable id for this card, written into the board's drop/move payloads.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: This card's position within its column. Optional: when left unset the column derives the insert index from the card's sibling order at drop time, so the documented usage (which omits Index) still produces a precise insert index instead of always targeting position 0.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the card's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the card's root element (a splatted @ondragstart still runs and wins over the internal handler).

events.name: OnClick
events.description: Fires when the card is clicked or activated via Enter/Space. Makes the card focusable/interactive.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Column title, shown in the default header layout when HeaderContent is not supplied.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The column's KanbanCards.

parameters.name: Badge
parameters.description: Optional count badge shown next to the title (default header layout only). Omitted when null.

parameters.name: HeaderContent
parameters.description: Custom header content. When supplied it replaces the default Title/Badge layout entirely.

parameters.name: AllowAdd
parameters.description: When true, renders an "add card" button in the column footer.

parameters.name: OnAdd
parameters.description: Fires when the "add card" button (shown when AllowAdd is true) is clicked.

parameters.name: OnDrop
parameters.description: Raw drop event. Still fired for fully-manual boards that track drag state themselves. For an enriched payload (which card, from/to column, index) give the column a ColumnId, give cards a CardId, and handle Kanban.OnCardDrop at the board level.

parameters.name: ColumnId
parameters.description: Stable id for this column, written into the board's drop/move payloads.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the column's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the column's root element.

events.name: OnAdd
events.description: Fires when the "add card" button (shown when AllowAdd is true) is clicked.

events.name: OnDrop
events.description: Raw drop event. Still fired for fully-manual boards that track drag state themselves. For an enriched payload (which card, from/to column, index) give the column a ColumnId, give cards a CardId, and handle Kanban.OnCardDrop at the board level.

records.name: ColumnContext
records.signature: ColumnContext
records.description: Cascaded to child cards so a card can advertise the insert index when it is the drag-over target.

Kbd.name: Kbd
Kbd.description: Keyboard shortcut glyph — renders <kbd> with styling.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The key or shortcut label (e.g. "⌘", "Ctrl", "Esc").

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Size of the badge. Defaults to Md.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root kbd element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes are splatted onto the root kbd element.

<Flex Align="center" Gap="1">

KpiCard.name: KpiCard
KpiCard.description: Dashboard KPI tile showing value, label, and trend.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Small caption shown next to the optional icon, above the value (e.g. "Revenue").

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The headline metric, rendered large and bold (e.g. "$12,400"). Pre-formatted by the caller — this component does not format numbers.

parameters.name: Delta
parameters.description: Change amount rendered via Delta (e.g. +12.5). Omitted when null.

parameters.name: DeltaFormat
parameters.description: How Delta is formatted: as a percentage (default) or an absolute number.

parameters.name: DeltaPositive
parameters.description: Whether a positive Delta should be styled as good (default) or bad — e.g. for metrics where a rising value is undesirable (cost, churn).

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Small icon rendered in a muted square badge next to Label.

parameters.name: SparkContent
parameters.description: Sparkline or mini-chart rendered alongside the delta, capped at 140px wide.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root div.

examples.title: Basic
examples.code:
<Grid Columns="3" Gap="4">
    <KpiCard Label="Total Revenue" Value="$48,295" />
    <KpiCard Label="Active Users" Value="12,843" />
    <KpiCard Label="Conversion Rate" Value="3.6%" />
</Grid>

examples.title: With Delta
examples.code:
<Grid Columns="3" Gap="4">
    <KpiCard Label="Monthly Revenue" Value="$18,450" Delta="8.3" />
    <KpiCard Label="Churn Rate" Value="2.1%" Delta="-0.4" DeltaPositive="KpiCard.KpiDeltaDirection.Bad" />
    <KpiCard Label="Support Tickets" Value="247" Delta="-12"
             DeltaFormat="KpiCard.KpiDeltaFormat.Absolute"
             DeltaPositive="KpiCard.KpiDeltaDirection.Bad" />
</Grid>

examples.title: With Icon and Sparkline
examples.code:
<KpiCard Label="Page Views" Value="94,210" Delta="5.7">
    <IconContent>
        <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.TrendingUp)" class="h-4 w-4" />
    </IconContent>
    <SparkContent>
        <Sparkline Data="@_viewData" Class="h-10 w-full" />
    </SparkContent>
</KpiCard>

Label.name: Label
Label.description: Form label that links to a control via for/id.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The label text/markup.

parameters.name: For
parameters.description: Id of the form control this label describes. Rendered as the native for attribute so clicking/focusing the label activates the control.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <label> element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the <label> element.

Link.name: Link
Link.description: Styled anchor with underline + color variants.

parameters.name: Href
parameters.description: The URL the link navigates to. Omitted from the rendered <a> entirely (no href attribute) when null, empty, or whitespace-only, so the link stays inert instead of navigating to the current page.

parameters.name: External
parameters.description: Opens the link in a new tab (target="_blank"), automatically adding rel="noopener noreferrer" unless the caller already supplied rel.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Renders the link as non-interactive: no href, aria-disabled="true", tabindex="-1" (removed from the tab order) and pointer events disabled.

parameters.name: AriaCurrent
parameters.description: Value for the aria-current attribute when this link represents the current item (e.g. "page" for the active nav link). Null omits it.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual style: "default" (primary color, underline on hover), "muted" (muted color, foreground on hover), or "underline" (always underlined).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Text size utility suffix (e.g. "sm" renders text-sm). Omitted when null, leaving the inherited font size.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The link's visible content.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <a> element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the <a> element.

examples.title: Default
examples.code: Default link style

examples.title: Variants
examples.code: Default Muted Underline

examples.title: External Link
examples.code: Visit GitHub

examples.title: With Size
examples.code: Extra small Small Default Large

List.name: List
List.description: Ordered/unordered list with Lumeo typographic styling.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: One or more ListItem elements, rendered inside the <ul>.

parameters.name: Bordered
parameters.description: Wraps the list in a rounded border and separates items with divider lines.

parameters.name: Hoverable
parameters.description: Highlights each ListItem on hover. Cascaded to items via ListContext.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Controls each ListItem's row padding. Cascaded to items via ListContext. Default Md.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <ul> element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the <ul> element.
