parameters.description: Render fragment for each item's content, receiving the item.

parameters.name: Handle
parameters.description: Restricts dragging to a dedicated grip handle on each row instead of the whole row. Default true.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables drag, touch, and keyboard reordering, rendering the list as static.

parameters.name: Group
parameters.description: Optional shared-group name. When set, it is emitted on the container as a data-sortable-group attribute so groups are observable in the DOM. NOTE: cross-list transfer between lists that share a group is NOT yet implemented — the attribute is reserved for that future capability and current behaviour is intra-list reordering only.

parameters.name: OnReorder
parameters.description: Raised in addition to ItemsChanged whenever an item is reordered (via drag, touch, or keyboard), carrying the moved item along with its old and new indices. Does not change the ItemsChanged contract.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: Optional selector that returns a stable, unique key for each item. Providing this ensures Blazor's diffing engine tracks items by identity rather than index — critical for drag-reorder to map DOM state correctly. If omitted, the item reference itself is used as the key.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the list container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the list container.

events.name: ItemsChanged
events.description: Raised with the freshly-reordered list whenever an item is moved, via drag, touch, or keyboard.

events.name: OnReorder
events.description: Raised in addition to ItemsChanged whenever an item is reordered (via drag, touch, or keyboard), carrying the moved item along with its old and new indices. Does not change the ItemsChanged contract.

records.name: SortableMoved
records.signature: SortableMoved(TItem Item, int OldIndex, int NewIndex)
records.description: Payload describing a single reorder: the moved Item and its OldIndex / NewIndex within the list. Supplied to OnReorder.

examples.title: Basic Sortable List
examples.code:
<SortableList TItem="string" Items="@_tasks" ItemsChanged="@(v => _tasks = v)">
    <ItemTemplate Context="item">
        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm">@item</Lumeo.Text>
    </ItemTemplate>
</SortableList>

examples.title: No Handle (drag entire card)
examples.code:
<SortableList TItem="string" Items="@_tasks" ItemsChanged="@(v => _tasks = v)" Handle="false">
    <ItemTemplate Context="item">
        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm">@item</Lumeo.Text>
    </ItemTemplate>
</SortableList>

examples.title: Rich Item Template
examples.code:
<SortableList TItem="TaskItem" Items="@_tasks" ItemsChanged="@(v => _tasks = v)">
    <ItemTemplate Context="task">
        <Flex Class="items-center gap-3 w-full">
            <DynamicIcon Name="@task.Icon" Class="h-4 w-4 text-primary" />
            <Stack Gap="0" Class="flex-1">
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm font-medium">@task.Title</Lumeo.Text>
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">@task.Assignee</Lumeo.Text>
            </Stack>
            <Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Secondary">@task.Priority</Badge>
        </Flex>
    </ItemTemplate>
</SortableList>

examples.title: Disabled
examples.code:
<SortableList TItem="string" Items="@_tasks" Disabled="true">
    <ItemTemplate Context="item">
        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm">@item</Lumeo.Text>
    </ItemTemplate>
</SortableList>

Spacer.name: Spacer
Spacer.description: Flex-grow spacer that pushes siblings apart.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Tailwind size token (e.g. "4", "px"). When set, renders a fixed-size square spacer. When unset, renders a flex-grow spacer that fills available space along the parent flex axis.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Hints which axis the parent flex container uses so the grow-spacer collapses to zero on the cross-axis instead of expanding it. Default Horizontal keeps the historical behaviour (flex-1 inside a row). Switch to Vertical when the parent is a column stack — without it, the spacer's flex-1 took width from the column instead of pushing siblings to opposite ends.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root div.

examples.title: Flex Spacer
examples.code:
<Flex Align="center">
    <Text Weight="semibold">Brand</Text>
    <Spacer />
    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">Login</Button>
</Flex>

examples.title: Fixed Size
examples.code:
<Stack Direction="Lumeo.Orientation.Horizontal" Align="center">
    <Avatar />
    <Spacer Size="4" />
    <Stack Gap="0">
        <Text Weight="medium" Size="sm">Jane Doe</Text>
        <Text Color="muted" Size="xs">jane@example.com</Text>
    </Stack>
</Stack>

examples.title: Vertical Grow Spacer
examples.code:
<Stack Direction="Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical" Class="h-48">
    <Text Weight="semibold">Header</Text>
    <Spacer Orientation="Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical" />
    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">Footer action</Button>
</Stack>

SparkCard.name: SparkCard
SparkCard.description: Small dashboard card with an inline sparkline chart.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Small caption shown above the value (e.g. "Revenue"). Omitted entirely when blank.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The headline metric, rendered large and bold below the label. Omitted entirely when blank.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: Numeric series rendered as an inline sparkline below the value. Needs at least two points to draw a line; anything shorter renders nothing. Ignored when ChildContent is supplied.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Custom content for the sparkline region, replacing the built-in chart driven by Data.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Controls padding, typography and sparkline height. Defaults to Md.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Card surface style: Default (standard border), Bordered (thicker 2px border), or Filled (muted background).

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Inline sparkline shape (Line / Area / Bars). (#276)

parameters.name: ShowArea
parameters.description: Fill the area under a line sparkline. (#276)

parameters.name: ShowLast
parameters.description: Mark the most recent point. (#276)

parameters.name: ShowTooltips
parameters.description: Per-point markers + hover tooltips on the inline sparkline. (#276)

parameters.name: SparkColor
parameters.description: Override the sparkline color (CSS color / var). (#276)

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root card.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the root card.

enums.name: SparkCardVariant
enums.description: Default = standard card border. Bordered = thicker 2px border. Filled = muted background.

examples.title: Basic
examples.code:
<Grid Columns="3" Gap="4">
    <SparkCard Label="Revenue" Value="$24,800" Data="@_revenueData" />
    <SparkCard Label="Sessions" Value="8,412" Data="@_sessionsData" />
    <SparkCard Label="Bounce Rate" Value="34.2%" Data="@_bounceData" />
</Grid>

@code {
    private IEnumerable<double> _revenueData = new double[] { 12, 18, 14, 22, 19, 25, 21, 30, 28, 35, 31 };
}

examples.title: Custom Sparkline Slot
examples.code:
<SparkCard Label="Page Views" Value="94,210">
    <Sparkline Data="@_data" Class="h-10 w-full" Color="var(--color-primary)" />
</SparkCard>

examples.title: Without Data (Label + Value only)
examples.code:
<SparkCard Label="Uptime" Value="99.98%" />
<SparkCard Label="SLA" Value="100%" />
<SparkCard Label="Incidents" Value="0" />

examples.title: Variant (interactive)
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_variant">
    <SegmentedItem Value="@SparkCard.SparkCardVariant.Default">Default</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@SparkCard.SparkCardVariant.Bordered">Bordered</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@SparkCard.SparkCardVariant.Filled">Filled</SegmentedItem>
</Segmented>
<Grid Columns="3" Gap="4">
    <SparkCard Label="Revenue" Value="$24,800" Data="@_data" Variant="@_variant" />
    <SparkCard Label="Sessions" Value="8,412" Data="@_data" Variant="@_variant" />
    <SparkCard Label="Bounce Rate" Value="34.2%" Data="@_data" Variant="@_variant" />
</Grid>

@code { private SparkCard.SparkCardVariant _variant = SparkCard.SparkCardVariant.Default; }

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Content wrapped by the sparkle field, rendered on top (z-10) so the animated sparkles sit behind it.

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: Number of sparkle particles to render. Defaults to 5. Positions are deterministically seeded so they stay stable across re-renders.

parameters.name: MinSize
parameters.description: Minimum sparkle size in pixels. Defaults to 6.

parameters.name: MaxSize
parameters.description: Maximum sparkle size in pixels. Defaults to 14.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: CSS color of the sparkles — defaults to var(--color-primary).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the outer wrapper. If it includes a positioning utility (absolute, fixed, or sticky, including variant-prefixed like md:absolute), the built-in lumeo-sparkles (position: relative) marker class is omitted so the consumer's utility wins.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the outer wrapper.

examples.title: Text Wrap
examples.code: New Feature Pro

examples.title: Custom Color
examples.code: Primary Amber

examples.title: Dense Sparkles
examples.code:
<Sparkles Count="12" MinSize="8" MaxSize="18">
    <Heading Level="2">Magical Heading</Heading>
</Sparkles>

examples.title: Subtle (Few Sparkles)
examples.code:
<Sparkles Count="2" MinSize="4" MaxSize="8">
    <Button>Upgrade</Button>
</Sparkles>

Sparkline.name: Sparkline
Sparkline.description: Inline SVG trend chart primitive — line, area, or bars for tables and KPI strips.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The data series to plot. A null or empty series renders a faint flat baseline instead of a chart. A single value renders as a flat line across the full width.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Height of the chart in px. The viewBox width is always normalized to 100 units, so the SVG scales to fill its container's width. Default: 32.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: CSS color for the line/bars/area. Defaults to the theme's primary color (var(--color-primary, currentColor)) when unset.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Chart shape: a connected Line (default), a filled Area under the line, or discrete Bars.

parameters.name: ShowArea
parameters.description: Fills the area under the line, without switching Type to Area explicitly.

parameters.name: ShowLast
parameters.description: Highlights the last data point with a filled dot.

parameters.name: ShowTooltips
parameters.description: Render a marker dot at every data point with a native SVG <title> (hover tooltip showing the value) — point highlight + tooltips with no JS. Off by default. (#275)

parameters.name: StrokeWidth
parameters.description: Line/outline stroke width. Applied with vector-effect="non-scaling-stroke" so it stays a constant pixel width regardless of the SVG's scaled size. Default: 1.5.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name for the chart (aria-label on the svg, role="img"). Default: "Trend".

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root svg.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the root svg.

SpeedDial.name: SpeedDial
SpeedDial.description: Floating action button that fans out sub-actions.

parameters.name: Items
parameters.description: The actions revealed when the dial opens, rendered as a menu of buttons with the item's label as an accessible name and a hover/focus tooltip.

parameters.name: Icon
parameters.description: Optional trigger icon override (text/emoji, like Icon). When unset, a Lucide plus icon that rotates into an × while open is rendered.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.description: Fixed-viewport corner the dial anchors to. Defaults to BottomRight.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.description: Direction the action menu fans out from the trigger. Also determines the menu's ARIA orientation (vertical for Up/Down, horizontal for Left/Right). Defaults to Up.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Trigger color variant following the Button variant names: "Default" (primary), "Secondary", "Destructive", "Outline", "Ghost".

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true, the trigger cannot be activated; an already-open menu is closed immediately so it can't be left trapped open.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the root wrapper.

examples.title: Basic Speed Dial (Bottom Right)
examples.code:
<SpeedDial Items="@_actions" />

@code {
    private List<SpeedDial.SpeedDialItem> _actions = new()
    {
        new() { Label = "New Document", Icon = "📄" },
        new() { Label = "Upload File", Icon = "📁" },
        new() { Label = "Add Reminder", Icon = "🔔" },
    };
}

examples.title: Bottom Left Position
examples.code: <SpeedDial Items="@_actions" Position="SpeedDial.SpeedDialPosition.BottomLeft" />

examples.title: Direction: Left
examples.code:
<SpeedDial Items="@_actions"
         Position="SpeedDial.SpeedDialPosition.TopRight"
         Direction="SpeedDial.SpeedDialDirection.Left" />

examples.title: Custom Trigger Icon & Variant
examples.code:
<SpeedDial Items="@_actions"
         Position="SpeedDial.SpeedDialPosition.TopLeft"
         Direction="SpeedDial.SpeedDialDirection.Right"
         Variant="secondary"
         Icon="⚡" />

Spinner.name: Spinner
Spinner.description: Indeterminate loading spinner with size variants.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Spinner size — scales the SVG ring / dot / bar dimensions and their gap. Default Md.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual style: Ring (rotating arc, default), Dots (three bouncing dots), or Bars (three pulsing bars).

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Visible text shown next to the spinner. Also used as the accessible name (via AriaLabel's fallback chain) when AriaLabel isn't set.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Screen-reader name when there is no visible Label. Defaults to "Loading"; set it to a localized string (or pass aria-label directly) so the spinner announces in the app's language.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: CSS text-color class (e.g. text-primary) applied to the wrapper; the SVG/dots/bars all paint via currentColor/bg-current, so this controls the spinner's color.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the wrapper div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the wrapper div.

examples.title: Sizes
examples.code:
<Spinner Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" />
<Spinner />
<Spinner Size="Lumeo.Size.Lg" />

examples.title: With Text
examples.code:
<Flex Align="center" Gap="2">
    <Spinner Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" />
    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Size="sm" Color="muted">Loading...</Lumeo.Text>
</Flex>

examples.title: In a Button
examples.code:
<Button Disabled="true">
    <Spinner Size="Lumeo.Size.Sm" Class="mr-2" />
    Please wait
</Button>

examples.title: Variant — interactive
examples.code:
<Spinner Variant="Spinner.SpinnerVariant.Ring" />
<Spinner Variant="Spinner.SpinnerVariant.Dots" />
<Spinner Variant="Spinner.SpinnerVariant.Bars" />

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: Label for the primary action button. Ignored when ChildContent is supplied.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Custom content for the primary action button, replacing Text.

parameters.name: MenuContent
parameters.description: The dropdown's menu items, rendered inside a DropdownMenuContent opened by the chevron half.

parameters.name: OnClick
parameters.description: Fires when the primary action button (the left half) is clicked.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Button color/style variant, applied to both halves. Defaults to Default.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Button size, applied to both halves and used to compute the chevron half's fixed width. Defaults to Default.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true, disables both halves — the primary action and the dropdown trigger.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the root wrapper.

events.name: OnClick
events.description: Fires when the primary action button (the left half) is clicked.

examples.title: Basic
examples.code:
<SplitButton Text="Save" OnClick="HandleSave">
    <MenuContent>
        <DropdownMenuItem OnClick="HandleSaveAndPublish">Save and Publish</DropdownMenuItem>
        <DropdownMenuItem OnClick="HandleSaveAsDraft">Save as Draft</DropdownMenuItem>
        <DropdownMenuSeparator />
        <DropdownMenuItem>Discard changes</DropdownMenuItem>
    </MenuContent>
</SplitButton>

@code {
    private void HandleSave() { /* primary action */ }
    private void HandleSaveAndPublish() { /* secondary */ }
    private void HandleSaveAsDraft() { /* secondary */ }
}

examples.title: Variants
examples.code:
<SplitButton Text="Default">
    <MenuContent>
        <DropdownMenuItem>Option A</DropdownMenuItem>
        <DropdownMenuItem>Option B</DropdownMenuItem>
    </MenuContent>
</SplitButton>
<SplitButton Text="Secondary" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Secondary">
    <MenuContent>
        <DropdownMenuItem>Option A</DropdownMenuItem>
        <DropdownMenuItem>Option B</DropdownMenuItem>
    </MenuContent>
</SplitButton>
<SplitButton Text="Outline" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">
    <MenuContent>
        <DropdownMenuItem>Option A</DropdownMenuItem>
        <DropdownMenuItem>Option B</DropdownMenuItem>
    </MenuContent>
</SplitButton>

examples.title: Sizes
examples.code:
<SplitButton Text="Small" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
    <MenuContent><DropdownMenuItem>Option A</DropdownMenuItem></MenuContent>
</SplitButton>
<SplitButton Text="Default">
    <MenuContent><DropdownMenuItem>Option A</DropdownMenuItem></MenuContent>
</SplitButton>
<SplitButton Text="Large" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Lg">
    <MenuContent><DropdownMenuItem>Option A</DropdownMenuItem></MenuContent>

examples.code:
<SplitButton Text="Disabled" Disabled="true">
    <MenuContent>
        <DropdownMenuItem>Option A</DropdownMenuItem>
    </MenuContent>
</SplitButton>

Splitter.name: Splitter
Splitter.description: Resizable split pane for horizontal/vertical layouts.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Axis panes are laid out and resized along. Horizontal arranges panes side-by-side with vertical dividers; Vertical stacks them with horizontal dividers. Defaults to Horizontal.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The splitter's SplitterPanes interleaved with SplitterDividers.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the root container.

records.name: SplitterContext
records.signature:
SplitterContext(
        string GroupId,
        Lumeo.Orientation Orientation,
        List<object> Children,
        Action<object> RegisterChild,
        Action<object> UnregisterChild,
        Func<object, double, Task> ResizeNeighbors
    )

examples.title: Two Panes — Horizontal
examples.code:
<Splitter Orientation="Lumeo.Orientation.Horizontal"
          Class="min-h-[200px] rounded-lg border">
    <SplitterPane Size="50">Left</SplitterPane>
    <SplitterDivider />
    <SplitterPane Size="50">Right</SplitterPane>
</Splitter>

examples.title: Three Panes — Sidebar + Main + Aside
examples.code:
<Splitter Orientation="Lumeo.Orientation.Horizontal"
          Class="min-h-[260px] rounded-lg border">
    <SplitterPane Size="20" MinSize="10">Sidebar</SplitterPane>
    <SplitterDivider />
    <SplitterPane Size="55">Main</SplitterPane>
    <SplitterDivider />
    <SplitterPane Size="25" MinSize="10">Aside</SplitterPane>
</Splitter>

examples.title: Vertical Stack
examples.code:
<Splitter Orientation="Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical"
          Class="h-[320px] rounded-lg border">
    <SplitterPane Size="60">Editor</SplitterPane>
    <SplitterDivider />
    <SplitterPane Size="40">Terminal</SplitterPane>
</Splitter>

examples.title: Nested — Horizontal containing Vertical
examples.code:
<Splitter Orientation="Lumeo.Orientation.Horizontal"
          Class="h-[360px] rounded-lg border">
    <SplitterPane Size="25">Files</SplitterPane>
    <SplitterDivider />
    <SplitterPane Size="75">
        <Splitter Orientation="Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical" Class="h-full">
            <SplitterPane Size="65">Code</SplitterPane>
            <SplitterDivider />
            <SplitterPane Size="35">Output</SplitterPane>
        </Splitter>
    </SplitterPane>
</Splitter>

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the divider.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the divider.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Initial percentage size of the pane. Panes left at 0 are auto-distributed the remaining space by the parent Splitter on first render.

parameters.name: MinSize
parameters.description: Minimum percentage size a divider drag can shrink this pane to. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: MaxSize
parameters.description: Maximum percentage size a divider drag can grow this pane to. Defaults to 90.

parameters.name: Collapsible
parameters.description: When true, the pane can collapse to CollapsedSize by dragging its divider below (roughly) half the pane's MinSize, and restores when dragged back out. Previously this flag was inert (#258).

parameters.name: CollapsedSize
parameters.description: Percentage size the pane shrinks to when collapsed. Default 0 (fully hidden). Only meaningful when Collapsible is true.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The pane's content.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the pane.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the pane.

Stack.name: Stack
Stack.description: Vertical flex wrapper with gap prop.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.description: Flex layout axis — Vertical (flex-col, default) or Horizontal (flex-row).

parameters.name: Gap
parameters.description: Tailwind spacing scale value rendered as gap-{Gap} (e.g. "2", "4"). No gap class is emitted when null.

parameters.name: Align
parameters.description: Cross-axis alignment keyword rendered as items-{Align} (e.g. "center", "start").

parameters.name: Justify
parameters.description: Main-axis distribution keyword rendered as justify-{Justify} (e.g. "between", "center").

parameters.name: Wrap
parameters.description: Allows children to wrap onto multiple lines (flex-wrap). Default false.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The stacked children.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root div.

Statistic.name: Statistic
Statistic.description: Big-number statistic display with label and unit.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Label shown above the value, in muted/secondary styling. Hidden when null/empty.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The raw value to display, as a string. Formatted per Precision and Culture when parseable as a number. Overridden by ValueContent when set.

parameters.name: ValueContent
parameters.description: Custom value content — overrides Value. Drop in a <NumberTicker> (Lumeo.Motion) here for an animated count-up, rather than duplicating animation logic in core. (#273)

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: Content rendered immediately before the value (e.g. a currency symbol or icon), styled at the same size as the value.

parameters.name: Suffix
parameters.description: Content rendered immediately after the value (e.g. a unit label), styled at the muted, smaller caption size.

parameters.name: Precision
parameters.description: Number of decimal places to format Value to, when it parses as a number. Unset (null) renders Value verbatim.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Culture used when Precision is set. Default (null) falls back to CurrentCulture so a de-DE user sees 12,34 and an en-US user sees 12.34 automatically.

parameters.name: ShowTrend
parameters.description: Shows a trend indicator (up/down arrow plus optional TrendValue) below the value.

parameters.name: TrendValue
parameters.description: Text shown next to the trend arrow (e.g. "+12%"). When empty, an accessible "Trending up"/"Trending down" label is synthesized instead since the arrow icon alone is decorative.

parameters.name: TrendDirection
parameters.description: Direction of the trend indicator: Up (success color, default) or Down (destructive color).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Controls the title/value/suffix text sizes (Sm, Md default, Lg).

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual chrome: Default/Minimal (no decoration) or Bordered (rounded border + padding card wrapper).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the outer wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the outer wrapper.

enums.name: StatisticVariant
enums.description: Default = plain (no decoration). Minimal = alias for Default (explicit opt-in to no chrome). Bordered = rounded border card wrapper.

examples.title: Basic Statistics
examples.code:
<Statistic Title="Total Users" Value="12,847" />
<Statistic Title="Revenue" Value="48250" Precision="2">
    <Prefix><Lumeo.Text As="span">$</Lumeo.Text></Prefix>
</Statistic>

examples.title: With Trend
examples.code:
<Statistic Title="Sales" Value="1,234" ShowTrend="true" TrendValue="+12.5%" TrendDirection="Statistic.TrendType.Up" />
<Statistic Title="Returns" Value="42" ShowTrend="true" TrendValue="-3.2%" TrendDirection="Statistic.TrendType.Down" />

examples.title: Size (interactive)
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_size">
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Lumeo.Size.Sm">Small</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Lumeo.Size.Md">Medium</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Lumeo.Size.Lg">Large</SegmentedItem>
</Segmented>
<Statistic Title="Monthly Revenue" Value="48,290" Size="@_size" ShowTrend="true" TrendValue="+12%" TrendDirection="Statistic.TrendType.Up">
    <Prefix><Lumeo.Text As="span">$</Lumeo.Text></Prefix>
</Statistic>

@code { private Lumeo.Size _size = Lumeo.Size.Md; }

examples.title: Variant (interactive)
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_variant">
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Statistic.StatisticVariant.Default">Default</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Statistic.StatisticVariant.Minimal">Minimal</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@Statistic.StatisticVariant.Bordered">Bordered</SegmentedItem>
</Segmented>
<Grid Columns="3" Gap="4">
    <Statistic Title="Users" Value="12,847" Variant="@_variant" />
    <Statistic Title="Revenue" Value="48,290" Variant="@_variant" ShowTrend="true" TrendValue="+8%" TrendDirection="Statistic.TrendType.Up">
        <Prefix><Lumeo.Text As="span">$</Lumeo.Text></Prefix>
    </Statistic>
    <Statistic Title="Churn" Value="2.1" Variant="@_variant" ShowTrend="true" TrendValue="-0.3%" TrendDirection="Statistic.TrendType.Down">
        <Suffix><Lumeo.Text As="span">%</Lumeo.Text></Suffix>
    </Statistic>
</Grid>

@code { private Statistic.StatisticVariant _variant = Statistic.StatisticVariant.Default; }

Stepper.name: Stepper
Stepper.description: Stateful multi-step wizard with navigation, validation gating, and header indicators.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The stepper's steps — a sequence of StepperSteps. Rendered off-screen once to let each step self-register; the active one's body is then shown in the panel below the indicators.

parameters.name: ActiveStep
parameters.description: The current step's 0-based index. Out-of-range values are clamped into range on the next parameter set (and the clamped value pushed back via ActiveStepChanged).

parameters.name: ActiveStepChanged
parameters.description: Raised whenever the active step changes — via Next/Back, clicking a step indicator (when AllowStepClick allows it), or an internal clamp. Pair with ActiveStep for @bind-ActiveStep.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Layout direction: Horizontal (default) shows indicators in a row with connector lines; Vertical stacks them with labels beside each indicator.

parameters.name: Linear
parameters.description: When true (default), step indicators can only be clicked forward up to the next available step (gated by the current step's validity); set false to allow free navigation to any step (still requires AllowStepClick).

parameters.name: AllowStepClick
parameters.description: When true, step indicators become clickable to jump between steps (subject to Linear gating). Default false (navigation only via the Next/Back/Finish buttons).

parameters.name: OnFinish
parameters.description: Invoked when the Finish button is clicked on the last step and the step's validity check passes.

parameters.name: NextLabel
parameters.description: Override the "Next" button label. When null, uses the localized L["Stepper.Next"]. (#245)

parameters.name: BackLabel
parameters.description: Override the "Back" button label. When null, uses the localized L["Stepper.Back"]. (#245)

parameters.name: FinishLabel
parameters.description: Override the "Finish" button label. When null, uses the localized L["Stepper.Finish"]. (#245)

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the stepper's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the stepper's root element.

events.name: ActiveStepChanged
events.description: Raised whenever the active step changes — via Next/Back, clicking a step indicator (when AllowStepClick allows it), or an internal clamp. Pair with ActiveStep for @bind-ActiveStep.

events.name: OnFinish
events.description: Invoked when the Finish button is clicked on the last step and the step's validity check passes.

records.name: StepperContext
records.signature:
StepperContext(
        int ActiveStep,
        EventCallback<int> SetActiveStep,
        Lumeo.Orientation Orientation,
        bool Linear,
        int StepCount,
        Stepper Parent,
        // The role=tablist element id — the anchor for the DOM-order roving-nav probe
        // so arrow/Home/End tab order tracks the live DOM after a keyed reorder.
        string ContainerId)

examples.title: Basic Stepper
examples.code:
<Stepper @bind-ActiveStep="_step" OnFinish="HandleFinish">
    <StepperStep Title="Account Info" Description="Set up your account">
        <Input Placeholder="Email address" />
    </StepperStep>
    <StepperStep Title="Profile" Description="Personalize your profile">
        <Input Placeholder="Display name" />
    </StepperStep>
    <StepperStep Title="Review">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Review your information.</Lumeo.Text>
    </StepperStep>
</Stepper>

@code {
    private int _step;
    private void HandleFinish() { /* complete */ }
}

examples.title: With Optional Step
examples.code:
<Stepper @bind-ActiveStep="_step">
    <StepperStep Title="Basics">
        <Input Placeholder="Your name" />
    </StepperStep>
    <StepperStep Title="Extras" Optional="true">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">This step is optional.</Lumeo.Text>
    </StepperStep>
    <StepperStep Title="Finish">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">All done.</Lumeo.Text>
    </StepperStep>
</Stepper>

examples.title: Validation Gating (IsValid)
examples.code:
<Stepper @bind-ActiveStep="_step">
    <StepperStep Title="Email" Description="Required to continue"
                 IsValid="@(() => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_email))">
        <Input @bind-Value="_email" Placeholder="Email address" />
    </StepperStep>
    <StepperStep Title="Done">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Step gating passed.</Lumeo.Text>
    </StepperStep>
</Stepper>

@code {
    private int _step;
    private string? _email;
}

examples.title: Clickable Step Headers (AllowStepClick)
examples.code:
<Stepper @bind-ActiveStep="_step" Linear="false" AllowStepClick="true">
    <StepperStep Title="One" Description="Jump freely">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Click any step header to navigate directly.</Lumeo.Text>
    </StepperStep>
    <StepperStep Title="Two" Description="Non-linear">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Reachable in any order.</Lumeo.Text>
    </StepperStep>
    <StepperStep Title="Three">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">The last step.</Lumeo.Text>
    </StepperStep>
</Stepper>

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The step's title, shown next to (horizontal) or beside (vertical) its indicator.

parameters.name: Description
parameters.description: Optional secondary text shown below the title.

parameters.name: Optional
parameters.description: When true, an "(optional)" hint renders below the title/description.

parameters.name: Completed
parameters.description: Forces the step's indicator to render as completed (checkmark) even before the stepper's active index has passed it.

parameters.name: IsValid
parameters.description: Validity check consulted by the parent's Next/Finish buttons (disabled while it returns false) and to flag a past step's indicator as errored. null (default) is always valid.

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Custom icon rendered in the indicator instead of the step number, while the step is neither completed nor errored.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The step's body content, shown in the stepper's panel while this step is active.

parameters.name: KeepMounted
parameters.description: When true, the step's body stays mounted (visually hidden, not removed) while inactive, preserving its input state across navigation instead of being recreated on each visit.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Reserved for API consistency with other components; not currently applied to any rendered element (the step's markup is composed by the parent Stepper).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Reserved for API consistency with other components; not currently applied to any rendered element (the step's markup is composed by the parent Stepper).

records.signature:
StepRegistration(

        RenderFragment? IconContent,
        RenderFragment? ChildContent,
        bool KeepMounted)

Steps.name: Steps
Steps.description: Numbered step indicator for wizards and progress flows.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The StepsItem elements that make up the progress trail.

parameters.name: CurrentStep
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the currently active step. Pair with CurrentStepChanged for two-way binding.

parameters.name: CurrentStepChanged
parameters.description: Raised when the user clicks a step (only fires while Clickable is true).

parameters.name: Clickable
parameters.description: When true, steps become clickable and clicking one navigates CurrentStep to it. Default is false.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Layout direction of the trail. Defaults to Horizontal.

parameters.name: Animated
parameters.description: When true, enables opt-in motion: the current step's indicator pulses, connector lines draw in on advance, and retract on reverse. Default is false (no animation, original behaviour).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes are splatted onto the root div element.

events.name: CurrentStepChanged
events.description: Raised when the user clicks a step (only fires while Clickable is true).

enums.name: StepsDirection
enums.description: Direction of the user's most recent step transition. Drives the animated connector keyframes (forward draw vs reverse unwind). Component-specific (not a navigation direction in the cardinal sense), so intentionally NOT replaced by Side.

records.name: StepsContext

StreamingText.name: StreamingText
StreamingText.description: Token-by-token streaming text renderer for AI responses.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to display. Growing this value across renders (e.g. as tokens arrive) only fade-in-animates the newly appended suffix; shrinking or replacing it resets the diff window and re-renders the whole string.

parameters.name: IsStreaming
parameters.description: Shows a blinking cursor caret after the text, signalling that more content is still arriving.

parameters.name: Prose
parameters.description: Applies Tailwind Typography (prose) styling to the rendered output. Meaningful on its own only when Markdown (or a consumer's own markup via MarkdownRenderer) produces real block/inline elements for the prose styles to target.

parameters.name: Markdown
parameters.description: Renders Text as markdown instead of plain text. Uses MarkdownRenderer when supplied, otherwise a built-in dependency-free CommonMark subset (headings, lists, bold/italic, inline + fenced code, links, blockquotes). Incremental fade-in is disabled in this mode because markdown re-parses the whole string each update.

parameters.name: MarkdownRenderer
parameters.description: Optional render hook: converts the raw markdown Text into a MarkupString. Supply this to plug in a full renderer (Markdig, etc.). The delegate is responsible for producing SAFE HTML — sanitise untrusted input before returning. Ignored unless Markdown is true.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root element.

examples.title: Static Text
examples.code: <StreamingText Text="Blazor is a framework for building interactive web UIs using C# instead of JavaScript." IsStreaming="false" />

examples.code:
<StreamingText Text="@_text" IsStreaming="@_streaming" />

@code {
    private string _text = string.Empty;

    // Update _text incrementally as tokens arrive from your AI API
}

}

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, setting aria-required and (outside a FormField) the bubble checkbox's required, plus appending a destructive-colored asterisk to the label.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Forces the invalid/error visual state (destructive ring) and aria-invalid, independent of the ambient FormField's validation state.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error message shown below the switch (outside a FormField) when Invalid is set. Takes precedence over HelperText.

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the switch (outside a FormField) when there is no error to display.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label text rendered before the switch (outside a FormField, which supplies its own label), linked via for/id so clicking it toggles the switch.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: HTML name for the hidden bubble checkbox that mirrors the switch state, so it posts in a native <form> (a plain <button> posts nothing). Falls back to a composing FormField's Name when unset.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Value submitted for this switch in a native <form> POST when Name is set and the switch is on. Mirrors the native checkbox default of "on".

parameters.name: Checked
parameters.description: Whether the switch is on. Controlled together with CheckedChanged; when left unbound the switch tracks its own toggles internally.

parameters.name: CheckedChanged
parameters.description: Fires when the user toggles the switch, with the new checked state.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true, the switch cannot be toggled and renders with reduced opacity.

parameters.name: Loading
parameters.description: When true, shows a small spinner in the thumb, disables toggling, and sets aria-busy — for an in-flight async state change.

parameters.name: OnLabel
parameters.description: Text shown next to the switch (and used as a fallback accessible name) while it is on.

parameters.name: OffLabel
parameters.description: Text shown next to the switch (and used as a fallback accessible name) while it is off.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name for the switch button, exposed via aria-label. Recommended for icon-only switches that have no visible text. If omitted but OnLabel/OffLabel are set, the current one is used automatically so screen readers announce the switch's purpose.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Switch size. Supports Sm, Md (default), Lg.

parameters.name: Animated
parameters.description: When true, the thumb animates with a bouncy spring cubic-bezier timing instead of the default linear transition. Default is false.

parameters.name: HapticFeedback
parameters.description: When true, triggers a short haptic vibration via navigator.vibrate() each time the switch is toggled. Silently no-ops on browsers without the Vibration API (e.g. iOS Safari). Default is false.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the switch's track button.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the switch's track button (a splatted id is stripped inside a FormField so the generated id keeps the label wired up).

events.name: CheckedChanged
events.description: Fires when the user toggles the switch, with the new checked state.

Tabs.name: Tabs
Tabs.description: Tabbed content with keyboard nav and animated active indicator.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The tab set's TabsList (with its TabsTriggers) and TabsContent panels.

parameters.name: ActiveValue
parameters.description: The currently active tab's value. Controlled together with ActiveValueChanged; must match a TabsTrigger/TabsContent pair's Value.

parameters.name: ActiveValueChanged
parameters.description: Fires when the active tab changes (click, arrow-key activation, or swipe navigation).

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Layout axis for the tab list: Horizontal (default) or Vertical (list and panel sit side by side).

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual style applied to the list and its triggers. Defaults to Default.

parameters.name: AnimatedIndicator
parameters.description: When true, renders a sliding underline that animates between active triggers inside each TabsList. Default is false (no underline, original behaviour).

parameters.name: RenderMode
parameters.description: Controls when each TabsContent panel is rendered into the DOM and whether its state survives a tab switch: Active (default) — only the active panel is in the DOM; switching away unmounts it (form inputs, scroll position and any component state are lost). Lightest footprint. Lazy — a panel is rendered the first time its tab becomes active, then kept in the DOM (hidden) so its state is preserved on subsequent switches. Like DevExpress OnDemand. Eager — every panel is rendered immediately; inactive ones are hidden. State always preserved, but the heaviest initial render. Like DevExpress Always. A single TabsContent can opt into eager rendering regardless of this setting via ForceMount="true".

parameters.name: SwipeEnabled
parameters.description: When true, a horizontal pointer drag (>50 px horizontal, <30 px vertical) on any TabsContent panel switches to the next or previous tab. Default is false (opt-in).

parameters.name: SwipeWrap
parameters.description: When true, swiping past the last tab wraps around to the first (and vice-versa). Default is false (no wrap-around at boundaries).

parameters.name: Activation
parameters.description: How arrow-key navigation activates a tab (WAI-ARIA tabs pattern): Automatic (default) — arrow keys move focus AND activate the focused tab immediately (its panel shows). Best when switching tabs is cheap. Manual — arrow keys only move focus; the user presses Enter or Space (or clicks) to activate. Preferred when activating a tab is expensive (loads data, runs queries).

parameters.name: IconReveal
parameters.description: Icon-tab reveal mode: inactive triggers collapse to icon-only, and the active trigger smoothly animates its text label into view next to the icon (the label width grows from 0 → auto via a CSS grid transition, plus a fade). Lets a compact icon bar stay readable — only the current tab spells itself out. Give each TabsTrigger an IconContent (the always-visible icon) and ChildContent (the revealed label). Default false (labels always shown).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the root container.

events.name: ActiveValueChanged
events.description: Fires when the active tab changes (click, arrow-key activation, or swipe navigation).

enums.name: TabsActivation
enums.description: Arrow-key activation mode for tabs. See Activation.

enums.name: TabsRenderMode
enums.description: Render strategy for TabsContent panels. See RenderMode for the semantics of each value.

records.name: TabsContext
records.signature:
TabsContext(
        string ActiveValue,
        EventCallback<string> SetValue,
        Lumeo.Orientation Orientation,
        TabsVariant Variant,
        Dictionary<string, (string TabId, string PanelId, bool Disabled)> TabIds,
        bool AnimatedIndicator,
        TabsRenderMode RenderMode,
        HashSet<string> ActivatedValues,
        bool SwipeEnabled,
        bool SwipeWrap,
        Action<string> RegisterContent,
        Action<string> UnregisterContent,
        Func<string, Task> SwipeNavigate,
        TabsReorderState Reorder,
        TabsActivation Activation = TabsActivation.Automatic)

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Tabs ActiveValue="account">
    <TabsList>
        <TabsTrigger Value="account">Account</TabsTrigger>
        <TabsTrigger Value="password">Password</TabsTrigger>
    </TabsList>
    <TabsContent Value="account">
        <Card>
            <CardHeader>
                <Heading Level="3" Size="lg" Class="leading-none tracking-tight">Account</Heading>
                <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Make changes to your account here.</Lumeo.Text>
            </CardHeader>
            <CardContent>
                <Stack Gap="4">
                    <FormField Label="Name">
                        <Input Value="Pedro Duarte" />
                    </FormField>
                    <FormField Label="Username">
                        <Input Value="@peduarte" />
                    </FormField>
                </Stack>
            </CardContent>
            <CardFooter>
                <Button>Save changes</Button>
            </CardFooter>
        </Card>
    </TabsContent>
    <TabsContent Value="password">
        <Card>
            <CardHeader>
                <Heading Level="3" Size="lg" Class="leading-none tracking-tight">Password</Heading>
                <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Change your password here.</Lumeo.Text>
            </CardHeader>
            <CardContent>
                <Stack Gap="4">
                    <FormField Label="Current password">
                        <Input type="password" />
                    </FormField>
                    <FormField Label="New password">
                        <Input type="password" />
                    </FormField>
                </Stack>
            </CardContent>
            <CardFooter>
                <Button>Save password</Button>
            </CardFooter>
        </Card>
    </TabsContent>
</Tabs>

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The panel's content, shown while Value matches the tab set's active value.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The tab value this panel belongs to; must match a sibling TabsTrigger's Value.

parameters.name: ForceMount
parameters.description: When true, this panel is always rendered into the DOM (hidden when inactive), regardless of the parent RenderMode. Use for panels whose state must survive every tab switch — e.g. a half-filled form — while leaving the rest of the tab set on the lighter default mode.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the panel.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the panel.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The list's TabsTriggers.

parameters.name: TrailingContent
parameters.description: Extra content rendered after the triggers (and before the sliding indicator), inside the same role="tablist" container — e.g. a persistent "add tab" button.

parameters.name: Scrollable
parameters.description: When true, the list scrolls along its main axis instead of wrapping when the triggers overflow. Pairs with Arrows for a scroll affordance.

parameters.name: Arrows
parameters.description: When true (and Scrollable), shows chevron buttons at the start/end of the list that scroll it when the tabs overflow. The arrows auto-hide at the scroll boundaries (and entirely when nothing overflows). Default true — a scrollable list with no way to reach off-screen tabs by mouse is a usability gap (#239). Set false to keep the bare scroll/swipe affordance.

parameters.name: Reorderable
parameters.description: When true, each enabled TabsTrigger becomes draggable (HTML5 drag-and-drop on desktop, touch drag on mobile). On a successful reorder the library raises OnReorder with the from/to indices and moved value — the library does NOT mutate the order. The consumer's handler is responsible for updating the underlying collection and re-rendering.

parameters.name: OnReorder
parameters.description: Fires when the user drops a dragged TabsTrigger on a new position. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true.

parameters.name: SavedLayout
parameters.description: Optional previously-persisted tab ordering. The library does not mutate the consumer's backing collection on its own — pass this to your parent component on init and apply Order to your collection before rendering. Mirrors DataGrid's SavedLayout parameter and pairs with OnLayoutChanged to round-trip the order through your storage of choice.

parameters.name: OnLayoutChanged
parameters.description: Fires immediately after each successful reorder with the post-drop tab order, so the consumer can persist it (e.g. to local storage). The snapshot reflects the order the consumer's collection will have once it applies the corresponding OnReorder. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the order through storage.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the tablist container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the tablist container.

events.name: OnReorder
events.description: Fires when the user drops a dragged TabsTrigger on a new position. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true.

events.name: OnLayoutChanged
events.description: Fires immediately after each successful reorder with the post-drop tab order, so the consumer can persist it (e.g. to local storage). The snapshot reflects the order the consumer's collection will have once it applies the corresponding OnReorder. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the order through storage.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The tab's label. In IconReveal mode this is the text that grows into view next to IconContent only while the tab is active.

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component.

parameters.name: TrailingContent
parameters.description: Extra content rendered after the label (e.g. a badge), before the close affordance.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The tab value this trigger activates; must match a sibling TabsContent's Value.

parameters.name: IsClosable
parameters.description: When true, renders an X affordance that raises OnClose (also reachable via the Delete key).

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true, the tab cannot be activated, is skipped by arrow-key navigation, and renders with reduced opacity.

parameters.name: OnClose
parameters.description: Fires when the tab's close affordance is activated (click or Delete key). The library does not remove the tab itself — the consumer's handler owns that.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger button.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the trigger button.

events.name: OnClose
events.description: Fires when the tab's close affordance is activated (click or Delete key). The library does not remove the tab itself — the consumer's handler owns that.

TagInput.name: TagInput
TagInput.description: Input that turns entries into removable tag chips.

parameters.name: Tags
parameters.description: The committed tags, rendered as removable chips before the text input. Two-way bindable via TagsChanged.

parameters.name: TagsChanged
parameters.description: Raised whenever a tag is added or removed, with the full updated list.

parameters.name: MaxTags
parameters.description: Maximum number of tags allowed. 0 (default) means unlimited; once reached, the input disables and MaxTagsHelperText shows.

parameters.name: AllowDuplicates
parameters.description: Allows the same value to be added more than once (by default, a duplicate add and duplicate suggestions are suppressed).

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: Placeholder shown in the text input while no tags are present. Falls back to the localized "TagInput.Placeholder" string when null; hidden once at least one tag exists.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the input and hides each chip's remove button.

parameters.name: Suggestions
parameters.description: Candidate values shown in the autocomplete dropdown as the user types, filtered to those matching the current input text and (unless AllowDuplicates) not already tagged.

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field required, rendering a trailing asterisk on Label and setting aria-required on the text input.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Forces the invalid (error) visual state — a destructive border/ring — even when not inside a FormField reporting an error. ORed with the ambient FormField error state and any live ValidateInput/ ValidateInputAsync failure.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error message shown below the field when invalid (and no live validation error is active). Only rendered when this input is standalone (outside a FormField, which owns its own error slot).

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the field when not invalid and not at the MaxTags limit. Only rendered when this input is standalone (outside a FormField).

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Field label rendered above the input. Suppressed when nested inside a FormField, which renders its own label.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The text input's name attribute. Falls back to the ambient FormField's name when null.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the field's container div.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the inner text <input> on first render. See Input.AutoFocus for why the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM.

parameters.name: MaxTagsHelperText
parameters.description: Message shown once MaxTags is reached (in place of HelperText). Falls back to the localized "TagInput.MaxTagsReached" string when null.

parameters.name: GetTagText
parameters.description: Converts a tag to its display text on a chip. Falls back to item?.ToString() when null — required for a non-string TItem to render sensibly.

parameters.name: GetSuggestionText
parameters.description: Converts a candidate to its display text in the suggestions dropdown. Falls back to GetTagText, then item?.ToString().

parameters.name: TagTemplate
parameters.description: Custom render fragment for a chip's content, replacing the default text span (the remove button still renders alongside it).

parameters.name: SuggestionTemplate
parameters.description: Custom render fragment for a suggestion row's content, replacing GetSuggestionText.

parameters.name: Separators
parameters.description: Characters that, when typed or pasted, split the current input into separate tags (each trimmed segment is added via CreateTag or, for TItem = string, used as-is). Defaults to [","].

parameters.name: ValidateInput
parameters.description: Synchronous validator run against free-text input before it becomes a tag. Returning a non-null string rejects the input and shows it as the field's error message. Run before ValidateInputAsync.

parameters.name: ValidateInputAsync
parameters.description: Asynchronous validator run after ValidateInput passes. Returning a non-null string rejects the input and shows it as the field's error message.

parameters.name: CreateTag
parameters.description: Converts validated free text into a TItem. Required when TItem is not string — otherwise free-text entry throws. Return null to silently discard the input.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the field's container div.

events.name: TagsChanged
events.description: Raised whenever a tag is added or removed, with the full updated list.

examples.title: Basic TagInput
examples.code: <TagInput @bind-Tags="_tags" Placeholder="Add tags..." />

examples.title: With Suggestions
examples.code: <TagInput @bind-Tags="_tags" Suggestions="@_suggestions" />

examples.title: Max Tags
examples.code: <TagInput @bind-Tags="_tags" MaxTags="3" />

Text.name: Text
Text.description: Paragraph text with size, color, weight props.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Tailwind font-size scale token (e.g. "sm", "lg"), emitted as text-{Size}. Null uses the browser/element default.

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.description: Tailwind font-weight token (e.g. "medium", "bold"), emitted as font-{Weight}. Null uses the default weight.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Theme color token ("foreground", "muted", "primary", "destructive", "success", "warning", "info", "accent", or "default"). Unknown values are ignored (no color class).

parameters.name: As
parameters.description: The HTML tag to render: "p" (default), "span", "div", "small", "strong", "em", "b", "i", "label", or "mark".

parameters.name: Leading
parameters.description: Tailwind line-height token (e.g. "tight", "relaxed"), emitted as leading-{Leading}.

parameters.name: Truncate
parameters.description: Clips overflowing text to a single line with an ellipsis. Takes precedence over LineClamp when both are set. Default false.

parameters.name: LineClamp
parameters.description: Clamp the text to a fixed number of lines via Tailwind's line-clamp-N. Supported range is 1–3 (the utilities shipped in the prebuilt Lumeo CSS bundle); values outside that range are ignored. Multi-line counterpart to Truncate, which clips to a single line. When both are set, Truncate wins (single-line ellipsis).

parameters.name: Align
parameters.description: Text alignment token (e.g. "center", "right"), emitted as text-{Align}.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The text content.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the rendered element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the rendered element.

Textarea.name: Textarea
Textarea.description: Multiline text input with auto-resize option.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The textarea's text content.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Fired on every keystroke with the updated text. Binding it makes Value controlled.

parameters.name: OnInput
parameters.description: Fired on every native input event, alongside ValueChanged, for consumers that need the raw ChangeEventArgs.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the textarea, preventing input and greying it out.

parameters.name: AutoResize
parameters.description: Grows the textarea's height to fit its content as the user types, up to MaxRows, instead of scrolling internally.

parameters.name: MaxRows
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows the textarea grows to when AutoResize is enabled, before it starts scrolling internally. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: ShowCount
parameters.description: Shows a character counter below the textarea. When true, drops the native hard maxlength cap in favor of a soft limit — typing past MaxLength is still allowed but the counter turns red.

parameters.name: MaxLength
parameters.description: Maximum character count. Enforced as a hard native cap unless ShowCount is enabled, in which case it becomes a soft/visual limit.

parameters.name: Resize
parameters.description: Which directions the user can manually resize the textarea via its resize handle. Defaults to Vertical.

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, rendering an asterisk next to Label and setting aria-required.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Forces the invalid/error visual state. Also set implicitly when a wrapping FormField reports a validation error, or when MaxLength is exceeded while ShowCount is enabled.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error message shown below the field when Invalid is true (and the textarea is not nested inside a FormField).

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the field when there is no error (and the textarea is not nested inside a FormField).

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label rendered above the textarea. Suppressed when nested inside a FormField, which renders its own label instead.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name attribute rendered on the <textarea>.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-textarea density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only padding / min-height shifts between values.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the underlying <textarea> on first render. See Input.AutoFocus — the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM because parse-time has already passed by the time the runtime adds the element to the DOM.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <textarea> element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the <textarea> element.

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Fired on every keystroke with the updated text. Binding it makes Value controlled.

events.name: OnInput
events.description: Fired on every native input event, alongside ValueChanged, for consumers that need the raw ChangeEventArgs.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to reveal, split on whitespace into individually-animated words.

parameters.name: Stagger
parameters.description: Delay in milliseconds added per word index, so later words reveal after earlier ones. Default 80.

parameters.name: Threshold
parameters.description: Fraction of the element that must be visible in the viewport before the reveal triggers, passed to the underlying IntersectionObserver. Clamped to [0, 1]. Default 0.3.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root <span>.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the root <span>.

parameters.name: Class

parameters.name: IncludeSystem
parameters.description: When true (default) the toggle walks the full three-way mode cycle (System → Dark → Light). Set to false for a binary Light ↔ Dark toggle: every click visibly changes the theme — the System step is skipped (useful in app shells where an "invisible" System→Dark click reads as a broken button when the OS is already dark).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the toggle button, merged with the Tooltip AsChild slot's own attributes.

Timeline.name: Timeline
Timeline.description: Vertical event timeline with icons and connectors.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The timeline's TimelineItem children.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Rail axis — Vertical (default, items stacked top to bottom) or Horizontal (items laid out left to right; Alternate has no effect here).

parameters.name: Animated
parameters.description: When true, each TimelineItem fades+translates in with a staggered delay, the active indicator pulses, and connector segments draw themselves as the active step advances. Default is false.

parameters.name: Layout
parameters.description: Visual arrangement of the items: Default — all items stacked on one side of the rail (the classic single-column timeline). Alternate — items zig-zag to either side of a centered rail (even indices on the left, odd on the right). Only applies to vertical orientation; horizontal falls back to Default.

parameters.name: ActiveIndex
parameters.description: When set AND Animated is true, drives the active-dot progression: items with index < ActiveIndex render as "completed" (solid primary), the item at ActiveIndex pulses, later items are pending (muted). Connectors up to the active index draw themselves. Use together with ActiveIndexChanged for two-way binding.

parameters.name: ActiveIndexChanged
parameters.description: Two-way binding callback for ActiveIndex.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root div.

events.name: ActiveIndexChanged
events.description: Two-way binding callback for ActiveIndex.

enums.name: TimelineLayout
enums.description: Visual arrangement for Timeline items.

records.name: TimelineContext
records.signature: TimelineContext(Lumeo.Orientation Orientation, bool Animated, int? ActiveIndex, int? PreviousActiveIndex, int ReplayKey, Timeline Parent, TimelineLayout Layout)

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The item's heading, rendered bold above Description. Also used (or a positional fallback) as the item's accessible label.

parameters.name: Description
parameters.description: Supporting text shown muted below Title.

parameters.name: Time
parameters.description: Timestamp/date text shown muted below Description.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Free-form content rendered in the item's body, after the structured Title / Description / Time (any of which may be omitted). Use this to compose richer entries — buttons, badges, avatars, nested markup — that the fixed fields can't express. Works in every orientation/layout.

parameters.name: IsActive
parameters.description: When set, marks this item as the "active" (current) item. When the parent Timeline has Animated="true", this item's indicator pulses. If the parent also sets ActiveIndex, that value wins — use IsActive for static cases only.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the item's root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the item's root div.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual style of the picker. Defaults to List.

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, rendering an asterisk next to Label and setting aria-required on the trigger.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Forces the invalid/error visual state on the trigger. Also set implicitly when a wrapping FormField reports a validation error.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error message shown below the field when Invalid is true (and the picker is not nested inside a FormField).

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the field when there is no error (and the picker is not nested inside a FormField).

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label rendered above the trigger. Suppressed when nested inside a FormField, which renders its own label instead.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name attribute rendered on the List-variant trigger button.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The selected time of day. null means nothing is selected and the trigger shows Placeholder.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Fired when the user picks an hour, minute, second, or AM/PM. Binding it makes Value controlled.

parameters.name: Use24Hour
parameters.description: When true renders a 24-hour clock; when false renders 12-hour with AM/PM. When null (default) the picker derives the choice from EffectiveCulture's ShortTimePattern — de-DE, fr-FR and similar see 24-hour; en-US sees 12-hour.

parameters.name: ShowSeconds
parameters.description: Adds a seconds column (List variant only — the Wheel variant does not support seconds). Defaults to false.

parameters.name: MinuteStep
parameters.description: Interval between selectable minute options (e.g. 5 shows :00, :05, :10, …). Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: SecondStep
parameters.description: Interval between selectable second options. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: Min
parameters.description: Earliest selectable time (inclusive). Times before this are rendered as disabled options. Null (default) means no lower bound.

parameters.name: Max
parameters.description: Latest selectable time (inclusive). Times after this are rendered as disabled options. Null (default) means no upper bound.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: Placeholder shown in the trigger when no time is selected. Defaults to the localized "TimePicker.Placeholder" string.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Overrides CurrentCulture for 24h/12h detection and AM/PM designators. Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the picker: the List variant's trigger is disabled, and the Wheel variant is forwarded to TimeWheelPicker.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-picker density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only the trigger height + horizontal padding shift between values.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the List variant's trigger button, or the Wheel variant's root element.

parameters.name: FullWidth
parameters.description: When true, the time picker expands to fill its container width — equivalent to FullWidth on a Button. Propagates w-full to the Popover wrapper, PopoverTrigger, and the trigger button so the constraint reaches the rendered element through the shrink-wrap chain.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the List variant's trigger button.

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Fired when the user picks an hour, minute, second, or AM/PM. Binding it makes Value controlled.

enums.name: TimePickerVariant
enums.description: Visual style of the picker. List renders the existing scrollable hour/minute columns inside a popover (default). Wheel renders inline iOS-style snap-scrolling columns. The Wheel variant doesn't show seconds.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Fired when a wheel column settles on a new hour, minute, or AM/PM value.

parameters.name: Use24Hour
parameters.description: When true renders a 24-hour clock; when false renders 12-hour with AM/PM. When null (default), derived from CultureInfo.DateTimeFormat.ShortTimePattern.

parameters.name: MinuteStep
parameters.description: Interval between selectable minute options in the minute wheel. Defaults to 1.
