Toast.name: Toast
Toast.description: Notification toast — renders from ToastService queue.

parameters.name: Id
parameters.description: Stable DOM id for the toast root. The provider sets this so it can register the touch swipe-to-dismiss gesture against this element via RegisterToastSwipe. When omitted (a standalone <Toast> rendered outside ToastProvider, e.g. a static variant preview), a generated id is used instead.

parameters.name: StackIndex
parameters.description: Stack position within its position group, 0 = newest/front. Set by ToastProvider when StackToasts is enabled; null renders no data-index attribute at all (legacy list, no stacking CSS applies). See lumeo.css's "Toast stacking" section.

parameters.name: Leaving
parameters.description: True while ToastProvider is playing this toast's exit animation (animate-toast-out) ahead of unmounting it. Owned here (rather than folded into Class by the caller) so CssClass can also strip the entrance class the instant this flips true — a dismiss that lands mid-entrance must play the exit cleanly, not fight a still-parked animate-toast-in.

examples.title: Default Toast
Success Toast
examples.code: ToastService.Success("Success", "Your changes have been saved.");

examples.title: Destructive Toast
examples.code: ToastService.Error("Error", "Something went wrong. Please try again.");

examples.title: All Variants

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name for the close button. Override for i18n.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.description: Default position for toasts that omit ToastOptions.Position. PR #357 round-9 (findings 3/6): changing this parameter affects only FUTURE admissions (toasts not yet admitted via TryAdmit) — an already-mounted or already-queued toast's resolved group is snapshotted once (at admission, or at its own last explicit Update) and never silently reclassified by a later change here. See ToastItem.ResolvedPosition.

parameters.name: DefaultDuration
parameters.description: Default auto-dismiss duration (ms) for toasts that omit ToastOptions.Duration; 0 resolves to persistent (see IsPersistent). PR #357 round-9 (finding 1) / round-10 (finding 1): changing this parameter affects only FUTURE admissions — the resolved duration/persistence of a toast is decided ONCE, at the exact instant it is ADMITTED (in TryAdmit, whether it mounts immediately or is enqueued), and frozen on ToastItem.ResolvedPersistent (once mounted) or QueuedToast.ResolvedDuration (while still queued) — a later change here never retroactively reclassifies it, even if it hasn't mounted yet. Only an explicit ToastService.Update re-resolves a toast's classification, whether mounted (ApplyToastMutation) or still queued (UpdateQueued).

parameters.name: MaxToasts
parameters.description: Maximum number of NON-PERSISTENT toasts (resolved Duration != 0 — see IsPersistent) mounted at once per position group; overflow queues FIFO (see _pendingQueue) and drains as slots free up. Non-positive values are clamped up to 1 — there's no "unlimited" mode and no "show nothing" mode, so a misconfigured 0/negative value falls back to the smallest usable cap rather than throwing on the very first Show() (see EffectiveMaxToasts). A persistent toast (resolved Duration == 0 — e.g. a loading/promise toast, or ANY toast that omits Duration under DefaultDuration="0") does NOT count against this cap and is never an eviction candidate: it stays mounted until explicitly dismissed/updated, exactly like before MaxToasts existed. PR #357 round-4 (P2): counting persistents against the cap let one fill every slot and starve EVERY later Show() forever — no exit timer was ever going to free the persistent's slot, yet the overflow-queue path (below) can't tell "waiting on toasts that are actually leaving" apart from "waiting on a toast that will never leave" and queued anyway. Excluding persistents from the count sidesteps that distinction entirely: a persistent simply isn't part of the accounting, so it can occupy a mounted slot ADDITIONAL to the MaxToasts non-persistent ones without ever blocking them.

parameters.name: StackToasts
parameters.description: Sonner-style stacking, default on. When a position group holds more than one toast, the newest renders in front and older toasts collapse behind it with a small offset/scale, expanding to the classic list on hover or keyboard focus. Set false to always render the plain list.

parameters.name: StackToasts
parameters.description: Whether the provider wants sonner-style stacking for this group.

parameters.name: ToastCount
parameters.description: Number of toasts currently rendered in this position group (including ones mid exit-animation). Stacking only kicks in above 1 toast.

parameters.name: OnExpandedChanged
parameters.description: PR #357 P2 fix: fires whenever Expanded flips (hover-enter/leave, focus-in/out — including the deferred focus-out collapse below), so a consumer (ToastProvider) can pause EVERY toast in this position group for the duration of the expansion, not just whichever one is directly under the pointer/keyboard focus. Without this, a stack with several auto-dismiss toasts only ever paused the specific toast a per-toast hover/focus handler fired for — siblings merely VISIBLE in the fanned-out expanded list kept their own timers running and could vanish out from under the user mid-read. Not invoked for the initial (collapsed) render — only for an actual flip, tracked via _notifiedExpanded — so a fresh viewport with nothing hovered never fires a spurious "resume" the first time this callback is wired up.

events.name: OnExpandedChanged
events.description: PR #357 P2 fix: fires whenever Expanded flips (hover-enter/leave, focus-in/out — including the deferred focus-out collapse below), so a consumer (ToastProvider) can pause EVERY toast in this position group for the duration of the expansion, not just whichever one is directly under the pointer/keyboard focus. Without this, a stack with several auto-dismiss toasts only ever paused the specific toast a per-toast hover/focus handler fired for — siblings merely VISIBLE in the fanned-out expanded list kept their own timers running and could vanish out from under the user mid-read. Not invoked for the initial (collapsed) render — only for an actual flip, tracked via _notifiedExpanded — so a fresh viewport with nothing hovered never fires a spurious "resume" the first time this callback is wired up.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The toggle's content (label text and/or icon).

parameters.name: Pressed
parameters.description: Whether the toggle is pressed (on). Two-way bindable via PressedChanged.

parameters.name: PressedChanged
parameters.description: Raised when the toggle is clicked and its pressed state flips.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual style: Default (borderless) or Outline (bordered).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Controls the toggle's height and horizontal padding. Default Md.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-toggle density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only height + horizontal padding shift — text size + radius stay governed by Size.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the toggle, preventing clicks and dimming its appearance.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name exposed via aria-label. Required when ChildContent is an icon only (no visible text) — otherwise screen readers announce the toggle as an unnamed button.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <button> element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the <button> element.

events.name: PressedChanged
events.description: Raised when the toggle is clicked and its pressed state flips.

ToggleGroup.name: ToggleGroup
ToggleGroup.description: Group of toggles with single or multiple selection.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The group's ToggleGroupItems.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Selection cardinality: Single uses Value/ValueChanged and behaves like a radio group; Multiple uses SelectedValues/SelectedValuesChanged and allows any number of items pressed. Defaults to Single.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual style applied to every item. Defaults to Default.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Item size, cascaded to every ToggleGroupItem. Defaults to Md.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Group layout axis. Sets data-orientation on the group and cascades it to each item (shadcn/Radix parity). Not exposed as aria-orientation — the group ARIA role does not support that attribute, so this is a pure keyboard-navigation/layout concern, not an accessibility-tree signal. Default Horizontal. Roving arrow-key navigation already accepts both axes.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The single selected item's value (Single mode). Controlled together with ValueChanged; when left unbound the group tracks the selection internally.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Fires in Single mode when the selection changes.

parameters.name: SelectedValues
parameters.description: The selected items' values (Multiple mode). Controlled together with SelectedValuesChanged; when left unbound the group tracks the selection internally.

parameters.name: SelectedValuesChanged
parameters.description: Fires in Multiple mode when the selection changes.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the group container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the group container.

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Fires in Single mode when the selection changes.

events.name: SelectedValuesChanged
events.description: Fires in Multiple mode when the selection changes.

records.name: ToggleGroupContext

examples.title: Single Selection

<ToggleGroup Type="ToggleGroup.ToggleGroupType.Single">

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value this item represents. Selecting it adds (or, in Single mode, sets) this to the parent ToggleGroup's selection.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The item's label content (typically an icon and/or short text).

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true, the item cannot be toggled, is skipped by arrow-key navigation, and renders with reduced opacity.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name exposed via aria-label. Required when ChildContent is an icon only (no visible text) — otherwise screen readers announce the item as an unnamed button (matches AriaLabel's contract).

parameters.name: PressEffect
parameters.description: Visual feedback when the item is pressed. Default is None.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the item's root button.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the item's root button.

Toolbar.name: Toolbar
Toolbar.description: Horizontal toolbar container with separator, spacer, and group sub-components.

parameters.name: Overflow
parameters.description: When true the toolbar collapses items that don't fit into a trailing "⋯" dropdown. Use VisibleCount to control how many items are shown inline, or leave it at -1 (the default) to let the toolbar auto-measure via ResizeObserver.

parameters.name: VisibleCount
parameters.description: Number of leading items to show inline when Overflow is true. -1 (default) — auto-measure mode: a ResizeObserver measures the container width on every resize and computes the maximum number of items that fit before a "⋯" trigger button is needed. No consumer configuration required. Positive integer — manual mode: exactly that many items are shown inline and the rest collapse into the "⋯" dropdown regardless of available space.

parameters.name: OverflowLabel
parameters.description: aria-label for the "⋯" overflow button. Default: "More".

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Layout/keyboard orientation. Horizontal (default) uses Left/Right arrows to move between items; Vertical uses Up/Down. Drives aria-orientation.

enums.name: ToolbarOrientation
enums.description: Toolbar layout/keyboard orientation.

enums.name: ToolbarRenderSlot
enums.description: Slot that a child item should render for in overflow mode.

records.name: ToolbarOverflowContext
records.signature: ToolbarOverflowContext(ToolbarRenderSlot Slot, int VisibleCount, int TotalCount)
records.description: Cascaded by Toolbar to all descendant items when overflow mode is active. Items use this to decide whether to render normally (Visible slot) or as a dropdown row (Dropdown slot), and whether to hide themselves when they fall past the VisibleCount boundary.

    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
        <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.Italic)" class="h-4 w-4" />
    </Button>
    <ToolbarSeparator />
    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
        <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.AlignLeft)" class="h-4 w-4" />
    </Button>

        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">Bold</Button>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">Italic</Button>

ToolCallCard.name: ToolCallCard
ToolCallCard.description: AI tool-invocation card showing call + result.

parameters.name: ToolName
parameters.description: The tool's name, shown truncated in monospace in the collapsed summary row.

parameters.name: Status
parameters.description: The call's lifecycle state, driving the status dot color, chip text, and (for Error) which of Output/ErrorMessage is shown. Default Pending.

parameters.name: Input
parameters.description: The tool call's input, shown (with a copy button) in a monospace block when the card is expanded.

parameters.name: Output
parameters.description: The tool call's output, shown (with a copy button) when Status is not Error (or ErrorMessage is empty).

parameters.name: ErrorMessage
parameters.description: The error message, shown (with a copy button, in destructive styling) instead of Output when Status is Error.

parameters.name: DurationMs
parameters.description: Elapsed time in milliseconds, formatted and shown at the end of the summary row (e.g. "420ms" or "1.2s"). Omitted when null.

parameters.name: DefaultOpen
parameters.description: Whether the card's details section is expanded on first render (the native <details open> attribute).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the card's <details> element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the card's <details> element.

examples.title: Status Variants
examples.code:
<ToolCallCard ToolName="search_web" Status="ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Pending"
              Input="{"query": "latest Blazor news"}" />

<ToolCallCard ToolName="read_file" Status="ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Running"
              Input="{"path": "/src/app.razor"}" />

<ToolCallCard ToolName="execute_code" Status="ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Success"
              Input="{"language": "csharp"}" Output="Hello" DurationMs="342" />

<ToolCallCard ToolName="fetch_url" Status="ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Error"
              ErrorMessage="Connection timeout after 5000ms" DurationMs="5001" />

examples.title: Expanded by Default
examples.code:
<ToolCallCard ToolName="analyze_sentiment"
              Status="ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Success"
              Input="{"text": "Lumeo makes Blazor development a joy!"}"
              Output="{"sentiment": "positive", "score": 0.96}"
              DurationMs="128"
              DefaultOpen="true" />

examples.title: Status (interactive)
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_status">
    <SegmentedItem Value="@ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Pending">Pending</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Running">Running</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Success">Success</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="@ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Error">Error</SegmentedItem>
</Segmented>
<ToolCallCard ToolName="run_query" Status="@_status"
              Input="{"sql": "SELECT * FROM users"}"
              Output="@(_status == Success ? "10 rows" : null)"
              ErrorMessage="@(_status == Error ? "Timeout" : null)" />

@code { private ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus _status = ToolCallCard.ToolCallStatus.Pending; }

Tooltip.name: Tooltip
Tooltip.description: Hover/focus tooltip with arrow and configurable placement.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The trigger (usually a TooltipTrigger) plus a TooltipContent describing it. Both cascade from a shared TooltipContext tracking open state and stable ids.

parameters.name: ShowDelay
parameters.description: Delay in milliseconds before the tooltip opens on hover. Defaults to 200. A value of 0 or less opens immediately.

parameters.name: HideDelay
parameters.description: Delay in milliseconds before the tooltip closes after the pointer leaves. Defaults to 0 (closes immediately).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Extra classes for the tooltip's wrapper element (the relative inline-flex box around the trigger). Merged via Merge so a consumer class wins conflicts without !important — e.g. Class="w-full" makes the wrapper fill its parent so a full-width trigger (e.g. a sidebar nav button) is not shrunk to content width.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the wrapper element.

records.name: TooltipContext
records.signature: TooltipContext(bool IsOpen, string WrapperId, string ContentId)

examples.title: Default
examples.code: Add to library

examples.title: Side Variations
examples.code:
Tooltip on top

Tooltip on bottom

examples.title: With Icons
examples.code:
Bold

Italic

examples.title: Placement
examples.code:
<Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Top</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Top">Top tooltip</TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>
<Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Bottom</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Bottom">Bottom tooltip</TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>
<Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Left</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Left">Left tooltip</TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>
<Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Right</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Right">Right tooltip</TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The tooltip's text/markup content.

parameters.name: Side
parameters.description: Preferred side of the trigger to position the tooltip on. Defaults to Top; auto-flips to the opposite side on viewport collision (echoed back via data-side).

parameters.name: Align
parameters.description: Alignment along the chosen Side relative to the trigger. Defaults to Center.

parameters.name: ShowArrow
parameters.description: Renders a small pointer arrow anchored to the trigger's center, following any collision flip. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Distance in pixels between the trigger and the tooltip box. Defaults to 8.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the tooltip content box.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the tooltip content box.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The trigger's content. When AsChild is false (default), wrapped in an inline-flex <div>; when AsChild is true and ChildContentSlot is unset, rendered directly with no wrapper.

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" onto their own single element (which adds the aria-describedby when the tooltip is open). Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, the trigger renders NO wrapper element and instead cascades a TriggerSlot that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element. The key effect is correct a11y: aria-describedby lands on the real focusable control (so a screen reader announces the tooltip on focus) rather than on a non-focusable wrapper <div>. Hover/focus open-close still works via the events bubbling to the Tooltip root. Default false preserves the current inline-flex wrapper exactly.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger wrapper (non-AsChild mode) or folded into the TriggerSlot's class (AsChild mode).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the trigger wrapper (non-AsChild mode) or folds them into the TriggerSlot's attributes for a cooperating child to splat (AsChild mode).

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The content the ripple overlays — the ripple host wraps this and spawns ripple spans on pointer-down over it.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Color of the ripple. Default: currentColor.

parameters.name: Opacity
parameters.description: Peak opacity of the ripple as it expands. Default: 0.25.

parameters.name: DurationMs
parameters.description: Duration of the ripple expand/fade animation in milliseconds, and how long its DOM node stays mounted before removal. Negative values are clamped to 0. Default: 500.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the ripple host div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the ripple host div.

examples.title: Basic Ripple on a Button
examples.code:
<TouchRipple Class="inline-block rounded-md">
    <Button>Tap me</Button>
</TouchRipple>

examples.title: Custom Color
examples.code:
<TouchRipple Color="#3b82f6" Class="inline-block rounded-md">

parameters.name: Steps
parameters.description: The ordered sequence of tour steps, each targeting an element by CSS selector with its own title/description/placement.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Whether the tour overlay + tooltip are shown. Set to true to start the tour; the component sets it back to false internally on skip/complete.

parameters.name: OpenChanged
parameters.description: Fired when the tour opens or closes (including internal skip/complete/Escape). Bind alongside Open to control it.

parameters.name: IsOpen
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for Open.

parameters.name: IsOpenChanged
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for OpenChanged.

parameters.name: CurrentStep
parameters.description: Zero-based index into Steps of the currently displayed step.

parameters.name: CurrentStepChanged
parameters.description: Fired when the current step changes via Next/Previous, an arrow key, or Close resetting it to 0. Bind alongside CurrentStep to control it.

parameters.name: OnComplete
parameters.description: Fired when the user finishes the last step ("Finish" button).

parameters.name: OnSkip
parameters.description: Fired when the user skips the tour (Skip button, overlay click, or Escape).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the step tooltip.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the step tooltip.

events.name: OpenChanged
events.description: Fired when the tour opens or closes (including internal skip/complete/Escape). Bind alongside Open to control it.

events.name: IsOpenChanged
events.description: Deprecated alias for OpenChanged.

events.name: CurrentStepChanged
events.description: Fired when the current step changes via Next/Previous, an arrow key, or Close resetting it to 0. Bind alongside CurrentStep to control it.

events.name: OnComplete
events.description: Fired when the user finishes the last step ("Finish" button).

events.name: OnSkip
events.description: Fired when the user skips the tour (Skip button, overlay click, or Escape).

records.name: TourStepConfig
records.signature: TourStepConfig(string? TargetSelector, string Title, string? Description = null, Lumeo.Side Placement = Lumeo.Side.Bottom)

examples.title: Basic Tour
examples.code:
<Flex Class="gap-3 flex-wrap">
    <Button id="tour-feature-1" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Dashboard</Button>
    <Button id="tour-feature-2" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Team</Button>
    <Button id="tour-feature-3" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Settings</Button>
</Flex>
<Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" OnClick="@(() => _tourOpen = true)">
    Start Tour
</Button>

<Tour @bind-Open="_tourOpen"
      Steps="@_tourSteps"
      OnComplete="@HandleComplete"
      OnSkip="@(() => _tourOpen = false)" />

@code {
    private bool _tourOpen;

    private List<Tour.TourStepConfig> _tourSteps = new()
    {
        new("#tour-feature-1", "Dashboard", "Get an overview of your key metrics.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
        new("#tour-feature-2", "Team Management", "Invite members and manage permissions.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
        new("#tour-feature-3", "Settings", "Customize your workspace and account.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
    };

    private void HandleComplete() => _tourOpen = false;
}

examples.title: Custom Placements
examples.code:
<Flex Class="justify-between gap-3">
    <Button id="tour-top"    Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Top</Button>
    <Button id="tour-right"  Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Right</Button>
    <Button id="tour-bottom" Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Bottom</Button>
    <Button id="tour-left"   Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Left</Button>
</Flex>
<Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Secondary" OnClick="@(() => _open = true)">
    Tour Placements
</Button>

<Tour @bind-Open="_open"
      Steps="@_steps"
      OnComplete="@(() => _open = false)"
      OnSkip="@(() => _open = false)" />

@code {
    private bool _open;

    private List<Tour.TourStepConfig> _steps = new()
    {
        new("#tour-top",    "Top Placement",    "Tooltip above the element.",  Lumeo.Side.Top),
        new("#tour-right",  "Right Placement",  "Tooltip to the right.",       Lumeo.Side.Right),
        new("#tour-bottom", "Bottom Placement", "Tooltip below the element.",  Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
        new("#tour-left",   "Left Placement",   "Tooltip to the left.",        Lumeo.Side.Left),
    };
}

examples.title: Controlled step + callbacks
examples.code:
<Flex Class="gap-2 items-center">
    <Button OnClick="@(() => { _step = 0; _open = true; })">Start</Button>
    <Button Disabled="@(!_open || _step == 0)" OnClick="@(() => _step--)">Prev</Button>
    <Button Disabled="@(!_open || _step >= _steps.Count - 1)" OnClick="@(() => _step++)">Next</Button>
    <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Step @(_step + 1) / @_steps.Count · @_status</Lumeo.Text>
</Flex>

<Tour @bind-Open="_open" @bind-CurrentStep="_step" Steps="@_steps"
      OnComplete="@(() => _status = "completed")"
      OnSkip="@(() => _status = "skipped")" />

@code {
    private bool _open;
    private int _step;
    private string _status = "idle";
    private List<Tour.TourStepConfig> _steps = new()
    {
        new("#ctl-step-1", "Search", "Find anything across your workspace.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
        new("#ctl-step-2", "Alerts", "Stay on top of notifications.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
        new("#ctl-step-3", "Profile", "Manage your account.", Lumeo.Side.Bottom),
    };
}

Transfer.name: Transfer
Transfer.description: Dual-list transfer picker — left/right with arrows.

parameters.name: SourceItems
parameters.description: Items available in the left (source) panel. A transient empty/null value is treated as a loading state and does not clear the current selection.

parameters.name: SourceItemsChanged
parameters.description: Raised whenever items move into or out of the source panel.

parameters.name: TargetItems
parameters.description: Items already moved to the right (target) panel.

parameters.name: TargetItemsChanged
parameters.description: Raised whenever items move into or out of the target panel.

parameters.name: SourceTitle
parameters.description: Source panel header text. Defaults to the localized "Available".

parameters.name: TargetTitle
parameters.description: Target panel header text. Defaults to the localized "Selected".

parameters.name: OnChange
parameters.description: Invoked after a Move action completes, with the direction ("left"/"right") and the items that moved.

parameters.name: ShowSearch
parameters.description: When true, each panel shows a search box that filters its rows by label substring match. Default false.

parameters.name: ShowSelectAll
parameters.description: When true (default), each panel header shows a select-all checkbox that toggles every enabled, currently-visible (filtered) item in that panel. The checkbox reflects an indeterminate state when only some items are selected.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root container.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root container.

events.name: SourceItemsChanged
events.description: Raised whenever items move into or out of the source panel.

events.name: TargetItemsChanged
events.description: Raised whenever items move into or out of the target panel.

events.name: OnChange
events.description: Invoked after a Move action completes, with the direction ("left"/"right") and the items that moved.

records.name: TransferItem
records.signature: TransferItem(string Label, string Value, bool Disabled = false)
records.description: A transfer row. A Disabled item can't be selected or moved (its checkbox is disabled and a select-all skips it) and renders dimmed.

records.name: TransferChangeEventArgs
records.signature: TransferChangeEventArgs(string Direction, List<TransferItem> MovedItems)

examples.title: Basic Transfer
examples.code:
<Transfer @bind-SourceItems="_source" @bind-TargetItems="_target"
          SourceTitle="Available" TargetTitle="Selected" />

examples.title: OnChange — interactive
examples.code:
<Transfer @bind-SourceItems="_source" @bind-TargetItems="_target"
          OnChange="@(args => _lastChange = $"Moved {args.MovedItems.Count} item(s) → {(args.Direction == ""right"" ? ""Selected"" : ""Available"")}")"
          SourceTitle="Available" TargetTitle="Selected" />
@if (_lastChange is not null)
{
    <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Last change: @_lastChange</Lumeo.Text>
}

@code { private string? _lastChange; }

examples.title: With Search
examples.code:
<Transfer @bind-SourceItems="_source" @bind-TargetItems="_target"
          SourceTitle="Users" TargetTitle="Team" ShowSearch="true" />

TreeSelect.name: TreeSelect
TreeSelect.description: Select input with a hierarchical tree dropdown.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The selected node's value (single-select mode). Use Values instead when Multiple is true.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Fired when the selected node changes (single-select mode). Binding it makes Value controlled.

parameters.name: Items
parameters.description: The hierarchical node tree rendered in the dropdown.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: Placeholder shown in the trigger when nothing is selected. Defaults to the localized "TreeSelect.Placeholder" string.

parameters.name: Searchable
parameters.description: Shows a search box at the top of the dropdown that filters nodes by label, auto-expanding ancestors of every match.

parameters.name: Multiple
parameters.description: Enables multi-select mode: any node (leaf or parent) toggles into Values on click, and aria-checked reflects tri-state selection for parents with partially-selected descendants.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The selected node values (multi-select mode, used when Multiple is true).

parameters.name: ValuesChanged
parameters.description: Fired when the selection changes in multi-select mode. Binding it makes Values controlled.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the trigger, preventing the dropdown from opening.

parameters.name: ExpandAll
parameters.description: Expands every node with children when the tree first renders (and whenever Items is replaced with a new reference).

parameters.name: Clearable
parameters.description: Shows a clear button in the trigger when a selection exists, letting the user reset Value/Values in one click.

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, rendering an asterisk next to Label and setting aria-required on the trigger.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Forces the invalid/error visual state on the trigger. Also set implicitly when a wrapping FormField reports a validation error.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error message shown below the field when Invalid is true (and the tree select is not nested inside a FormField).

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the field when there is no error (and the tree select is not nested inside a FormField).

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label rendered above the trigger. Suppressed when nested inside a FormField, which renders its own label instead.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The form field name, forwarded for consistency with other form controls (not rendered directly by this component).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the wrapper div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the wrapper div.

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Fired when the selected node changes (single-select mode). Binding it makes Value controlled.

events.name: ValuesChanged
events.description: Fired when the selection changes in multi-select mode. Binding it makes Values controlled.

examples.title: Basic Tree Select
examples.code:
<TreeSelect @bind-Value="_selected" Items="@_categories" />

@code {
    private string? _selected;
    private List<TreeSelect.TreeSelectItem> _categories = new()
    {
        new() { Label = "Electronics", Value = "electronics", Children = new()
        {
            new() { Label = "Phones", Value = "phones" },
            new() { Label = "Computers", Value = "computers" },
        }},
        new() { Label = "Books", Value = "books" },
    };
}

examples.title: Searchable
examples.code: <TreeSelect @bind-Value="_selected" Items="@_categories" Searchable="true" />

examples.title: Multi-select
examples.code:
<TreeSelect Values="@_values" ValuesChanged="@(v => _values = v)"
            Items="@_categories" Multiple="true" Searchable="true" />

examples.title: Expand All
examples.code: <TreeSelect @bind-Value="_selected" Items="@_categories" ExpandAll="true" />

TreeView.name: TreeView
TreeView.description: Hierarchical tree with expand/collapse and selection.

parameters.name: Items
parameters.description: The hierarchical node tree. Node identity is tracked internally across rebuilds (see the ownership-model notes below), so a controlled parent that replaces this list with fresh instances still preserves expansion/loading/selection state for matching nodes.

parameters.name: ShowCheckboxes
parameters.description: Shows a checkbox on every node for multi-select-style checking, independent of row selection. Combine with CascadeCheck for tri-state parent boxes.

parameters.name: Expandable
parameters.description: Whether nodes with children can be expanded/collapsed at all. When false, every node renders as a flat leaf-style row with no chevron.

parameters.name: ExpandOnRowClick
parameters.description: When true (the default), clicking anywhere on a parent row — a node that has, or can lazily load, children — both selects it AND toggles its expansion (the whole row is the expand/collapse target, giving a large hit/touch area; the VS Code file-tree pattern). Leaf rows always just select. Set to false to keep the chevron as the sole expand trigger, so a row click only selects. The chevron and keyboard (ArrowRight/Left) expand in both modes; a Ctrl/Meta/Shift (multi-select modifier) click only selects, never expands. Has no effect when Expandable is false.

parameters.name: OnItemClick
parameters.description: Fired when a node row is clicked/activated (selection). Fires alongside the row's own expand/collapse toggle when ExpandOnRowClick applies.

parameters.name: OnItemCheck
parameters.description: Fired when a node's checkbox is toggled (requires ShowCheckboxes).

parameters.name: ShowSearch
parameters.description: Shows a search input above the tree that filters nodes by label, auto-expanding ancestors of every match.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root div.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root div.

parameters.name: LoadChildren
parameters.description: Fetches a node's children on demand the first time it is expanded, enabling lazy-loaded subtrees. Node identity/state tracking preserves loading and loaded children across a controlled parent's rebuilds (see the ownership-model notes below).

parameters.name: CascadeCheck
parameters.description: When ShowCheckboxes is enabled, checking/unchecking a node cascades to all its descendants and derives a tri-state (indeterminate) check on ancestors. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: CheckedValuesChanged
parameters.description: Fired with the full set of checked node values whenever a checkbox toggle changes the checked set.

parameters.name: NodeTemplate
parameters.description: Custom render fragment for a node's row content, receiving the TreeViewItem{T}. When null, the default label (+ optional icon) renders.

parameters.name: ExpandAll
parameters.description: Expands every node with children on first render (and whenever this flips from false to true). When LoadChildren is set, lazy children are then fetched in the background.

parameters.name: MultiSelect
parameters.description: Allows selecting multiple rows at once (Ctrl/Meta/Shift-click), instead of single-row selection.

parameters.name: SelectedValues
parameters.description: The selected node values, resolved by identity against Items. A value shared by multiple nodes selects every match.

parameters.name: SelectedValuesChanged
parameters.description: Fired when the selection changes (row click, or a checkbox in checkbox-driven selection modes). Binding it makes SelectedValues controlled.

events.name: OnItemClick
events.description: Fired when a node row is clicked/activated (selection). Fires alongside the row's own expand/collapse toggle when ExpandOnRowClick applies.

events.name: OnItemCheck
events.description: Fired when a node's checkbox is toggled (requires ShowCheckboxes).

events.name: CheckedValuesChanged
events.description: Fired with the full set of checked node values whenever a checkbox toggle changes the checked set.

events.name: SelectedValuesChanged
events.description: Fired when the selection changes (row click, or a checkbox in checkbox-driven selection modes). Binding it makes SelectedValues controlled.

records.name: TreeViewItem
records.signature: TreeViewItem

records.name: TreeViewContext
records.signature: TreeViewContext(TreeView<T> Tree)
records.description: Cascaded once per tree (IsFixed) so recursively-rendered nodes can reach shared tree state: roving focus, search visibility, node ids.

examples.title: Basic Tree
examples.code: <TreeView TItem="string" Items="_items" />

examples.title: Chevron-only Expand (ExpandOnRowClick=false)
examples.code: <TreeView TItem="string" Items="_items" ExpandOnRowClick="false" />

examples.title: With Checkboxes (Tri-state)
examples.code: <TreeView TItem="string" Items="_items" ShowCheckboxes="true" CascadeCheck="true" />

examples.title: With Search
examples.code: <TreeView TItem="string" Items="_items" ShowSearch="true" />

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The node this instance renders, including its children (recursed into for nested TreeViewNode{T} instances when expanded).

parameters.name: Level
parameters.description: Nesting depth (0 = root), driving indent and the ARIA aria-level.

parameters.name: PosInSet
parameters.description: 1-based position among this node's siblings, for ARIA aria-posinset.

parameters.name: SetSize
parameters.description: Total number of siblings at this level, for ARIA aria-setsize.

parameters.name: ShowCheckboxes
parameters.description: Shows a checkbox on this node. Threaded down from ShowCheckboxes.

parameters.name: Expandable
parameters.description: Whether this node can be expanded/collapsed. Threaded down from Expandable.

parameters.name: OnItemClick
parameters.description: Fired when this row is clicked/activated. Threaded down from OnItemClick.

parameters.name: OnItemCheck
parameters.description: Fired when this node's checkbox is toggled. Threaded down from OnItemCheck.

parameters.name: CascadeCheck
parameters.description: Whether checking this node cascades to its descendants and derives a tri-state on ancestors. Threaded down from CascadeCheck.

parameters.name: MultiSelect
parameters.description: Whether multiple rows can be selected at once. Threaded down from MultiSelect.

parameters.name: NodeTemplate
parameters.description: Custom render fragment for this node's row content. Threaded down from NodeTemplate.

parameters.name: LoadChildren
parameters.description: Fetches this node's children on demand when first expanded. Threaded down from LoadChildren.

parameters.name: OnSelectionChanged
parameters.description: Fired when this node's selection state changes (click or checkbox-driven selection). Bubbles up to SelectedValuesChanged.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the node's root div.

events.name: OnItemClick
events.description: Fired when this row is clicked/activated. Threaded down from OnItemClick.

events.name: OnItemCheck
events.description: Fired when this node's checkbox is toggled. Threaded down from OnItemCheck.

events.name: OnSelectionChanged
events.description: Fired when this node's selection state changes (click or checkbox-driven selection). Bubbles up to SelectedValuesChanged.

parameters.name: OnFilesSelected
parameters.description: Files chosen in the picker. Fires once per picker confirmation; the consumer is responsible for any subsequent state / upload logic.

parameters.name: Multiple
parameters.description: Allow multiple files. Maps to the native picker's multi-select.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: MIME-type / extension filter passed to accept=. E.g. "image/*", ".pdf,.docx". Null = no filter.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the trigger.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual variant. Matches the corresponding Button variant so the trigger composes visually with surrounding buttons.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Size. Matches the corresponding Button size.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: Trigger label. Falls back to localized "Pick a file" when null. Ignored if ChildContent is provided.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Custom label content. Overrides Text.

parameters.name: Icon
parameters.description: Custom leading icon. Defaults to LumeoIcons.Upload.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger's <label>.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the inner <InputFile> element (a splatted disabled is stripped so the typed Disabled parameter is the single source of truth).

events.name: OnFilesSelected
events.description: Files chosen in the picker. Fires once per picker confirmation; the consumer is responsible for any subsequent state / upload logic.

examples.title: Basic Trigger
examples.code:
<UploadTrigger Text="Upload file" OnFilesSelected="@HandleFiles" />

@code {
    private void HandleFiles(InputFileChangeEventArgs e)
    {
        // read e.File / e.GetMultipleFiles() and upload
    }
}

examples.title: Variants
examples.code:
<UploadTrigger Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Default" Text="Default" OnFilesSelected="@HandleFiles" />
<UploadTrigger Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Secondary" Text="Secondary" OnFilesSelected="@HandleFiles" />
<UploadTrigger Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Text="Outline" OnFilesSelected="@HandleFiles" />
<UploadTrigger Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Ghost" Text="Ghost" OnFilesSelected="@HandleFiles" />

examples.title: Accept Filter and Multiple
examples.code:
<UploadTrigger Text="Pick images" Accept="image/*" Multiple="true" OnFilesSelected="@HandleFiles" />
<UploadTrigger Text="Pick a PDF" Accept=".pdf,.docx" OnFilesSelected="@HandleFiles" />

examples.title: Custom Label and Icon
examples.code: Replace photo @* Empty ChildContent suppresses the default text label *@

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: Text tiled as the repeating watermark, rendered via a rotated SVG mask over the host's current text color. Ignored when Image is set.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: Optional image URL to tile as the watermark instead of Text. When set, the image is repeated edge-to-edge; ImageWidth/ImageHeight control each tile's size and Opacity dims it. Takes precedence over Text when both are provided.

parameters.name: ImageWidth
parameters.description: Tile width in px for the Image watermark. Default 120.

parameters.name: ImageHeight
parameters.description: Tile height in px for the Image watermark. Default 64.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The content the watermark overlay is stamped on top of.

parameters.name: Rotation
parameters.description: Rotation in degrees applied to the text watermark tile. Defaults to -22.

parameters.name: Gap
parameters.description: Spacing in pixels between repeated text watermark tiles. Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: FontSize
parameters.description: Font size in pixels of the text watermark. Defaults to 14.

parameters.name: Opacity
parameters.description: Opacity of the watermark overlay, from 0 to 1. Defaults to 0.1.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the outer wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures unmatched attributes and applies them to the outer wrapper.

examples.title: Basic Watermark
examples.code:
<Watermark Text="Confidential">
    <Stack Gap="4" Class="p-8">
        <Lumeo.Text Color="foreground">This document contains sensitive information.</Lumeo.Text>
    </Stack>
</Watermark>

examples.title: Custom Settings
examples.code:
<Watermark Text="DRAFT" Rotation="-30" FontSize="20" Opacity="0.15" Gap="120">
    <Stack Gap="2" Class="p-8">
        <Heading Level="3" Size="lg">Draft Document</Heading>
        <Lumeo.Text Color="muted">This is a work in progress.</Lumeo.Text>
    </Stack>
</Watermark>

Window.name: Window
Window.description: Non-modal draggable and resizable floating panel with minimize and maximize support.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Whether the window is visible. Pair with OpenChanged for two-way binding; leave unbound for uncontrolled use (Escape/close-button clicks self-manage visibility).

parameters.name: OpenChanged
parameters.description: Raised when the window is closed (Escape, close button) or reopened.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Plain-text title shown in the title bar. Ignored when TitleContent is set.

parameters.name: TitleContent
parameters.description: Custom title bar content, overriding Title. Not shown in the minimized/maximized title bars, which always render plain Title.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The window's body content.

parameters.name: FooterContent
parameters.description: Optional content rendered in a bordered footer below the body.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Initial width as a CSS pixel value (e.g. "480px"). Defaults to "480px". Only read once on first open; live size afterward tracks user resizing.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Initial height as a CSS pixel value. Defaults to "360px". Only read once on first open; live size afterward tracks user resizing.

parameters.name: Resizable
parameters.description: When true (default), shows the bottom-right resize handle and allows dragging it to resize the window.

parameters.name: Draggable
parameters.description: When true (default), the title bar can be dragged to reposition the window.

parameters.name: ShowClose
parameters.description: Shows the close button in the title bar. Default is true. Also gates whether Escape closes the window.

parameters.name: ShowMinimize
parameters.description: Shows the minimize button in the title bar. Default is true.

parameters.name: ShowMaximize
parameters.description: Shows the maximize button in the title bar. Default is true.

parameters.name: InitialX
parameters.description: Initial horizontal position as a CSS pixel value. Defaults to 80px when unset. Only read once on first open; clamped to stay within the viewport.

parameters.name: InitialY
parameters.description: Initial vertical position as a CSS pixel value. Defaults to 80px when unset. Only read once on first open; clamped to stay within the viewport.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root window element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes are splatted onto the root window element.

events.name: OpenChanged
events.description: Raised when the window is closed (Escape, close button) or reopened.

examples.title: Basic Window
examples.code:
Drag the title bar to move. Drag the bottom-right corner to resize.

 @code { private bool _open; }

examples.title: Window with Footer
examples.code: You have unsaved changes.

examples.title: Non-Resizable Window
examples.code: This window has a fixed size.
